Barcode value printed on the physical label affixed to this storage location. Scanned by warehouse operatives using handheld devices or fixed scanners during pick, putaway, and cycle count operations to confirm location identity.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Barcode Scanning; GS1 Barcode Standards

description: Identifier for the individual bin or slot within the shelf. The most granular physical unit of the warehouse location hierarchy. Directly maps to a scannable barcode label affixed to the physical bin in the warehouse.

Unit of measure in which the capacity of this storage location is expressed. Aligns with the WMS capacity management configuration for the warehouse node (e.g., 'pallets' for bulk storage, 'units' for pick-face bins, 'kg' for weight-constrained locations).
Timestamp when this warehouse location master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 27001 - Audit Logging; SOX - Internal Controls

description: Target number of days between cycle counts for this location. Drives the WMS cycle count scheduling engine. High-velocity or high-value locations typically have shorter intervals (e.g., 7 days) while low-velocity locations may be counted quarterly (e.g., 90 days).
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Cycle Count Scheduling; SOX - Inventory Accuracy Controls

description: Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code of the product permanently assigned to this location under a fixed slotting strategy. Null for floating or multi-SKU locations. Used by WMS to enforce single-SKU integrity and prevent mixed-SKU putaway errors.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Fixed Slotting; Product Information Management System (PIM)

description: Date from which this storage location record became operationally active and available for inventory allocation in the WMS. Supports bi-temporal data management and warehouse expansion tracking.
reference: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Standards; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Location Lifecycle

description: Date on which this storage location record ceases to be operationally active. Null for currently active locations. Used to manage decommissioned or seasonally inactive locations without hard-deleting master records.
reference: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Standards; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Location Lifecycle

description: Hazardous materials classification code assigned to this location, indicating the category of dangerous goods that may be stored here (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives). Null for non-hazmat locations. Governs WMS slotting restrictions and regulatory compliance for storage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: class_1_explosives|class_2_gases|class_3_flammable|class_4_flammable_solids|class_5_oxidizers|class_6_toxic|class_7_radioactive|class_8_corrosives|class_9_misc|none — promote to reference product]
reference: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - Hazardous Materials Regulations; NIST Cybersecurity Framework; Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC)

description: Physical height dimension of the storage location in centimetres. Used for vertical clearance validation during slotting and for determining forklift or reach-truck accessibility requirements.

Indicates whether this location is designated as a bulk or reserve storage position. Bulk locations hold overstock inventory that replenishes pick face locations. Used in WMS replenishment trigger logic.
Indicates whether this storage location has been certified and approved for storing hazardous materials. Drives WMS slotting rules to prevent non-approved locations from receiving hazmat inventory.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Hazardous Materials Storage; Federal Trade Commission (FTC)

description: Indicates whether this location is designated as an active pick face — a forward-pick position from which pickers directly fulfil customer orders. Pick face locations are replenished from bulk storage and are optimized for pick speed and ergonomics.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Pick Face Management

description: Indicates whether this location is designated as a quarantine hold area. Inventory placed in quarantine locations is blocked from pick and shipment pending quality inspection, regulatory hold, or Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) processing.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Product Safety Hold Procedures; ISO 9001 - Quality Management

description: Indicates whether this is a virtual (system-only) storage location with no physical counterpart in the warehouse. Virtual locations are used for in-transit inventory, damaged goods holding, or system adjustment buckets in the WMS.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Virtual Location Configuration

description: Date on which the most recent cycle count was performed at this storage location. Used to schedule future cycle counts, assess inventory accuracy compliance, and prioritize locations overdue for counting per the warehouse cycle count program.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Cycle Count Management; SOX - Inventory Controls

description: Physical length dimension of the storage location in centimetres. Used in WMS slotting optimization to match product dimensions to available location space and to calculate volumetric utilization.

Numeric vertical level of the storage location within the rack structure (e.g., 1 = ground level, 2 = second level). Used for ergonomic slotting rules, forklift accessibility constraints, and pick-path optimization in the WMS.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this storage location within the warehouse. Used by WMS for pick-pack-ship operations, barcode scanning, and slotting optimization. Typically formatted as ZONE-AISLE-RACK-SHELF-BIN (e.g., A-01-03-02-05).
Human-readable descriptive name for the storage location, used in WMS user interfaces, slotting reports, and operational dashboards (e.g., 'Aisle 3 Rack B Shelf 2 Bin 5').

Current operational lifecycle state of the storage location. Controls whether the WMS can allocate inventory to or pick from this location. 'blocked' indicates a temporary hold (e.g., quality inspection); 'under_maintenance' indicates physical inaccessibility.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Location Lifecycle Management

description: Functional classification of the storage location within the warehouse. Determines how the WMS uses this location in fulfillment workflows: pick_face (active picking slot), bulk_storage (reserve/overflow stock), receiving_dock (inbound goods receipt), returns_area (Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) processing), virtual (system-only allocation node), staging (pre-shipment consolidation area).
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Location Type Reference

description: Indicates whether inventory stored at this location must be tracked at the lot or batch level. Required for locations holding perishables, pharmaceuticals, or regulated goods where First In First Out (FIFO) / First Expired First Out (FEFO) lot traceability is mandated.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Traceability Requirements; ISO 9001 - Lot Control

description: Maximum storage capacity of this location expressed in the unit defined by capacity_uom. Used by WMS to enforce inventory allocation limits and prevent overstocking. Critical for slotting optimization and replenishment planning.

Maximum inventory quantity (in capacity_uom) that should be stocked at this location. Used by the WMS to cap replenishment quantities and prevent overfilling. Typically set equal to or less than max_capacity to allow for operational buffer.

Maximum allowable weight in kilograms that this storage location can safely bear. Enforced by WMS during inventory allocation to prevent structural overloading. Particularly relevant for high-density racking and mezzanine-level locations.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Structural Safety Standards; ISO 9001

description: Minimum inventory quantity (in capacity_uom) that should be maintained at this location before a replenishment task is triggered. Used by the WMS replenishment engine to prevent Out of Stock (OOS) conditions at pick face locations.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Replenishment Management; Out of Stock (OOS) Prevention

business_glossary_term: Mixed Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) Allowed Flag
description: Indicates whether multiple distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) may be co-located in this storage position simultaneously. When false, the WMS enforces single-SKU integrity. Mixed-SKU locations are common in bulk storage but typically prohibited at pick faces to prevent picking errors.

Numeric sequence number used by the WMS to order this location within a pick path or wave. Lower numbers are visited first. Optimized to minimize picker travel distance and maximize throughput in fulfillment operations.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Pick Path Optimization

description: Strategy used by the WMS to direct incoming inventory to this location during receiving and putaway operations. Fixed: always assigned to specific Stock Keeping Units (SKUs); floating: any compatible SKU; directed: WMS algorithm assigns; nearest_empty: proximity-based assignment.
Identifier for the rack or bay within the aisle where this location is physically situated. Part of the hierarchical location address used by WMS for precise inventory positioning.

Inventory rotation and replenishment strategy applied to this location. Governs the order in which stock is consumed and replenished: First In First Out (FIFO), Last In First Out (LIFO), First Expired First Out (FEFO) for perishables, manual (operator-directed), or none (no automated replenishment).
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Replenishment Configuration; ISO 9001 - Inventory Control

description: RFID tag identifier associated with this storage location for automated inventory tracking and real-time location visibility. Null if the location is not RFID-enabled. Supports real-time inventory visibility in RFID-equipped warehouse nodes.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - RFID Integration; GS1 EPC Standards

description: Identifier for the shelf level within the rack where this location is physically situated. Shelf codes are used for vertical positioning in WMS pick-path logic and ergonomic slotting (e.g., golden zone placement for high-velocity Stock Keeping Units (SKUs)).

ABC/D velocity classification assigned to this location based on the throughput of Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) slotted here. Class A: highest-velocity (golden zone, ergonomic height); Class B: medium-velocity; Class C: low-velocity; Class D: very slow-moving or seasonal. Drives slotting optimization decisions.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Slotting Optimization; Best Seller Rank (BSR) Analytics

description: Temperature classification of the storage location, defining the environmental condition maintained at this position. Drives product-to-location compatibility rules in WMS slotting: ambient (room temperature), chilled (2–8°C), frozen (below -18°C), controlled (specific regulated range for pharmaceuticals or electronics).
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Storage Condition Standards; ISO 9001 - Quality Management

description: Timestamp when this warehouse location master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) processing, incremental ETL loads, and audit compliance in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: ISO 27001 - Audit Logging; SOX - Internal Controls; ETL Change Data Capture

description: Physical width dimension of the storage location in centimetres. Used alongside length_cm and height_cm for volumetric capacity calculations and product-to-location fit validation in WMS slotting.

Identifier for the warehouse zone in which this location resides. Zones group locations by operational function, temperature requirement, or product category (e.g., DRY, COLD, HAZMAT, RETURNS). Used for slotting optimization and pick-path routing.
Unique surrogate identifier for a warehouse node record in the fulfillment network. Primary key for the warehouse_node master data product.
Primary street address line of the warehouse node facility. Used for carrier pickup scheduling, regulatory filings, and geographic routing in the TMS.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Facility Address

description: Secondary address line for the warehouse node (suite, dock number, building identifier). Supplements address_line1 for precise carrier and logistics routing.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Facility Address

description: Total usable floor area of the warehouse node in square feet. Principal capacity measurement used for space utilization reporting, lease management, and network capacity planning.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Capacity; ISO 9001 - Facility Management

description: Comma-separated list of carrier codes (e.g., 'UPS,FEDEX,USPS,DHL') representing the shipping carriers that service this warehouse node. Used by the TMS for carrier selection and rate shopping during order fulfillment.
reference: Transportation Management System (TMS) - Carrier Assignment; SLA Management

description: City in which the warehouse node is physically located. Used for geographic clustering, last-mile delivery zone assignment, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.

Date on which the warehouse node was officially commissioned and became operational in the fulfillment network. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, depreciation calculations, and network history reporting.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Fixed Asset Management; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Lifecycle

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the warehouse node is located. Drives cross-border trade compliance, customs documentation, and WTO regulatory requirements.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO International Trade Regulations; EU Electronic Commerce Directive

description: Timestamp when the warehouse node master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management per ISO 27001 information security standards.
reference: ISO 27001 - Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: Date on which the warehouse node was decommissioned and removed from the active fulfillment network. Null for active nodes. Used for network topology history and asset lifecycle reporting.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Fixed Asset Management; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Lifecycle

description: Number of inbound receiving dock doors at the warehouse node. Determines inbound throughput capacity for purchase order receiving, supplier deliveries, and FTL/LTL carrier appointments.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Dock Management; Transportation Management System (TMS)

description: Number of outbound shipping dock doors at the warehouse node. Determines outbound throughput capacity for order fulfillment, carrier pickups, and cross-dock operations.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Dock Management; Transportation Management System (TMS)

description: Primary fulfillment model supported by this node. Determines order routing eligibility and SLA commitments. FBA-style nodes handle seller-stocked inventory; BOPIS nodes support Buy Online Pick Up In Store; DTC nodes serve direct-to-consumer shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fba_style|bopis|dtc|b2b|omnichannel|cross_dock — promote to reference product]
reference: Order Management System (OMS) - Fulfillment Routing Rules; WMS Facility Configuration

description: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger cost center code associated with this warehouse node for financial reporting, operational cost allocation, and SOX-compliant expense tracking.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Cost Center Accounting; SOX - Internal Controls; ISO 27001

description: GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) — a 13-digit internationally standardized identifier for this warehouse node. Used in EDI transactions, supply chain partner communications, and global trade compliance.
reference: GS1 GLN Standard; WTO International Trade Regulations; EDI Standards

description: Indicates whether the warehouse node operates on a 24-hour, continuous basis. When true, overrides operating_hours_start and operating_hours_end for SLA and routing calculations.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Operating Hours Configuration

description: Indicates whether this warehouse node is operated by a third-party logistics (3PL) provider rather than owned/operated by the business. Drives cost allocation, SLA contract management, and operational reporting.
reference: 3PL Industry Standards; Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) SAP S/4HANA - Procurement

description: Indicates whether the warehouse node has temperature-controlled cold storage capability. Used to route perishable or temperature-sensitive SKUs to eligible nodes during inventory allocation.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Temperature Zone Management; ISO 22000 - Food Safety

description: Indicates whether the warehouse node holds the required certifications to store and handle hazardous materials (hazmat). Drives SKU-level inventory routing and CPSC compliance checks.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Hazmat Storage Standards; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Indicates whether this warehouse node is authorized to receive and process customer returns and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) shipments. Controls return routing logic in the OMS.
reference: Order Management System (OMS) - Returns Routing; Warehouse Management System (WMS) - RMA Processing

description: Indicates whether this is a virtual warehouse node (e.g., a drop-ship node or virtual inventory pool) with no physical facility. Virtual nodes participate in inventory allocation and order routing but have no physical location attributes.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Virtual Node Configuration; OMS - Drop-Ship Routing

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the warehouse node in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used by the TMS for route optimization, delivery radius calculations, and geospatial analytics.
reference: ISO 6709 - Geographic Coordinates; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Route Optimization

description: Date on which the facility lease for this warehouse node expires. Used for network planning, lease renewal management, and long-term capacity forecasting. Applicable to leased facilities only.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Fixed Asset and Lease Management; SOX - Financial Reporting

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the warehouse node in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used by the TMS for route optimization, delivery radius calculations, and geospatial analytics.
reference: ISO 6709 - Geographic Coordinates; Transportation Management System (TMS) - Route Optimization

business_glossary_term: Warehouse Node Maximum Weight Capacity Kilograms
description: Maximum total weight capacity of the warehouse node in kilograms. Used for structural load compliance, inventory planning, and safety management per facility engineering specifications.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Capacity Planning; ISO 45001 - Occupational Health and Safety

description: Hierarchical tier classification of the warehouse node within the fulfillment network topology. Hub nodes serve as primary distribution points; spoke nodes serve last-mile delivery zones. Drives inventory positioning and replenishment flow.
reference: Transportation Management System (TMS) - Network Topology; WMS - Replenishment Planning

description: Externally-known, human-readable alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the warehouse node across all operational systems (WMS, TMS, OMS). Used as the business key in EDI, carrier integrations, and fulfillment routing logic.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Master; GS1 GLN Standard

description: Full descriptive name of the warehouse node as used in operational communications, reporting dashboards, and carrier documentation (e.g., 'Dallas Fulfillment Center - North').
Current operational lifecycle status of the warehouse node. Controls whether the node is eligible to receive inventory allocations, fulfill orders, or participate in replenishment planning.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Lifecycle Management

description: Classification of the warehouse node by its operational role in the fulfillment network. Drives routing logic, SLA assignment, and capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fulfillment_center|distribution_center|dark_store|3pl_facility|drop_ship_node|returns_center — promote to reference product]
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Type Classification; 3PL Industry Standards

description: Comma-separated list of operating days of the week for the warehouse node (e.g., 'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI'). Used for SLA cutoff scheduling, carrier appointment booking, and fulfillment routing logic.
Standard daily operating end time for the warehouse node in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used to determine carrier pickup cutoff windows, SLA calculation, and order routing eligibility.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Operating Hours; SLA Management

description: Standard daily operating start time for the warehouse node in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used to determine carrier pickup cutoff windows, SLA calculation, and order routing eligibility.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Operating Hours; SLA Management

description: Total number of standard pallet positions available in the warehouse node. Used for inventory capacity planning, MOQ compliance, and replenishment scheduling in the WMS.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Capacity Planning; GS1 Pallet Standards

description: Postal or ZIP code of the warehouse node facility. Used for carrier zone mapping, last-mile delivery cost calculation, and geographic analytics.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Universal Postal Union (UPU) Standards

description: Internal geographic region code grouping warehouse nodes for network planning, carrier contract management, and regional fulfillment analytics (e.g., 'US-WEST', 'EU-CENTRAL').
reference: Transportation Management System (TMS) - Network Region Configuration; ERP SAP S/4HANA - Organizational Structure

description: State or province code where the warehouse node is located. Used for tax nexus determination, labor law compliance, and regional fulfillment routing. Follows ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes.
reference: ISO 3166-2; SOX - Tax Nexus Reporting; CCPA

description: Maximum maintained temperature in degrees Celsius for the warehouse node's temperature-controlled storage areas. Applicable only when is_cold_storage is true. Used for SKU eligibility routing and compliance audits.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Temperature Zone Configuration; ISO 22000

description: Minimum maintained temperature in degrees Celsius for the warehouse node's temperature-controlled storage areas. Applicable only when is_cold_storage is true. Used for SKU eligibility routing and compliance audits.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Temperature Zone Configuration; ISO 22000

description: IANA timezone identifier for the warehouse node location (e.g., 'America/Chicago'). Used to correctly interpret operating hours, SLA cutoff times, and carrier pickup windows across distributed fulfillment networks.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; WMS - Operating Hours Configuration

description: Location code assigned by the Transportation Management System (TMS) for this warehouse node. Used for carrier pickup scheduling, route optimization, and shipment origin identification.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the warehouse node master record. Used for change detection, data synchronization with WMS/TMS, and audit trail compliance.
reference: ISO 27001 - Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity

description: Native facility identifier assigned by the Warehouse Management System (WMS) for this warehouse node. Used for system integration, EDI message routing, and reconciliation between the WMS and the lakehouse SSOT.
reference: Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Facility Master; EDI Standards

Floor level within the warehouse where the zone is located.

Target relative humidity percentage for the zone when humidity control is enabled.
Number of distinct SKUs currently stored in the zone.

Maximum total weight the zone can safely hold, expressed in kilograms.

Operational category of the zone based on flow direction and purpose.
System-generated unique identifier for the carrier record.

description: Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: UI displays carrier logo on tracking pages; each carrier must reference a single digital asset containing its logo.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required for Carrier Compliance Program enrollment; carriers must be linked to a compliance program to track certification and audit status.
First line of the carrier's primary business address.
Second line of the carrier's primary business address (optional).
Maximum weight capacity the carrier can transport per shipment.
Full legal name of the transportation carrier.

description: Current lifecycle status of the carrier (e.g., active, inactive, suspended, pending onboarding).
Internal tier used to prioritize carriers based on performance, volume, and strategic importance.
Primary service category the carrier provides (e.g., Full‑Truck‑Load, Less‑Truck‑Load, parcel, last‑mile, cross‑border).
City component of the carrier's primary address.
Indicates whether the carrier holds a valid C‑TPAT certification.

Indicates whether the carrier complies with WTO trade regulations for cross‑border shipments.
Email address for the carrier's primary contact.

Phone number for the carrier's primary contact.
Date when the carrier contract expires or is scheduled for renewal (nullable for open‑ended contracts).
Unique identifier for the active contract with the carrier.
Date when the carrier contract becomes effective.
Three‑letter ISO country code where the carrier is headquartered.
Date‑time when the carrier record was first created in the system.
Three‑letter ISO currency code used for carrier contract financials.
Seven‑digit identifier assigned by the U.S. Department of Transportation to the carrier.
Current status of Electronic Data Interchange integration with the carrier.
Total monetary value of the carrier's liability insurance coverage.

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Contract expenses must be posted to a GL account; linking contracts to general_ledger enables accurate financial statements.

Unit of measure for the contracted volume.

Foreign key linking to search.query_log. Business justification: Attribution of each order to the originating search query for marketing ROI and search performance reporting.

Specifies the domain of the cancellation (e.g., order‑level, line‑item, shipment, payment).
Monetary value of the cancelled items before any refunds.
Total quantity of items cancelled (relevant for partial cancellations).
Timestamp when the cancellation process reached its final state (refunded or closed).
Timestamp when the cancellation record was first created in the system.
True if the cancellation is partial; false if it is a full order cancellation.
Additional free‑form comments or internal notes about the cancellation.
Standardized code representing why the cancellation occurred.
Free‑text description providing additional context for the cancellation reason.
Monetary amount to be refunded to the customer as a result of the cancellation.
Three‑letter ISO currency code of the refund amount.
Indicates whether the cancellation qualifies for a refund according to policy.
Timestamp when the refund was actually processed and posted.
Timestamp when the cancellation request was rejected.
Explanation why a cancellation request was denied.
Timestamp when the cancellation was initially requested.
Indicates whether the cancellation applies to the entire order or only specific line items.
Channel through which the cancellation request was received.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the cancellation record.

Identifier of the order to which this payment allocation belongs.

Identifier of the merchant (seller) receiving the payment.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Payment processing must adhere to PCI DSS obligations; linking each payment to its compliance obligation enables security audit and reporting.
Identifier of the payment transaction from the payment gateway.

Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Required for marketplace split‑payment reconciliation; each payment line must be tied to the seller receiving funds, a standard financial report in e‑commerce.
Gross amount authorized for this payment allocation.
JSON string capturing audit events for this payment allocation.
Timestamp when the payment was authorized.
Timestamp when the payment was captured.
Channel through which the payment was initiated.
Timestamp when this record was created in the data lake.
ISO 4217 currency code of the payment.
Exchange rate applied if settlement currency differs from transaction currency.
Processing fee charged by the payment processor.
Result of fraud detection checks for this payment.
Numeric fraud risk score assigned by the payment gateway.
True if part of the payment was made using a gift card.
Indicates if this record represents a refund transaction.
Indicates if the order payment is part of a split payment across multiple methods.
True if part of the payment was made using store credit.
Number of payment units allocated (typically 1).
Sequence number of this payment allocation line within the payment transaction.
Net amount after taxes and fees.
Additional notes or remarks about the payment.
Optional free-text description of the payment allocation.
Method used for the payment.
Current status of the payment allocation.
Status of financial reconciliation for this payment allocation.
External reference or token from the payment gateway for this payment transaction.
Amount refunded for this payment allocation, if any.
Currency code used for settlement, may differ from transaction currency.
Timestamp when the payment was settled with the acquiring bank.
Tax component of the payment amount, if applicable.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.

Identifier of the specific order line to which the promotion was applied, when applicable.
Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated the promotion, if applicable.
Identifier of the order to which this promotion is applied.
Identifier of the loyalty program associated with the promotion, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to search.merchandising_rule. Business justification: Allows analysis of promotion effectiveness tied to specific merchandising rules, required for promotion performance reporting.
Identifier of the marketplace seller that funded or owns the promotion.
Mechanism that applied the promotion: system, customer service agent, or automated rule.
Exact time when the promotion was applied to the order.
JSON string capturing audit events related to the promotion record.
Timestamp of the most recent compliance verification.
Regulatory compliance state of the promotion (e.g., GDPR, PCI).
Timestamp when the promotion record was first created in the system.
Monetary value of the discount applied (currency defined by discount_currency).
Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which discount_amount is expressed.
Percentage value of the discount when promotion_type is percentage.
Human‑readable explanation when eligibility_status is ineligible or pending.
Result of promotion eligibility validation for this order.
Date when the promotion expires and can no longer be applied.
Indicates whether the promotion is currently active in the system.
Indicates if this promotion may be combined with other promotions beyond stacking_rule logic.
Indicates if the promotion excludes all other promotions when applied.
Indicates whether the promotion discount was later reversed or refunded.
Ordinal position of the promotion within the order's promotion list.
Maximum total number of times a single customer may use this promotion.
Maximum number of times this promotion can be applied within a single order.
Discount amount after tax adjustments, if applicable.
Free‑form field for additional remarks or internal comments.
Human‑readable description of the promotion's purpose and mechanics.
High‑level classification of the promotion for reporting.
Business code that uniquely identifies the promotion rule.
Origin of the promotion – marketing campaign, loyalty program, seller‑funded, or system generated.
Category of promotion: percentage discount, fixed amount, buy‑one‑get‑one, or free shipping.
Number of discount units applied (e.g., number of items covered by a BOGO).
Monetary amount refunded for the promotion, if any.
Time when the promotion refund was processed.
Reference to the specific regulation or policy governing the promotion (e.g., GDPR, PCI DSS).
Rule that defines whether this promotion can be combined with other promotions.
Date when the promotion becomes valid for use.
Indicates whether the discount amount is exempt from sales tax.
System user or process that performed the last update.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion record.
System user or process that created the promotion record.
Unique identifier for the subscription order record.
Identifier of the customer who owns the subscription.

Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: subscription_order represents recurring orders; linking to order_header centralizes order-level data and removes duplication, connecting the previously siloed table.
Identifier of the subscription plan associated with this order.
Revenue recognized on an annual basis from this subscription.
Indicates whether the subscription automatically renews after each cycle.
Recurrence interval for billing the subscription.
Reason provided by the customer or system for cancelling the subscription.
Customer‑selected delivery option for recurring shipments.
Percentage discount applied to the subscription.
Date when the subscription terminates or is scheduled to end (null for open‑ended).
Indicates whether the subscription is currently in a trial period.
Date of the most recent successful payment for this subscription.
Revenue recognized on a monthly basis from this subscription.
Date on which the next recurring order will be generated.
Scheduled date for the upcoming payment.
Free‑form text for internal comments or special instructions.
Code of any promotion applied to the subscription.
Timestamp when the subscription order record was first created.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscription order record.
Standard recurring charge for each billing cycle.
Number of completed renewal cycles for the subscription.
Number of billing cycles the customer has chosen to skip.
Date when the subscription becomes effective.
Total length of the subscription contract in months.
Date when the trial period expires and regular billing begins.
Primary key for subscription_plan
Self-referencing FK on subscription_plan (upgraded_from_subscription_plan_id)
Indicates whether the plan automatically renews at the end of each billing cycle.
Frequency at which customers are billed for the plan.
Policy governing how and when a subscriber may cancel the plan.
Detailed marketing description of the plan, including benefits and features.
Date when the plan becomes legally binding and available for subscription.
Date when the plan is retired or expires; null for open‑ended plans.
Business rules defining which customers may subscribe to the plan.
Identifier of the plan in an external billing or CRM system.
Indicates whether this plan is the default offering for new customers.
Flag to highlight the plan in marketing channels.
Maximum number of items (e.g., orders, downloads) a subscriber may consume per billing cycle.
Maximum number of distinct user accounts allowed under the plan.
Human‑readable name of the subscription plan displayed to customers.
Monetary charge applied per unit when usage exceeds the defined limit.
Business classification of the plan for segmentation and pricing strategy.
External business identifier code for the subscription plan, used in marketing and billing systems.
Category of the plan based on billing cadence or usage model.
Version number of the plan definition, incremented on each change.
Base monetary amount charged per billing cycle.
Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the plan price.
Optional code that provides a discount or special terms for the plan.
Timestamp when the subscription plan record was first created in the data lake.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscription plan record.
Number of months after which the subscription is renewed when auto‑renew is enabled.
Current lifecycle status of the plan.
Email address for customers to contact support regarding this plan.
Indicates whether the plan is exempt from sales tax.
Applicable tax rate percentage for the plan when not tax‑exempt.
Number of days a customer can use the plan for free before regular billing starts.
Monetary amount charged during the trial period, if any.
Overall usage limit (e.g., API calls, storage) associated with the plan.
Unique surrogate key for each tax line applied to an order.
reference: Primary key for the order tax record, globally unique.

description: Identifier of the order to which this tax line belongs.

Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Tax can be applied at the line level; adding order_line_id FK enables precise tax calculations per line and removes ambiguity of using only header_id.
Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Tax compliance requires attributing tax amounts to the responsible seller for jurisdictional reporting and settlement.
Calculated monetary tax amount (taxable_amount * tax_rate).
Exact time when the tax engine performed the calculation.
Timestamp when the tax record was first created in the system.
Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR).
Opaque identifier returned by the tax calculation engine for audit and traceability.
Indicates whether the tax was exempted for this order line (true = exempt, false = taxable).
Reason code or description for tax exemption, if applicable.
Code representing the tax jurisdiction (state, province, country) that governs the tax calculation.
Sequential number of the tax line within the order for ordering purposes.
ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where tax nexus exists.
State or province code within the nexus country that creates tax liability.
Applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0750 for 7.5%).
Current processing status of the tax line.
Category of tax applied: sales tax, VAT, GST, customs duty, or other.
Monetary amount of the order line(s) that is subject to this tax.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the tax record.
Unique system-generated identifier for the Return-to-Vendor (RTV) goods return transaction record. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Returned‑goods audits ensure compliance, detect fraud, and validate supplier performance.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing must allocate reversal amounts to the originating cost center for accurate cost accounting.
Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Links return transactions to the GL account to post reversal entries, required for financial reconciliation.
Reference to the internal procurement buyer or purchasing agent who initiated and owns this RTV return transaction. Used for workflow routing, approval tracking, and performance reporting.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Purchasing Group (T024); Procurement Workflow

description: Reference to the original Goods Receipt (GR) document that recorded the inbound delivery from the supplier. Establishes the SSOT link between the return and the original receipt event.
Reference to the original Purchase Order (PO) against which the goods were received and are now being returned.

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Enables Returns Management dashboard to associate returned items with SKU master data for warranty, inventory, and financial reconciliation.
Reference to the supplier/vendor to whom goods are being returned. Links to the supplier master record in the procurement domain.
Reference to the warehouse or fulfillment center from which the goods are being dispatched back to the supplier.
Timestamp when the RTV return was formally approved by the designated approver. Null if approval is not yet granted or not required. Used for SOX internal control audit trails.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Approval Workflow; SOX Section 404

description: Monetary amount of the vendor chargeback raised against the supplier for costs incurred due to the return (e.g., handling, inspection, re-stocking, logistics). Null if no chargeback is raised.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Accounts Payable (FI-AP); SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether a vendor chargeback has been formally raised against the supplier for this return (True = chargeback raised, False = no chargeback). Triggers the vendor chargeback workflow in accounts payable.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Accounts Payable (FI-AP); Vendor Contract Management

description: System timestamp recording when this goods return record was first created in the ERP/procurement system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Change Document; ISO 27001 Audit Logging

description: Calendar date on which the supplier issued the credit memo for the returned goods. Null until the return reaches 'credit_issued' status. Triggers accounts payable offset in the finance domain.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Accounts Payable (FI-AP); SOX Section 404

description: Actual monetary value of the credit memo received from the supplier. May differ from the expected amount due to restocking fees, partial credit, or supplier disputes. Variance triggers chargeback resolution workflow.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Accounts Payable (FI-AP); SOX Section 404

description: Expected monetary value of the credit memo to be issued by the supplier for the returned goods, calculated based on the original purchase price and return quantity. Used for accounts payable offset and vendor chargeback workflows.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Accounts Payable (FI-AP); SOX Section 404; PCI DSS

description: Supplier-issued credit memo document number received as financial settlement for the returned goods. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and vendor chargeback tracking in the finance domain.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Accounts Payable (FI-AP); SOX Section 404

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this return record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the currency of the original Purchase Order.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ERP SAP S/4HANA - Currency Configuration

description: Customs or export declaration document number required for cross-border return shipments. Null for domestic returns. Required for WTO and EU Electronic Commerce Directive compliance.
reference: WTO International Trade Regulations; Electronic Commerce Directive (EU); Customs and Border Protection

description: Calendar date on which the RTV return was formally initiated by the procurement team. Represents the principal business event date for this transaction. Used for SLA tracking and supplier performance measurement.

Free-text notes recorded by the quality inspector detailing defects, damage observations, or non-conformance findings that support the return decision. Used for supplier quality management and dispute resolution.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; CPSC Product Safety Standards

description: Result of the quality inspection performed on goods prior to initiating the return. PASS indicates goods confirmed defective/returnable, FAIL indicates goods do not meet return criteria, PENDING indicates inspection in progress, WAIVED indicates inspection bypassed per supplier agreement.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; CPSC Product Safety Standards

description: System timestamp recording the most recent modification to this goods return record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail compliance.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Change Document; ISO 27001 Audit Logging

description: Manufacturer or supplier lot/batch number of the returned goods. Critical for traceability in quality defect scenarios, product recalls, and supplier quality audits.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; CPSC Product Safety Standards; GS1 Traceability Standards

description: Human-readable name of the product being returned, as recorded at the time of the return. Denormalized for operational reporting without requiring a product catalog join.
Monetary amount charged by the supplier as a restocking fee for accepting the return. Deducted from the credit memo amount. Tracked for supplier contract compliance and procurement cost management.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Accounts Payable (FI-AP); Supplier Contract Terms

description: Supplier-issued authorization number approving the return of goods. Required before physical shipment of returned goods to the supplier. Distinct from the internal RTV number.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Procurement; Supplier Contract Terms

description: Name of the logistics carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider used to ship the returned goods back to the supplier (e.g., FedEx, UPS, DHL, XPO Logistics).
Number of units of the SKU being returned to the supplier in this RTV transaction. Must be a positive integer and cannot exceed the originally received quantity on the referenced Goods Receipt.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Material Document; WMS Inventory Adjustment

description: Standardized code classifying the reason for returning goods to the supplier. Drives supplier quality management, vendor chargeback workflows, and procurement analytics. Values: QUALITY_DEFECT (goods fail quality inspection), OVERSHIPMENT (supplier shipped excess quantity), WRONG_ITEM (incorrect SKU received), DAMAGED_IN_TRANSIT (goods damaged during inbound delivery), EXPIRED_GOODS (goods received past expiry), CONTRACT_VIOLATION (supplier breached contract terms).
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Return Reason Codes; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Free-text narrative providing additional detail about the reason for the goods return, supplementing the standardized return reason code. Used for supplier dispute resolution and quality audit trails.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Procurement; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Current lifecycle status of the RTV goods return transaction. Tracks progression from initiation through supplier receipt and credit issuance. Values: initiated (RTV created, pending shipment), shipped (goods dispatched to supplier), received_by_supplier (supplier confirmed receipt), credit_issued (credit memo received and applied), cancelled (return voided).
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Procurement Workflow; FTC Consumer Protection Standards

description: Classification of the return scope. FULL_RETURN indicates all received units are being returned; PARTIAL_RETURN indicates a subset of received units; REPLACEMENT_REQUEST indicates goods are returned in exchange for correct replacement items from the supplier.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Procurement; Supplier Contract Management

description: Specific storage location or bin within the warehouse where returned goods are staged prior to dispatch to the supplier. Used by WMS for pick-pack-ship operations.

Externally-known business identifier for the Return-to-Vendor (RTV) transaction. Used in supplier communications, logistics documentation, and credit memo reconciliation. Format: RTV-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Return Purchase Order; ISO 6422 Business Document Standards

description: Individual unit serial number for serialized products being returned. Applicable for high-value or regulated items requiring unit-level traceability. Null for non-serialized goods.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Serial Number Management; ISO 9001 Traceability

description: Calendar date on which the returned goods were physically dispatched from the warehouse to the supplier. Null until the return reaches 'shipped' status.

Mode of transportation used for the return shipment. FTL (Full Truck Load) for large returns, LTL (Less Than Truck Load) for partial loads, PARCEL for small packages, EXPRESS for urgent returns, FREIGHT for heavy/bulk goods.
reference: Transportation Management System (TMS); WTO International Trade Regulations

description: Email address of the supplier representative managing this return. Used for return authorization communications and credit memo follow-up. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR.
reference: GDPR Article 4; ERP SAP S/4HANA - Vendor Contact

description: Name of the supplier representative responsible for managing this return authorization and credit memo issuance. Used for procurement communication and dispute escalation.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Vendor Contact (LFA1); GDPR Article 6

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the supplier's location to which goods are being returned. Used for customs documentation, international trade compliance, and logistics routing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; WTO International Trade Regulations; Electronic Commerce Directive (EU)

description: Calendar date on which the supplier confirmed receipt of the returned goods. Null until the return reaches 'received_by_supplier' status. Used to calculate supplier return processing lead time.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Procurement; Supplier SLA Management

description: Carrier-issued tracking number for the return shipment. Enables real-time shipment status monitoring via the Transportation Management System and provides proof of dispatch for supplier disputes.
reference: Transportation Management System (TMS); Carrier API Integration

description: Unit of measure for the return quantity (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Litre, M=Metre, BOX=Box, PALLET=Pallet). Aligns with the unit of measure on the original Purchase Order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|KG|LB|L|M|BOX|PALLET — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Unit of Measure (T006); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Per-unit price at which the goods are being returned to the supplier, typically matching the original Purchase Order unit price. Used to calculate the expected credit memo amount and for procurement cost analysis.
reference: ERP SAP S/4HANA - Purchase Order Pricing (EKPO); SOX Section 404

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each purchase order line item record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 — Data Element Naming and Definition

description: Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Needed for line‑item cost analysis: each PO line item references the exact price‑list item that supplied its unit price.

Foreign key reference to the parent purchase order header. Establishes the header-to-line relationship enabling multi-SKU PO tracking and partial fulfillment analysis. Mandatory for all line items. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: HEADER_REFERENCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Purchase Order (EKPO/EKKO tables); ISO/IEC 11179

description: Reference to the product/material master record for the item being procured on this line. Links to the Product Information Management System for catalog enrichment and attribute management. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: RESOURCE_REFERENCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master (MARA/MARC); GS1 Global Product Classification

description: Reference to the supplier/vendor fulfilling this line item. Enables supplier-level performance analysis, lead time tracking, and vendor compliance monitoring within the procure-to-pay process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (LFA1); ISO 9001 Supplier Management

description: ERP account assignment category determining how the PO line item cost is posted in the general ledger (e.g., K=Cost Center, F=Order, P=Project, A=Asset, C=Sales Order, U=Unknown). Required for financial posting and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-KNTTP; SOX Section 302/404; IFRS IAS 16

description: The actual date on which goods were received at the warehouse for this line item. Populated upon goods receipt posting in the WMS. Used for supplier on-time delivery performance measurement and lead time actuals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MKPF-BUDAT (Goods Receipt); WMS Receiving Module

description: The delivery date confirmed by the supplier for this line item, as communicated via PO acknowledgement or supplier portal. Used for lead time analysis, on-time delivery KPI tracking, and logistics planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-EINDT (confirmed); ANSI X12 EDI 855 PO Acknowledgement

description: ERP cost center to which the procurement cost for this line item is allocated. Used for budget tracking, departmental spend reporting, and financial controlling. Applicable when account_assignment_category = K.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — EKKN-KOSTL; SOX Section 302/404; IFRS Management Accounting

description: Timestamp when this PO line item record was first created in the source ERP system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and procurement process timing analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA change documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — CDHDR/CDPOS Change Documents; ISO 27001 Audit Logging; SOX Section 302

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit price and line total amount on this PO line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency procurement and FX reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM — EKKO-WAERS; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Difference in calendar days between the confirmed delivery date and the actual delivery date for this line item. Positive value indicates late delivery; negative indicates early delivery. Key supplier on-time delivery (OTD) performance metric.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Derived from EKPO dates; Supplier Performance Management (ISO 9001)

description: Percentage discount negotiated with the supplier for this line item, applied to the gross unit price to arrive at the net unit price. Supports procurement cost analysis and vendor contract compliance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — KONP-KBETR (Pricing Conditions); Supplier Contract Terms

description: Indicates whether the supplier has confirmed this as the final delivery for this PO line item, closing it against further goods receipts. True = final delivery confirmed; False = further deliveries expected. Used to close open PO lines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-ELIKZ; Procurement Lifecycle Management

description: General ledger account code to which the procurement expense or inventory value for this line item is posted. Supports financial reporting, accounts payable accruals, and SOX audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL — EKKN-SAKTO; SOX Section 302/404; IFRS IAS 1

description: Total monetary value of goods received against this PO line (goods_receipt_quantity × unit_price). Used for inventory valuation, accrual accounting, and accounts payable liability recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI — MSEG-DMBTR; SOX Section 302/404; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories

description: Indicates whether goods receipt posting is blocked for this PO line item (e.g., due to quality inspection hold, price discrepancy, or compliance issue). True = GR blocked; False = GR allowed. Used in WMS receiving workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-WEPOS/SPEME; WMS Quality Control; ISO 9001

description: Total quantity of this line item received into the warehouse against this PO line. Populated from WMS goods receipt transactions. Used for partial fulfillment tracking, open quantity calculation, and 3-way invoice matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG-MENGE (Movement Type 101); WMS Receiving Module

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and supplier for this line item. Critical for cross-border procurement and logistics cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM — EKKO-INCO1; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: Indicates whether invoice verification (3-way match: PO vs. GR vs. invoice) has been completed for this line item. True = verified; False = pending verification. Used for accounts payable processing and payment release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — EKPO-REPOS; SOX Section 302/404; PCI DSS (payment processing)

description: Total monetary value invoiced by the supplier for this PO line. Used for 3-way match variance analysis, accounts payable processing, and financial accrual reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — RSEG-WRBTR; SOX Section 302/404; IFRS IAS 37

description: Total quantity of this line item for which a supplier invoice has been received and matched. Used for 3-way match (PO quantity vs. GR quantity vs. invoice quantity), accounts payable processing, and payment settlement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — RSEG-MENGE (Invoice Verification); SOX Section 302/404

description: Short description of the product or material being procured on this line, as captured at the time of PO creation. May differ from the current PIM catalog description if the product was updated after PO issuance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-TXZ01; ANSI X12 EDI 850

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PO line item record in the source ERP system. Used for incremental data loading (ETL), change tracking, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — CDHDR/CDPOS Change Documents; ISO 27001 Audit Logging; SOX Section 302

description: Number of calendar days between PO issuance and expected delivery for this line item, as agreed with the supplier. Used for inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and supplier performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Info Record (EINE-WEBAZ); Supply Chain Planning Best Practices

description: Sequential line number within the parent purchase order, used to uniquely identify and order line items within a PO. Enables multi-SKU PO navigation and EDI interchange alignment. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: LINE_SEQUENCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-EBELP; ANSI X12 EDI 850 Purchase Order

description: Current workflow status of this purchase order line item. Tracks the lifecycle from open order through goods receipt, invoicing, and closure. Enables partial fulfillment monitoring and open PO reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_received|fully_received|invoiced|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-ELIKZ/EREKZ; ANSI X12 EDI 855 PO Acknowledgement

description: Total monetary value of this line item (ordered_quantity × unit_price). Used for PO value tracking, budget commitment, accounts payable accruals, and financial reporting. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — EKPO-NETWR; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 21001

description: The supplier's minimum order quantity requirement for this SKU as defined in the purchase info record or vendor contract. Used to validate moq_compliance_flag and support procurement planning decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Info Record (EINE-MINBM); Supplier Contract Terms

description: Indicates whether the ordered quantity on this line meets or exceeds the supplier's Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement. True = MOQ compliant; False = below MOQ threshold. Critical for supplier contract compliance and procurement workflow validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Info Record (EINE-MINBM); Supplier Contract Terms

description: Remaining quantity yet to be received against this PO line (ordered_quantity minus goods_receipt_quantity). Supports open PO reporting, inventory replenishment monitoring, and supplier follow-up workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Derived from EKPO-MENGE minus EKPO-WEMNG; Procurement Analytics

description: Quantity of the SKU ordered on this line item as agreed with the supplier. Used for MOQ compliance validation, inventory replenishment planning, and partial fulfillment tracking. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role category: LINE_QUANTITY.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-MENGE; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: ERP plant or fulfillment center code to which this PO line item is being delivered. Identifies the receiving warehouse or distribution center in the fulfillment network. Used for inventory planning and logistics routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-WERKS; WMS Facility Management

description: Monetary difference between the PO agreed unit price and the actual invoiced price for this line item. A key procurement KPI used for cost control, supplier negotiation analysis, and financial reporting under SOX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Purchase Price Variance (PPV); SOX Section 302/404; IFRS IAS 2

description: Standardized code indicating the reason a PO line item was rejected, cancelled, or blocked (e.g., quality failure, price discrepancy, supplier non-compliance). Used for procurement analytics and supplier performance management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKPO-ABGRU; ISO 9001 Non-Conformance Management

reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Fiscal Year; SOX Financial Controls

description: Name or code of the business unit or department that originated the purchase requisition. Supports spend analytics, budget allocation, and organizational reporting across procurement categories.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; Internal Procurement Policy

description: Indicates whether this requisition has been reviewed and confirmed as compliant with applicable procurement policies, vendor compliance requirements, and regulatory standards (e.g., CPSC product safety, FTC advertising standards). Set to True upon compliance review completion.
reference: FTC Consumer Protection Standards; CPSC Product Safety; Internal Procurement Compliance Policy

description: Date and time when the purchase requisition record was first created in the system, whether by a business user, buyer, or automated replenishment trigger. Serves as the audit trail anchor for the procure-to-pay workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Creation Date (BADAT); SOX Internal Controls

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated cost values on this requisition (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency procurement operations and financial consolidation in the ERP.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Configuration

description: Physical delivery address for the requisitioned goods, which may differ from the default warehouse address (e.g., direct-to-store, drop-ship, or project site delivery). Used for purchase order delivery instructions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Delivery Address; Internal Logistics Policy

description: The SAP S/4HANA purchase requisition document number (BANFN) assigned by the ERP system upon creation. Serves as the cross-system reference key linking the data lakehouse record to the source-of-truth ERP transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Purchase Requisition Number (BANFN); EDI Integration Standards

description: Total estimated cost for the requisition line, calculated as requested_quantity multiplied by estimated_unit_cost. Used for budget validation, approval routing by spend threshold, and procurement spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Total Value; SOX Financial Controls; Internal Procurement Policy

description: Estimated cost per unit of the requested item at the time of requisition creation. Used for budget validation, approval threshold checks, and spend forecasting. May be sourced from contract pricing, catalog price, or buyer estimate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Valuation Price (PREIS); SOX Financial Controls

description: The General Ledger (GL) account code to which the requisition expenditure will be posted upon purchase order creation and goods receipt. Ensures correct financial classification and ERP account assignment for procure-to-pay.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module - GL Account (SAKNR); SOX Financial Controls; GAAP

description: Indicates whether this requisition was automatically generated by the min/max reorder point replenishment system (True) or manually raised by a business user or buyer (False). Supports process analytics and system-vs-manual sourcing performance comparison.
reference: Warehouse Management System - Replenishment Rules; SAP S/4HANA MRP

description: Free-text description of the goods or services being requested. Used when a specific SKU is not available or for service requisitions where no catalog item exists. Supports buyer interpretation and sourcing decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Short Text (TXZ01); ISO 9001

description: Free-text business justification provided by the requester explaining the need for the goods or services. Required for high-value or non-catalog requisitions to support approval decisions and audit trail documentation.
reference: Internal Procurement Policy - Requisition Justification Requirements; SOX Internal Controls

description: The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required by the preferred supplier for the requested item. Used to validate that the requested_quantity meets supplier requirements and to adjust order quantities during PO conversion.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Minimum Order Quantity (MINBM); Supplier Contract Terms

description: Business priority level assigned to the requisition, indicating processing urgency. Drives buyer workqueue prioritization, approval SLA targets, and expediting decisions for time-sensitive procurement needs.
reference: Internal Procurement Policy - SLA Framework; ISO 9001

description: Spend category classification for the requisitioned item or service (e.g., Direct Materials, Indirect Goods, IT Equipment, Logistics Services, Marketing). Used for category management, spend analytics, and sourcing strategy alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Group (MATKL); UNSPSC Classification Standard

description: Reason code or free-text explanation provided when a requisition is rejected by an approver. Used for requester feedback, process improvement analytics, and audit trail documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Rejection Reason; Internal Procurement Policy

description: The specific replenishment trigger method that initiated this requisition when is_automated_trigger is True. Values include min/max inventory rules, reorder point (ROP), MRP planning run, manual buyer action, or Kanban signal.
reference: Warehouse Management System - Replenishment Configuration; SAP S/4HANA MRP

description: The quantity of goods or services requested in the requisition, expressed in the unit of measure. Must meet or exceed the supplier Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) for the preferred supplier where applicable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Quantity (MENGE); GS1 Standards

description: The date by which the requested goods or services must be delivered or available. Drives procurement urgency classification, supplier lead time validation, and delivery SLA commitments. Key input for purchase order scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Delivery Date (LFDAT); ISO 9001 Procurement Process

description: Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the requisition, used in communications with approvers, buyers, and finance teams. Follows the format REQ-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Purchase Requisition Number (BANFN)

description: Current lifecycle state of the purchase requisition within the procure-to-pay workflow. Drives routing, approval actions, and downstream PO conversion eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Requisition Processing Status; ISO 9001 Procurement Process

description: Classification of the requisition by procurement category and urgency. Determines applicable approval thresholds, sourcing rules, and processing SLAs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|blanket|emergency|replenishment|service|capital — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Requisition Document Type; ISO 9001

description: Recommended or applied sourcing approach for fulfilling this requisition. Determines whether the buyer should reference an existing contract, use a catalog price, issue an RFQ, or execute a spot buy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Source of Supply; Internal Procurement Policy; ISO 9001

description: Date and time when the requisition was formally submitted for approval, transitioning from draft to pending_approval status. Key lifecycle event timestamp for SLA measurement and approval cycle time analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Release Date; ISO 9001 Procurement Process

description: Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt for the preferred supplier. Used to validate the required_by_date feasibility and to calculate the latest PO creation date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Planned Delivery Time (PLIFZ); Supplier Performance Management

description: Standard unit of measure for the requested quantity (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, KG=Kilogram, L=Litre). Aligns with GS1 and SAP UOM standards for inventory and procurement processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|KG|LB|L|M|M2|M3|PK|BX|DZ|HR|DAY — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Unit of Measure (MEINS); GS1 Standards; ISO 80000

description: Date and time when the purchase requisition record was most recently modified, including status changes, quantity revisions, or approval actions. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Change Timestamp; SOX Internal Controls

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier invoice record in the procurement data platform. Primary key for the supplier_invoice data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO - AP Invoice Verification

description: Reference to the employee or procurement manager who approved or rejected this invoice in the AP verification workflow. Required for audit trail and SOX segregation of duties compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Workflow SWI; SOX Section 302 - Management Certification; COSO Framework

description: Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: Invoices for purchased assets need to reference the digital asset to reconcile payments, cost allocation, and licensing.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supplier invoice expense must be charged to a cost center for budgeting and variance analysis; replaces denormalized codes.
Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Links each supplier invoice to the GL account for proper posting, required by the accounts payable workflow.

Reference to the Goods Receipt (GR) document confirming physical receipt of goods or services. Required for three-way match validation (PO/GR/Invoice). Maps to MIGO goods receipt posting in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIGO - Goods Receipt; WMS Receiving Module

description: Reference to the originating Purchase Order (PO) against which this invoice is submitted. Central to three-way match validation — the PO defines the agreed quantities, prices, and terms that the invoice must reconcile against.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME21N - Purchase Order; ISO 6422

description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Finance: invoices are issued to sellers for goods received; linking supports accounts payable and tax reporting per seller.
Reference to the supplier (vendor) who submitted this invoice. Links to the supplier master record for vendor details, payment terms, and compliance status.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master (LFA1); ERP Procurement Module

description: Current state of the invoice in the procurement approval workflow. Distinct from invoice_status which tracks the full lifecycle; approval_status specifically tracks the authorization decision required before payment release. ESCALATED indicates the invoice has been routed to a higher approval authority due to amount threshold or discrepancy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Workflow (SWI); SOX Section 302 - Financial Controls

description: Date and time when the invoice was approved (or rejected) by the designated approver in the procurement workflow. Critical for SLA (Service Level Agreement) measurement of invoice processing cycle time and SOX audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Workflow SWI; SOX Section 302; COSO Internal Controls

description: Masked or tokenized reference to the supplier's bank account designated for payment of this invoice. Stored as a reference token rather than raw account number to comply with PCI DSS. Full banking details are maintained in the secure vendor master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Vendor Bank Details (LFBK); PCI DSS v4.0 Section 3; SOX Section 404

description: Date and time when the supplier invoice record was first created in the procurement data platform. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and is used for invoice aging calculations from receipt date. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category requirement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CPUDT/CPUTM; SOX Section 404; ISO 27001 - Audit Logging

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP processing and foreign exchange revaluation. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO WAERS currency key.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA WAERS; GDPR Article 5

description: Cash discount amount available if payment is made within the early payment discount period as defined by the payment terms. Supports working capital optimization and dynamic discounting programs. Maps to SAP S/4HANA SKFBT cash discount base amount.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SKFBT; ERP Procurement Module; SOX Section 404

description: The deadline by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount specified in the payment terms. After this date, the full gross_amount is due by payment_due_date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Payment Terms ZTERM; ERP Procurement Module

description: Monetary value of the discrepancy between the invoiced amount and the expected amount based on PO and GR. Quantifies the financial exposure of the mismatch for prioritization of resolution and escalation decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO Tolerance Keys; SOX Section 404 - AP Controls

description: Free-text description of the specific discrepancy identified during three-way match validation or manual review. Provides context for resolution workflow, supplier dispute management, and audit trail. Populated when three_way_match_status is FAILED or PARTIAL_MATCH.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO; COSO Internal Controls Framework; SOX Section 404

description: Classification of the discrepancy identified during three-way match validation. PRICE_VARIANCE: invoiced unit price differs from PO price beyond tolerance; QUANTITY_VARIANCE: invoiced quantity exceeds GR quantity; MISSING_GR: no goods receipt found; DUPLICATE_INVOICE: invoice number already processed; INCORRECT_PO: PO reference invalid or mismatched; TAX_MISMATCH: tax amount inconsistency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO Tolerance Keys; COSO Internal Controls Framework; SOX Section 404

description: Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to the company's functional/local currency at the time of invoice posting. Relevant for cross-border supplier invoices. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO KURSF exchange rate field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO KURSF; ISO 4217; SOX Section 404 - FX Controls

description: The fiscal accounting period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice is posted. Two-digit format (01-12). Used for monthly accruals, period-end AP aging, and financial close reporting. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MONAT posting period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MONAT; SOX Section 404; GAAP/IFRS Period Reporting

description: The fiscal year in which the invoice is posted for accounting and financial reporting purposes. Used for period-end close, budget consumption tracking, and SOX-compliant financial reporting. Maps to SAP S/4HANA GJAHR fiscal year field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GJAHR; SOX Section 404; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting

description: Gross invoice amount converted to the company's functional (local) currency using the exchange_rate at posting time. Used for GL posting, financial reporting, and SOX-compliant AP ledger entries in the finance domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO DMBTR; SOX Section 404; IFRS IAS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Total invoice amount inclusive of all taxes and charges (net_amount + tax_amount). Represents the total liability to be settled with the supplier. Used for AP aging, cash flow forecasting, and payment run in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO WRBTR Gross; ISO 4217; SOX Section 404

description: Business category of the goods or services being invoiced. GOODS: physical merchandise; SERVICES: professional or managed services; FREIGHT: logistics and shipping charges; UTILITIES: energy, water, telecom; CAPEX: capital expenditure items; INTERCOMPANY: invoices between related entities. Used for spend analytics and GL account determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Account Assignment Category; ERP Procurement Module; SOX Section 404

description: The date printed on the supplier's invoice document, representing the date the supplier issued the invoice. Used as the principal business event date for payment due date calculation and aging analysis. Distinct from the system receipt date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO BLDAT; ISO 6422 - Layout of Trade Documents

description: Externally-assigned invoice number as submitted by the supplier on the invoice document. Used as the primary business identifier for three-way match (Purchase Order/Goods Receipt/Invoice) validation and AP correspondence. Maps to MIRO BELNR field in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO; ISO 6422 - Layout of Trade Documents

description: Current lifecycle state of the invoice within the procurement AP verification workflow.
