RECEIVED: invoice logged; UNDER_REVIEW: validation in progress; MATCHED: three-way match successful; DISCREPANCY: match failed; APPROVED: cleared for payment; REJECTED: invoice rejected; PAID: payment disbursed; CANCELLED: voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECEIVED|UNDER_REVIEW|MATCHED|DISCREPANCY|APPROVED|REJECTED|PAID|CANCELLED — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO Workflow Status; SOX Section 404 - AP Controls

description: Classification of the invoice document type. STANDARD: regular goods/services invoice; CREDIT_NOTE: supplier-issued credit reducing liability; DEBIT_NOTE: additional charge from supplier; PRO_FORMA: preliminary invoice for customs/planning; RECURRING: periodic service invoice.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Invoice Document Types; ISO 6422

description: The invoiced amount for goods or services before tax, representing the base payable amount. Used as the primary monetary value for PO price comparison in three-way match. Part of the monetary triplet (net_amount + tax_amount = gross_amount).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO WRBTR; ISO 4217 Currency Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether the invoice has been blocked from payment processing. True when a payment block is active due to discrepancy, pending approval, compliance hold, or manual block. Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment block indicator (ZLSPR) on the AP document. Finance domain uses this flag to exclude the invoice from payment run.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO ZLSPR Payment Block; SOX Section 404 - AP Controls

description: The date by which payment must be made to the supplier to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date and supplier payment terms but stored explicitly for operational payment scheduling and cash flow planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Payment Terms (ZTERM); SOX Section 404 - AP Controls

description: The payment instrument or method designated for settling this invoice. ACH: Automated Clearing House bank transfer; WIRE: international wire transfer; CHECK: paper check; VIRTUAL_CARD: single-use virtual procurement card; DYNAMIC_DISCOUNT: early payment via supply chain finance program. Informs the payment run configuration in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Payment Method (ZLSCH); PCI DSS v4.0 - Payment Controls; ERP Procurement Module

description: Code identifying the agreed payment terms for this invoice (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for early payment discount). Determines the payment due date and any applicable early payment discount. Maps to SAP S/4HANA ZTERM payment terms key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ZTERM Payment Terms; ERP Procurement Module

description: The date the invoice was physically or electronically received by the procurement team. Used to calculate payment due dates based on payment terms and to measure invoice processing cycle time. Maps to MIRO BUDAT (posting date) in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO BUDAT; FTC Consumer Protection Standards

description: Free-text explanation provided by the approver when the invoice is rejected. Used for supplier communication, dispute resolution, and process improvement analytics. Populated only when approval_status is REJECTED.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO; FTC Consumer Protection Standards; ERP Procurement Module

description: The SAP S/4HANA accounting document number (BELNR) generated upon posting of the invoice in MIRO. Serves as the cross-reference key between the procurement data product and the finance domain's AP ledger and GL entries. Null until the invoice is posted in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO BELNR; SOX Section 404 - AP Audit Trail

description: The originating system or channel through which the invoice was received and entered into the procurement platform. SAP_MIRO: direct SAP AP verification entry; EDI: Electronic Data Interchange automated feed; SUPPLIER_PORTAL: self-service supplier submission; EMAIL: email-based invoice receipt; MANUAL: manual data entry by AP team.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO; EDI X12 810 Invoice Standard; ERP Procurement Module

description: Supplier's own internal reference or order number associated with this invoice, as provided by the vendor. Used for supplier reconciliation, dispute resolution, and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) matching with supplier systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO XBLNR External Reference; EDI X12 810 Invoice Standard

description: Total tax amount charged on the invoice, including VAT, GST, or applicable sales tax as specified by the supplier. Used for tax reconciliation and input tax credit recovery. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO WMWST tax amount field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO WMWST; GDPR Article 5; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; SOX Section 404

description: Tax classification code applied to the invoice for VAT/GST determination and input tax recovery. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO MWSKZ tax code, which drives the tax calculation procedure and GL tax account assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO MWSKZ; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; GDPR Article 5

description: Result of the three-way match validation comparing the Purchase Order (PO), Goods Receipt (GR), and supplier invoice for quantity and price alignment. PENDING: match not yet executed; MATCHED: all three documents reconcile within tolerance; PARTIAL_MATCH: some line items match; FAILED: material discrepancy detected. Core control in the procure-to-pay process per SAP MIRO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MIRO Three-Way Match; SOX Section 404; COSO Internal Controls Framework

description: Date and time when the supplier invoice record was last modified in the procurement data platform. Tracks the most recent status change, discrepancy update, or approval action. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AEDAT/AEZET; SOX Section 404; ISO 27001 - Audit Logging

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a Request for Quotation (RFQ) or strategic sourcing event record in the procurement domain.

Reference to the employee or manager who approved the RFQ for issuance to suppliers. Required for procurement governance and SOX internal controls compliance. Populated when the RFQ transitions from draft to issued status.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Release Strategy (ME28); SOX Section 302 Internal Controls; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Reference to the supplier record that was selected as the winner of this RFQ. Populated upon award decision. For split awards, references the primary/lead supplier. Links to the supplier master in the procurement domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master (LFA1); ISO 9001 Clause 8.4.1 Supplier Selection

description: Reference to the cost center that will bear the procurement spend resulting from this RFQ. Used for budget allocation, spend tracking, and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA CO module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center (CSKS); SOX Financial Reporting; GAAP Cost Allocation Standards

description: Foreign key linking to customer.profile. Business justification: Captures which customer.profile initiated an RFQ, essential for spend attribution and RFQ performance analysis.

Reference to the internal employee or procurement category manager responsible for managing this RFQ from initiation through award. Used for accountability tracking, workload management, and audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Sourcing Event Responsible Person; ISO 9001 Clause 5.3 Organizational Roles

description: Date on which the sourcing event award decision was finalized and communicated to the winning supplier(s). Marks the closure of the competitive bidding phase and triggers purchase order or contract creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Quotation Award; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4 Supplier Selection

description: Outcome of the sourcing event evaluation: single_award (one supplier selected for full volume), split_award (volume distributed across multiple suppliers), no_award (no suitable bid received), re_tender (event cancelled and reissued). Drives downstream purchase order and contract creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Award Decision; WTO GPA Article XIII Award of Contracts; CIPS Award Procedures

description: Total monetary value of the contract or purchase agreement awarded as a result of this RFQ, expressed in the RFQ currency. Used for spend commitment tracking, budget allocation, and savings realization reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Value; SOX Financial Reporting; CIPS Contract Value Management

description: Standardized procurement category code classifying the goods or services being sourced (e.g., electronics, packaging, 3PL logistics, IT hardware). Aligns with the enterprise category taxonomy maintained in SAP S/4HANA and used for spend analytics and category management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group; UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code)

description: Human-readable name of the procurement category for the sourcing event (e.g., 'Consumer Electronics', 'Corrugated Packaging', 'Last-Mile Delivery Services'). Used in reporting dashboards and supplier communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group Description; UNSPSC Category Taxonomy

description: Deadline by which suppliers may submit questions or requests for clarification on the RFQ requirements. Responses to clarifications are typically shared with all invited suppliers to ensure fair competition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Clarification Period; WTO GPA Article XI; FTC Fair Competition Standards

description: Planned or actual date on which the resulting contract or purchase agreement expires. Used for contract renewal planning and procurement calendar management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Validity End; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: Planned or actual date from which the resulting contract or purchase agreement becomes effective following the RFQ award. Used for procurement planning and contract lifecycle management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Outline Agreement Validity Start; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4

description: System timestamp recording when the RFQ record was first created in the procurement system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and procurement cycle time analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Log; ISO 27001 Audit Trail Requirements; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values (target price, estimated savings, bid amounts) for this RFQ are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting and FX conversion in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Configuration; SOX Financial Reporting Standards

description: Percentage weight assigned to the delivery/lead time criterion in the supplier evaluation scorecard. Reflects the importance of supply chain reliability and on-time delivery performance in the award decision.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Evaluation Weighting; CIPS Supplier Performance Framework; WTO GPA Article X

description: Projected total annual spend value for the goods or services covered by this RFQ, used for spend analysis, savings opportunity sizing, and procurement prioritization. Expressed in the RFQ currency. Confidential business data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Spend Analytics; CIPS Category Management; SOX Spend Controls

description: Projected cost savings expected to be realized through this sourcing event, calculated as the difference between the current/baseline spend and the anticipated awarded price. Used for procurement KPI reporting and budget planning. Expressed in the RFQ currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Savings Tracking; CIPS Procurement Savings Methodology; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Structured description of the criteria used to evaluate and score supplier responses (e.g., price, quality, delivery lead time, sustainability, financial stability, compliance). Stored as a structured text or JSON-serialized string capturing criterion names and their relative weightings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Evaluation Scorecard; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4.1 Supplier Evaluation; WTO GPA Article X Award Criteria

description: Target date by which the procurement team must complete evaluation and scoring of all supplier responses. Used to track procurement cycle time and ensure timely award decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Evaluation Timeline; CIPS Procurement Cycle Time KPI

description: Classification of the sourcing event methodology: open_tender (unrestricted supplier participation), selective (pre-qualified suppliers only), single_source (sole supplier negotiation), reverse_auction (real-time competitive bidding), annual_category_bid (periodic category-level strategic sourcing).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Sourcing Event Types; WTO Government Procurement Agreement (GPA) Article VII

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) specifying the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and supplier for this RFQ. Standardized by the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM Incoterms Configuration; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: Number of suppliers formally invited to participate in this RFQ or sourcing event. Used to assess competitive intensity and compliance with procurement policy (e.g., minimum 3 quotes requirement).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Supplier Invitation; WTO GPA Article IX; CIPS Competitive Tendering Policy

description: Indicates whether this RFQ is classified as confidential, restricting visibility to authorized procurement personnel only. Confidential RFQs may involve sensitive pricing strategies, new product launches, or strategic supplier negotiations.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 Data Minimization; SOX Internal Controls

description: Indicates whether this sourcing event involves multiple bidding rounds (e.g., initial bid followed by best-and-final-offer round). True for complex strategic sourcing events; False for single-round tactical RFQs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Multi-Round Bidding; CIPS Strategic Sourcing Methodology

description: The calendar date on which the RFQ was formally issued and made available to invited suppliers. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the sourcing event lifecycle. Used to calculate response windows and measure procurement cycle times.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME41 RFQ Issue Date; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4 Procurement Process

description: Maximum acceptable supplier lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to delivery, as specified in the RFQ requirements. Used as a mandatory or scored criterion in supplier bid evaluation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record Lead Time; CIPS Supply Chain Lead Time Management

description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required per supplier response for this RFQ. Defines the smallest acceptable order volume that suppliers must be able to fulfill. Used in supplier bid evaluation and purchase order planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record MOQ; CIPS Procurement Terms

description: Standard payment terms required for the resulting contract or purchase order (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Communicated to suppliers as part of the RFQ commercial requirements and used in SAP S/4HANA accounts payable configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms (T052); CIPS Commercial Terms; SOX Accounts Payable Controls

description: Percentage weight assigned to the price criterion in the supplier evaluation scorecard. Together with other criterion weights, must sum to 100%. Used to ensure transparent and auditable supplier selection decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Evaluation Weighting; WTO GPA Article X; FTC Fair Competition Standards

description: Percentage weight assigned to the quality criterion in the supplier evaluation scorecard. Reflects the relative importance of product/service quality versus price and other factors in the award decision.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Evaluation Weighting; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4.1; WTO GPA Article X

description: Actual cost savings achieved through this sourcing event, calculated as the difference between the baseline spend and the awarded amount. Populated post-award. Used for procurement performance reporting and ROI measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Savings Tracking; CIPS Savings Realization; SOX Financial Controls

description: Actual number of supplier quotation responses received by the submission deadline. Used to measure supplier engagement, assess market competitiveness, and determine if re-tendering is required.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Quotation Comparison; CIPS Supplier Engagement Metrics

description: Detailed narrative description of the scope, requirements, and objectives of the sourcing event. Includes business context, technical specifications summary, and any special conditions communicated to suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Sourcing Event Description; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4.3 Information for External Providers

description: Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the RFQ or sourcing event, used in supplier communications, ERP references, and audit trails. Follows the format RFQ-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing — RFQ Object (ME41); ISO 9001 Procurement Process Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the RFQ or sourcing event: draft (being prepared), issued (sent to suppliers), responses_received (supplier bids collected), evaluated (bids scored), awarded (supplier selected and notified), cancelled (event terminated without award).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing — RFQ Status Management; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4 External Provider Control

description: Estimated savings as a percentage of the baseline spend, providing a normalized measure of sourcing event value. Used in procurement performance dashboards and category management reporting.
reference: CIPS Procurement Savings KPI; SAP S/4HANA Savings Reporting

description: Narrative description of the procurement strategy applied to this sourcing event, such as multi-supplier diversification, preferred supplier consolidation, total cost of ownership (TCO) optimization, or category-specific strategic sourcing approach. Free-text field capturing the strategic rationale documented by the category manager.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Strategic Sourcing; CIPS (Chartered Institute of Procurement and Supply) Sourcing Strategy Framework

description: Exact date and time by which suppliers must submit their quotation responses. After this timestamp, late submissions are rejected. Critical for procurement SLA compliance and supplier portal access control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Sourcing Event Deadline; WTO GPA Article XI Tendering Procedures

description: Indicates whether this RFQ includes mandatory sustainability, ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance), or ethical sourcing compliance requirements as part of the supplier evaluation criteria.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; CIPS Ethical Procurement Standards; EU Electronic Commerce Directive

description: Internal benchmark or should-cost price per unit that the procurement team aims to achieve through the sourcing event. Used as the baseline for evaluating supplier bids and calculating estimated savings. Confidential — not disclosed to suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Price Conditions; CIPS Should-Cost Modeling; SOX Internal Controls

description: Short descriptive title of the RFQ or sourcing event as communicated to invited suppliers (e.g., 'Annual Electronics Components Tender Q1 2025', 'Last-Mile Carrier Bid — Southeast Region'). Used in supplier portal listings and internal procurement dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SRM Sourcing Event Header; CIPS Procurement Best Practices

description: Aggregate quantity of all goods or services requested across all line items in the RFQ. Used for volume-based negotiation leverage and supplier capacity planning. Unit of measure is defined at the line item level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM RFQ Quantity; ISO 9001 Clause 8.4.3

description: Total number of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) or line items included in this RFQ. Indicates the complexity and scope of the sourcing event. Used for workload estimation and supplier capability assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM RFQ Line Items; CIPS Sourcing Complexity Assessment

description: System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the RFQ record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with downstream systems, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Change Document Log; ISO 27001 Audit Trail; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the supplier quotation response submitted in reply to an RFQ. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete procurement business event with a clear lifecycle.
reference: ISO 10845 Construction Procurement Standards; ERP SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing

description: Reference to the Purchase Order (PO) created as a result of awarding this RFQ response. Populated when response_status is 'awarded'. Enables end-to-end procure-to-pay traceability from sourcing event to order fulfillment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME21N Purchase Order; ISO 10845; SOX Procure-to-Pay Controls

description: Reference to the originating RFQ document to which this supplier response is submitted. Links the quotation response back to the sourcing event.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing — ME41/ME47 RFQ Object; ISO 10845

description: Reference to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which this quotation response is submitted. Links the procurement response to the product catalog for inventory planning and cost analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Material Master (MARA); PIM System SKU Catalog

description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) who submitted this quotation response. Enables multi-supplier price comparison and sourcing decision support. PARTY_REFERENCE category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.

Date and time when this quotation response was formally awarded, resulting in a purchase order being raised. Populated only when response_status transitions to 'awarded'. Supports procurement cycle time analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Award Date; ISO 10845; SOX Procurement Controls

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the quoted goods are manufactured or sourced. Required for customs compliance, import duty calculation, and trade regulation adherence.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; WTO Rules of Origin; CPSC Product Safety Standards; FTC Made-in-USA Standards

description: Timestamp when this RFQ response record was first captured in the procurement system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this quotation response (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and financial settlement. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Configuration

description: Code identifying the destination warehouse or fulfillment center to which the supplier will deliver the goods under this quotation. Used for logistics planning and last-mile delivery coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant/Storage Location; WMS Receiving Configuration

description: Absolute monetary discount offered by the supplier on the total quoted amount. Complements discount_percentage for cases where suppliers offer fixed-value discounts rather than percentage-based reductions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Discount Amount; ISO 10845

description: Percentage discount offered by the supplier on the quoted unit price. Represents the adjustment component of the monetary triplet. Used in total cost of ownership analysis and supplier negotiation.

Composite numerical score assigned to this quotation response during the multi-criteria sourcing evaluation (e.g., 0-100 scale). Incorporates price, lead time, quality, and compliance factors. Supports sourcing decision support and supplier performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Supplier Evaluation (ME61); ISO 10845; WTO Procurement Principles

description: Freight and shipping cost quoted by the supplier for delivery of the goods to the specified delivery location. Used in total landed cost calculation and logistics cost analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Condition Type Freight; TMS Carrier Rate Management; ISO 10845

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and supplier (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP, EXW). Governs logistics cost allocation and risk management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; WTO International Trade Regulations; SAP S/4HANA ME47 Incoterms

description: Named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., 'Port of Shanghai' for FOB Shanghai). Specifies the exact point of risk and cost transfer as required by ICC Incoterms 2020.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: Indicates whether the responding supplier holds preferred supplier status in the procurement system at the time of this RFQ response. Influences sourcing decisions and award prioritization.

Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) stipulated by the supplier as a condition of this quotation. Orders below this threshold may not be accepted or may incur additional charges. Critical for inventory planning and procurement cost optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record MOQ; ISO 10845

description: Net total amount after applying all discounts to the total quoted amount. Represents the final payable value if this supplier is awarded the purchase order. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET net total.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Net Value; ISO 10845; SOX Financial Controls

description: Supplier-offered payment terms code for this quotation (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Drives accounts payable scheduling and cash flow planning. Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Payment Terms (OBB8); ISO 10845; SOX Financial Controls

description: Human-readable description of the payment terms offered by the supplier (e.g., 'Net 30 days from invoice date', '2% discount if paid within 10 days, net 30'). Supplements the payment terms code for business user clarity.

Comma-separated list of quality or compliance certification codes applicable to the quoted product (e.g., ISO9001, CE, RoHS, FCC). Supports vendor compliance management and regulatory sourcing requirements.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; CPSC Product Safety; FTC Consumer Protection; ISO 27001

description: Number of calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the destination warehouse, as quoted by the supplier. Used for supply chain planning, inventory replenishment scheduling, and SLA compliance assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record Planned Delivery Time; ISO 10845

description: Quantity of the item for which the supplier has provided this quotation. May differ from the RFQ requested quantity if the supplier can only fulfill a partial quantity.

Supplier's quoted price per unit of the item as stated in the RFQ response. Core monetary field enabling multi-supplier price comparison and sourcing decisions. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Net Price; PCI DSS (financial data handling); ISO 10845

description: Reason code or description explaining why this quotation response was rejected during the sourcing evaluation. Populated when response_status is 'rejected'. Supports supplier feedback and sourcing process improvement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Rejection Reason; ISO 10845; FTC Procurement Standards

description: Free-text notes or comments provided by the supplier as part of the quotation response. May include special conditions, exclusions, assumptions, or clarifications relevant to the quoted price and terms.

Externally-known business identifier for this quotation response, typically assigned by the supplier or the procurement system (e.g., QUOT-2024-00123). Used for cross-referencing with supplier documents and ERP purchase order creation. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Quotation Number; ISO 10845

description: Current lifecycle state of the supplier quotation response within the sourcing workflow. Drives procurement decision-making and supplier communication. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|under_review|shortlisted|rejected|awarded|withdrawn — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Quotation Status; ISO 10845; FTC Procurement Standards

description: Indicates whether the supplier can provide product samples prior to purchase order placement. Supports quality assurance processes and product evaluation before committing to full procurement.
reference: CPSC Product Safety Standards; ISO 10845 Procurement

description: Number of days required by the supplier to deliver product samples if sample_available is true. Used for quality evaluation scheduling and procurement timeline planning.

Code identifying the operational system of record from which this RFQ response was sourced (e.g., SAP_S4HANA, ARIBA, COUPA). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system procurement integration in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 27001 Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA Integration Framework; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Date and time when the supplier formally submitted this quotation response to the procurement team. Represents the principal real-world business event time. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47; ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Supplier's own part number or item code for the quoted product. Used for cross-referencing internal SKUs with supplier catalog codes during purchase order creation and goods receipt matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record Supplier Material Number; EDI Standards

description: Total tax amount (VAT, GST, or applicable sales tax) included in or applicable to this quotation. Required for accurate total landed cost calculation and accounts payable processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition; SOX Financial Controls; GDPR (financial data)

description: Indicates whether the supplier offers volume-based tiered pricing for this quotation. When true, additional pricing tiers are captured in the associated RFQ response pricing tier detail records.

Total quoted value for the full quantity requested in the RFQ, calculated as quoted unit price multiplied by quoted quantity. Represents the gross monetary commitment if this supplier is awarded. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Total Value; ISO 10845; SOX Financial Controls

description: Unit of measure applicable to the quoted quantity and unit price (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, CS for case, PK for pack). Aligns with the UOM defined in the RFQ.
reference: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; SAP S/4HANA UOM Configuration

description: Timestamp when this RFQ response record was last modified in the procurement system. Supports change tracking and audit compliance. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: ISO 27001 Information Security Management; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Date after which the supplier's quoted prices and terms expire and are no longer binding. Procurement must raise a purchase order before this date to secure the quoted price. Critical for sourcing decision timelines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Validity Period End; ISO 10845

description: Date from which the supplier's quoted prices and terms are valid. Defines the start of the binding quotation window during which a purchase order can be raised at the quoted price.
reference: SAP S/4HANA ME47 Validity Period Start; ISO 10845

description: Duration in months of the product warranty offered by the supplier as part of this quotation. Relevant for total cost of ownership analysis, returns management, and vendor contract negotiations.
reference: CPSC Product Safety Standards; FTC Warranty Disclosure Rules; ISO 10845

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier price list record. Primary key for this entity in the procurement domain Silver layer.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179 - Data Element Naming and Definition

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price‑list maintenance needs cost‑center attribution for budgeting of negotiated pricing programs.
Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Ensures price‑list changes are reflected in the GL for expense forecasting and audit compliance.
Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Enables segment‑specific pricing; the pricing engine selects a price list based on the customer.segment of the buyer.

Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Pricing: sellers receive negotiated price lists from suppliers; linking allows price‑list selection and margin calculations per seller.
Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this price list entry applies. Each price list record covers exactly one SKU.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master (MM60)

description: Reference to the newer supplier_price_list record that replaced this one when status is 'superseded'. Enables version chain traversal for price history analysis and audit. Null if this is the current active version.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Versioning; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Reference to the supplier (vendor) who owns this price list. Links to the supplier master record in the procurement domain.
Reference to the vendor contract or purchasing outline agreement under which this price list is governed. Null if the price list is a standalone purchasing info record not tied to a formal contract.
The date on which this price list was formally approved by the authorized procurement officer. Required for SOX compliance audit trails and procurement governance reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls; ISO 9001 Document Approval; SAP S/4HANA MM - Release Strategy Date

description: The name or employee identifier of the procurement officer or manager who approved this price list for use in purchase order processing. Supports SOX procurement controls and audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting; SAP S/4HANA MM - Release Strategy; ISO 9001 Procurement Approval

business_glossary_term: Automatic Purchase Order (PO) Pricing Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this price list is enabled for automatic price determination during purchase order creation in the ERP system. When True, the system automatically applies this price list during PO generation without manual price entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Automatic Price Determination (OMFI); Procurement Automation Best Practices

description: The timestamp when this price list record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Audit Trail Requirements; ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price and all pricing tiers are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX conversion in cost-of-goods calculations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Currency (EINE-WAERS)

description: The agreed percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the net unit price. Represents the base-level trade discount negotiated with the supplier, separate from volume-tier discounts. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Condition (KONP-KBETR); CIPS Procurement Standards

description: The date from which this price list becomes valid and can be used for automated purchase order price determination and cost-of-goods calculations. Aligns with the validity start date in the ERP purchasing info record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Validity Period (EKPO-MEPRF); ISO 8601 Date Standard

business_glossary_term: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Purchasing Info Record Number
description: The SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record (PIR) number corresponding to this price list entry. Enables direct traceability between the Silver layer data product and the ERP system of record for procurement price determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (ME11/ME12/ME13); ERP Integration Standards

description: The date after which this price list is no longer valid for purchase order pricing. Null indicates an open-ended price list with no defined expiration. Used to trigger price list renewal workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Validity Period (EKPO-MEPRF); ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: Indicates whether freight/shipping costs are included in the quoted unit price. When True, no separate freight charge is added during PO creation. When False, freight is billed separately or calculated via the TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Freight Condition (FRA1); ICC Incoterms 2020; APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: Indicates whether this price list includes a volume discount schedule (quantity break tiers) in addition to the base unit price. When True, the associated supplier_price_list_tier records define the tiered pricing structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scale Pricing (Condition Scales); CIPS Volume Discount Standards

description: The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between the supplier and the e-commerce platform for shipments under this price list (e.g., FOB, DDP, EXW). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Incoterms (EKKO-INCO1); WTO International Trade Regulations

description: The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., 'Port of Shanghai' for FOB, 'Buyer Warehouse' for DDP). Required to fully specify the delivery terms per ICC Incoterms 2020 rules.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Incoterms Location (EKKO-INCO2)

description: The agreed supplier lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the warehouse. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and SLA monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Planned Delivery Time (EINE-WEBAZ); APICS Supply Chain Standards

description: The minimum quantity of the SKU that must be ordered per purchase order line to qualify for the agreed unit price. Orders below the MOQ may be rejected by the supplier or subject to a surcharge. Key procurement constraint for replenishment planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Minimum Order Quantity (EINE-MINBM); CIPS Procurement Standards

description: Free-text field for procurement team annotations, special conditions, or supplier-communicated caveats associated with this price list (e.g., 'Price valid only for orders placed before end of quarter', 'Subject to raw material surcharge above $X/tonne').
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Text; ISO 9001 Document Management

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country of origin for the SKU under this price list. Used for import duty calculation, customs compliance, trade restriction checks, and landed cost estimation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; WTO Rules of Origin; US CBP Import Regulations; EU Customs Code

description: The agreed payment terms code applicable to purchases under this price list (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Aligns with the payment terms configured in the ERP vendor master and accounts payable module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP - Payment Terms (T052); SOX Section 404 - Procurement Controls

description: Indicates whether the unit price represents the gross list price (before discounts), the net agreed price (after discounts), or the landed cost (inclusive of freight, duties, and insurance). Critical for correct COGS calculation and PO price validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Type (KONP); CIPS Cost Analysis Standards; APICS Landed Cost

description: Business category of the price list indicating its commercial purpose. 'standard' = regular agreed pricing; 'promotional' = time-limited promotional pricing; 'seasonal' = seasonal pricing adjustment; 'clearance' = end-of-life or excess inventory pricing; 'emergency' = expedited sourcing price.
reference: CIPS Procurement Category Management; SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Category

description: Human-readable name or description of the price list, such as 'Q1 2025 Electronics Pricing' or 'Annual Volume Agreement - Supplier XYZ'. Used for display and search in procurement portals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record; Procurement Best Practices

description: Externally-known business identifier for this price list, used in supplier communications, purchase order references, and ERP purchasing info records. Follows the format PL-{alphanumeric code}.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record (ME11/ME12); ISO/IEC 11179

description: Classification of the price list by its commercial basis. 'contract_based' = tied to a formal vendor contract; 'standalone_pir' = standalone Purchasing Info Record (PIR) in ERP; 'spot_quote' = one-time quoted price; 'catalog' = supplier-published catalog price; 'blanket_order' = price under a blanket purchase order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Category; CIPS Procurement Standards

description: Version number of this price list, enabling tracking of successive revisions for the same supplier-SKU combination. Incremented each time the supplier submits a revised pricing schedule.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record versioning; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: The maximum allowable percentage deviation between the price list unit price and the actual invoice price before a three-way match exception is raised. Used in automated invoice verification to flag price discrepancies requiring manual review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP - Invoice Verification Tolerance (OMR6); SOX Section 404 - AP Controls; CIPS Invoice Matching Standards

description: The quantity basis for which the unit price is quoted (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units). Used to correctly compute per-unit cost when the supplier quotes prices in bulk multiples. Corresponds to the SAP Purchasing Info Record 'Price Unit' field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Price Unit (EINE-PEINH); GS1 Unit of Measure Standards

description: The number of days before the expiry date at which a renewal reminder notification should be triggered to the procurement team. Supports proactive price list renewal management and prevents procurement disruption due to expired pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Reminder; CIPS Contract Management Best Practices

description: The reference number of the source document from which this price list was derived, such as a supplier quotation number, RFQ response number, or EDI catalog transmission ID. Provides traceability back to the originating supplier communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Request for Quotation (ME41); EDI X12 843 Price/Sales Catalog; ISO 9001 Document Traceability

description: Current lifecycle state of the price list. 'active' = currently valid for PO pricing; 'expired' = past expiry date; 'superseded' = replaced by a newer version; 'draft' = under preparation; 'pending_approval' = awaiting procurement approval; 'suspended' = temporarily deactivated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Status; ISO 9001 Procurement Controls

description: The supplier's own part number or item code for this SKU, as used in the supplier's catalog and order acknowledgements. Required for EDI purchase order transmission and supplier invoice matching. Distinct from the internal SKU identifier.
reference: GS1 GTIN Standards; EDI X12 850 Purchase Order Standard; SAP S/4HANA MM - Info Record Supplier Material Number (EINE-IDNLF)

description: The supplier's description of the SKU as it appears in their catalog or price list document. Used for three-way matching during invoice verification and for resolving discrepancies between internal and supplier item descriptions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Info Record Material Description (EINE-TXZ01); EDI X12 850 Standard

description: The tax classification code assigned to this SKU-supplier combination for VAT/GST determination during purchase order processing. Maps to the tax condition type in the ERP pricing procedure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Tax Indicator (EINE-MWSKZ); EU VAT Directive; GDPR (not applicable); FTC Consumer Protection

description: The number of quantity break tiers defined in the volume discount schedule for this price list. Zero if has_volume_tiers is False. Provides a quick summary of pricing complexity without joining to tier detail records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Scale Count; CIPS Volume Pricing Standards

description: The agreed supplier unit price for one unit of the SKU at the base quantity tier. This is the SSOT for supplier-agreed cost used in automated PO price validation and cost-of-goods (COGS) calculations. Does not represent the selling price or invoice amount.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Purchasing Info Record Net Price (EINE-NETPR); PCI DSS (not applicable - non-payment data); CIPS Procurement Standards

description: The unit of measure in which the SKU is priced and ordered (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, L for litre). Aligns with the UOM defined in the product master and used in purchase order line items.
reference: GS1 Unit of Measure Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Master UOM (MARA-MEINS); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: The timestamp when this price list record was last modified. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Audit Trail Requirements; ISO/IEC 27001 - Information Security; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the vendor contract record in the procurement data product. Primary key for the vendor_contract entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Outline Agreement (ME31K/ME32K)

description: Reference to the employee who provided final authorization and approval for this contract or its most recent amendment. Supports SOX audit trail requirements for procurement authorization controls.
reference: SOX Section 302/404 — Authorization Controls; SAP S/4HANA Workflow Approval

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for B2B contract management reports linking each supplier contract to the buying customer.account.

Reference to the internal procurement employee or category manager who owns and is accountable for this vendor contract. Used for contract governance, renewal notifications, and performance review assignments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR — Employee Master; CIPS Contract Ownership Framework

description: Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Contract management: contracts are negotiated between a specific seller and a supplier; linking enables contract compliance and SLA reporting per seller.
Reference to the supplier/vendor party who is the counterparty to this contract. Links to the vendor master record in the procurement domain.

Total number of formal amendments (change orders) that have been executed against this contract since its original execution. Provides a quick indicator of contract stability and negotiation history. Incremented each time a change order is approved.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Change History; CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management

description: Indicates whether the contract is configured to automatically renew upon reaching its expiry date without requiring a new negotiation cycle. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action. Critical for contract lifecycle management and budget planning.
reference: CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management; SAP S/4HANA Scheduling Agreement Renewal

description: Data classification level assigned to this contract document indicating the sensitivity of its commercial terms and conditions. Restricted: highly sensitive strategic terms. Confidential: standard commercial terms not for external disclosure. Internal: accessible to all internal staff. Public: terms disclosed publicly (rare for vendor contracts).
reference: ISO 27001 — Information Classification; GDPR Article 5 — Data Minimisation; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the vendor contract as assigned by the procurement or ERP system (e.g., SAP scheduling agreement or outline agreement number). Used in all vendor communications, purchase orders, and audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Outline Agreement Number (EKKO.EBELN); ISO 9001 Procurement Documentation

description: Current lifecycle state of the vendor contract governing its operational validity. Draft: under internal review and not yet binding. Active: fully executed and in force. Amended: modified via change order but still active. Suspended: temporarily halted pending resolution. Expired: reached end date without renewal. Terminated: ended before expiry by either party.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Release/Status Indicator; CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management

description: Descriptive human-readable title or short name of the vendor contract (e.g., 'Annual Packaging Materials Supply Agreement FY2025'). Used for display in procurement dashboards and contract registers.
reference: ISO 9001 Procurement Documentation; SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KDATB

description: Classification of the procurement contract by commercial structure. Framework agreements establish terms for future orders; blanket purchase orders cover recurring purchases up to a ceiling value; spot contracts are one-time buys; consignment contracts allow inventory held at buyer premises billed on consumption; scheduled agreements define delivery schedules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: framework|blanket|spot|consignment|scheduled — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Agreement Type (EKKO.BSART); CIPS Procurement Classification

description: The timestamp when this vendor contract record was first created in the procurement system (SAP S/4HANA or data lakehouse). Represents the system-of-record creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.AEDAT; SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail; ISO 8601

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the contract value, pricing, and payment obligations are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX exposure reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA EKKO.WAERS; PCI DSS — Financial Transaction Currency

description: Code identifying the designated delivery destination (warehouse, fulfillment center, or plant) where the vendor must deliver goods under this contract. Maps to the WMS facility or SAP S/4HANA plant/storage location.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Plant (EKKO.WERKS); WMS Facility Master

description: The contractually agreed mechanism for resolving commercial disputes between the buyer and vendor (e.g., binding arbitration, mediation, or court litigation). Determines the legal process to follow in case of contract breach or disagreement.
reference: ICC Arbitration Rules; WTO Dispute Settlement; UCC Article 2

description: The date on which the vendor contract becomes legally binding and commercially effective. Marks the start of the contract period for SLA enforcement, payment terms, and procurement eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KDATB (Agreement Start Date); UCC Article 2 — Contract Formation

description: Indicates whether this contract grants the vendor exclusive supply rights for the specified goods or services category, preventing the buyer from sourcing from competing vendors during the contract term. True = exclusive; False = non-exclusive.
reference: FTC — Exclusive Dealing Arrangements; CIPS Supplier Relationship Management

description: Free-text description of the scope of exclusivity when the exclusivity flag is True. Defines the product categories, geographic regions, or channels covered by the exclusive arrangement (e.g., 'Exclusive supplier of private-label packaging materials for APAC fulfillment centers').
reference: FTC — Exclusive Dealing Arrangements; EU Electronic Commerce Directive

description: The date on which the vendor contract is scheduled to expire and cease to be binding, unless renewed or extended. Nullable for open-ended evergreen contracts. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance alerts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KDATV (Agreement End Date); CIPS Contract Lifecycle Management

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processing Agreement (DPA) Flag
description: Indicates whether a GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) has been executed with this vendor, required when the vendor processes personal data on behalf of the company. True = DPA in place; False = DPA not required or not yet executed. Mandatory for GDPR Article 28 compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 28 — Processor Obligations; CCPA Section 1798.100; ISO 27001

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this contract (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Critical for cross-border procurement and dispute resolution.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO International Trade Regulations; GDPR Article 3 — Territorial Scope

description: International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the allocation of delivery costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and vendor. Governs logistics cost accounting and insurance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA EKKO.INCO1; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: The date on which the most recent formal amendment or change order to this contract was executed and became effective. Null for contracts that have never been amended. Used to track contract evolution and trigger re-approval workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Amendment History; SOX Section 404

description: Free-text description of the business reason for the most recent contract amendment (e.g., 'Price adjustment due to raw material cost increase Q3 2025', 'Extended term by 12 months following supplier performance review'). Supports audit trail and change management documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Reason; SOX Section 302 — Change Documentation; CIPS Contract Management

description: The contractually agreed number of calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the designated receiving location. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and WMS receiving scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Planned Delivery Time (EINE.PLIFZ); APICS Supply Chain Dictionary

description: The minimum quantity per purchase order that the vendor requires under this contract, as negotiated in the commercial terms. MOQ is a standard e-commerce and supply chain procurement metric used for inventory planning and replenishment decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Minimum Order Quantity (EINE.MINBM); CIPS Procurement Terms

business_glossary_term: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure applicable to the minimum order quantity (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, PAL for pallet). Follows ISO 80000 / UN/CEFACT unit codes as used in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: UN/CEFACT Recommendation 20 — Units of Measure; SAP S/4HANA MEINS

description: Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is in place with this vendor as part of or alongside this contract. True = NDA executed; False = no NDA. Relevant for IP protection, proprietary pricing, and technology sharing arrangements.
reference: ISO 27001 — Confidentiality Agreements; GDPR Article 28 — Data Processing Agreements; NIST

description: The agreed method by which payments to the vendor will be made under this contract. Bank transfer (ACH/wire) is most common for B2B procurement; letter of credit is used for international suppliers; open account for trusted long-term vendors.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Payment Method (LFBK.ZWELS); PCI DSS — Payment Processing

description: Coded payment terms agreed with the vendor governing invoice due dates and early payment discounts (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment terms key (ZTERM) used in accounts payable processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP — Payment Terms (T052.ZTERM); UCC Article 2 — Payment Terms

description: The maximum cumulative financial penalty amount (in contract currency) that can be levied against the vendor under this contract over its full term. Caps total liability exposure for both parties. Null when no penalty cap is defined.
reference: CIPS Contract Management; UCC Article 2 — Limitation of Liability; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP

description: Indicates whether this contract contains financial penalty clauses for SLA breaches, late delivery, or quality failures. True = penalty clauses exist; False = no financial penalties defined. Drives penalty calculation workflows in accounts payable.
reference: CIPS Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Penalty Processing; UCC Article 2

description: The contractually defined financial penalty rate expressed as a percentage of the affected order value or invoice amount, applied when the vendor breaches SLA commitments. Null when penalty_clause_flag is False.
reference: CIPS Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; UCC Article 2 — Liquidated Damages

description: Standardized spend category code classifying the goods or services covered by this contract (e.g., UNSPSC or internal category taxonomy code). Enables spend analytics, category management, and strategic sourcing decisions.
reference: UNSPSC — United Nations Standard Products and Services Code; SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Group (EKKO.MATKL); CIPS Category Management

description: Number of calendar days before the contract expiry date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Used to trigger automated procurement alerts and prevent unintended auto-renewals.
reference: CIPS Contract Management Best Practice; SAP S/4HANA Contract Reminder Configuration

description: The date on which the contract was formally executed (signed) by all authorized parties. May differ from the effective date when contracts are backdated or post-dated. Critical for legal enforceability and audit.
reference: UCC Article 2; SOX Section 302 — Contract Execution Records

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Maximum Defect Rate Percentage
description: Contractually agreed maximum acceptable defect/rejection rate (as a percentage) for goods delivered under this contract. Exceeding this threshold constitutes an SLA breach and may trigger return merchandise authorization (RMA) processes and financial penalties.
reference: CIPS SLA Framework; CPSC Product Safety Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Order Fill Rate Target Percentage
description: Contractually committed minimum order fill rate (as a percentage) representing the proportion of ordered quantity that the vendor must fulfill completely per shipment. A fill rate below this threshold triggers SLA breach notifications and potential penalties.
reference: CIPS SLA Framework; APICS Supply Chain Dictionary — Fill Rate; ISO 9001

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) On-Time Delivery Target Percentage
description: Contractually committed minimum on-time delivery rate (as a percentage) that the vendor must achieve. Measured as the percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the agreed delivery date. Used for vendor performance scorecards and penalty clause triggers.
reference: CIPS SLA Framework; SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Evaluation (ME61); ISO 9001 Supplier Performance

description: The actual date on which the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that expired naturally or are still active. Distinct from expiry_date which is the scheduled end; this captures unplanned or negotiated early termination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Termination; UCC Article 2 — Contract Termination; CIPS

description: Coded reason for early contract termination. Mutual agreement: both parties agreed to end early. Vendor breach: vendor failed to meet contractual obligations. Buyer breach: buyer defaulted. Force majeure: unforeseeable external event. Regulatory: compliance or legal requirement. Convenience: buyer exercised termination for convenience clause.
reference: UCC Article 2 — Contract Termination; CIPS Contract Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The total maximum committed monetary value of the vendor contract over its full term, expressed in the contract currency. For blanket/framework agreements this is the ceiling value; for spot contracts it is the fixed order value. Used for spend analytics, budget commitment tracking, and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.KTWRT (Target Value); SOX Section 404 — Procurement Spend Controls; CIPS Spend Analysis

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this vendor contract record in the procurement system. Updated on every change including amendments, status transitions, and field updates. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EKKO.AEDAT; SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail; ISO 8601; ETL Change Data Capture

description: System-generated unique identifier for each record in the approved vendor list.
FK to procurement.supplier
Unique identifier of the vendor (supplier) referenced in the approved list.
Self-referencing FK on approved_vendor_list (superseded_approved_vendor_list_id)

True if the vendor adheres to GDPR data‑protection obligations.
True if the vendor qualifies under the company's diversity supplier program.

Average number of days the vendor takes to deliver after order receipt.

Numerical score reflecting how well the vendor meets qualification criteria.
Risk tier derived from the risk score.
Aggregated risk rating assigned to the vendor based on financial, compliance, and performance factors.
Name of the upstream system that supplied the vendor data (e.g., SAP, Oracle).
Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.
Broad product or service category the vendor supplies (e.g., electronics, apparel).
Classification of the vendor based on its business role.
Primary key for evaluation_period
Self-referencing FK on evaluation_period (prior_evaluation_period_id)
Timestamp when the evaluation period record was first created in the system.
Detailed description of the purpose and scope of the evaluation period.
Last calendar date on which the evaluation period is active; nullable for open‑ended periods.
Classification of the evaluation period based on its recurrence pattern.
How often the evaluation period repeats or is scheduled.
