description: Primary email address for household-level communications and notifications.
Primary phone number for household-level communications and customer service interactions.
Percentage of orders returned by household members, calculated as (returned orders / total orders) * 100.
reference: Order Management System (OMS) - Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) Tracking

description: Risk assessment score for the household based on payment history, fraud indicators, and return patterns. Range: 0.00 to 100.00.
Marketing or analytical segment classification assigned to the household based on behavioral, demographic, and spending patterns.
Indicates whether household members can pool and share loyalty points across the household unit.
reference: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) - Loyalty Program Framework

description: Name of the operational system that originated this household record (e.g., Customer Data Platform, CRM, Order Management System).
IANA timezone identifier for the household's primary location, used for scheduling and time-sensitive communications.
Cumulative gross merchandise value of all orders placed by household members across their lifetime. Measured in USD.
Total number of customer profiles ever associated with this household, including inactive members.
reference: Customer Data Platform - Household Composition Tracking

description: Total number of orders placed by all household members across their lifetime.
reference: Order Management System (OMS) - Order Tracking Standard

description: Timestamp when the household record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Unique identifier for the CLTV scoring record. Primary key for this operational scoring entity that drives real-time personalization and retention workflows.
Reference to the customer for whom this CLTV score is calculated. Links to the unified customer profile in the Customer Data Platform.
Average monetary value per order placed by this customer. Key input to CLTV calculation and used to identify high-ticket customers for upsell opportunities.
Predicted likelihood that this customer will stop purchasing within the next 90 days. Expressed as a probability between 0 and 1. Scores above 0.7 trigger retention campaigns.

Categorical segmentation of customer value. High-value customers receive premium service and retention offers; at-risk customers trigger win-back campaigns; dormant customers enter re-engagement workflows.
Statistical confidence level of the CLTV prediction, expressed as a probability between 0 and 1. Higher values indicate more reliable predictions based on sufficient historical data.
Timestamp when this CLTV score record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this record. Ensures correct interpretation of CLTV amounts in multi-currency environments.
Business segment classification based on CLTV tier, RFM scores, and behavioral patterns. Drives differentiated marketing strategies and service levels.
Number of days elapsed since the customer placed their first order. Represents customer tenure and is used to calculate customer lifetime metrics.
Number of days elapsed since the customer placed their most recent order. Critical input to recency scoring and churn prediction models.
RFM frequency component: score from 1 to 5 representing how often the customer purchases. 5 = most frequent, 1 = least frequent. Part of the RFM composite scoring model.
Actual cumulative revenue generated by this customer to date. Represents realized value from all completed orders and transactions.
Comma-separated list of feature names used by the CLTV model for this customer. Enables model explainability and debugging of score anomalies.
Version identifier of the CLTV prediction model used to calculate this score. Enables model performance tracking and A/B testing of scoring algorithms.
RFM monetary component: score from 1 to 5 representing how much the customer spends. 5 = highest spender, 1 = lowest spender. Part of the RFM composite scoring model.
Scheduled date for the next CLTV score refresh. Ensures scores remain current and reflect recent customer behavior. Typically set to 30, 60, or 90 days from calculation date.
Forecasted revenue expected from this customer over the next 12 months. Used for short-term marketing budget allocation and campaign targeting.
Forecasted revenue expected from this customer over the next 36 months. Used for long-term strategic planning and customer segment investment decisions.
Product category with the highest purchase frequency or spend for this customer. Used for personalized product recommendations and targeted marketing campaigns.
Normalized score representing how often this customer makes purchases compared to the customer base average. Higher scores indicate more frequent buyers.
RFM recency component: score from 1 to 5 representing how recently the customer made a purchase. 5 = most recent, 1 = least recent. Part of the RFM composite scoring model.
Three-digit RFM score combining recency, frequency, and monetary scores. Example: 555 represents the best customers (recent, frequent, high-value). Used for customer segmentation and targeting.
Date when this CLTV score was calculated. Enables tracking of score changes over time and ensures users understand the freshness of the prediction.
Current lifecycle status of this CLTV score record. Active scores are used in real-time decisioning; expired scores trigger recalculation workflows.
Total number of orders placed by this customer to date. Used as a key input to frequency scoring and customer lifecycle stage determination.
Timestamp when this CLTV score record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record for change data capture and audit purposes.
Unique identifier for each consent event record. Primary key for the consent event log.
Identifier of the customer who performed the consent action. Links to the customer master record.
Reference to a previous consent event that this event modifies or supersedes. Enables tracking of consent change chains and audit trail reconstruction.
Channel or interface through which the consent event was captured. Identifies the touchpoint where the customer interacted with consent management.
Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the language in which the consent was presented and captured. Ensures consent was obtained in the customer's preferred language.
Category of consent or preference being managed. Defines the specific processing purpose or communication channel covered by this consent event.
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(a), GDPR Article 7, CCPA Section 1798.100(b)

description: Geographic or operational scope to which this consent applies. Defines the boundaries of consent applicability across different jurisdictions or business units.
The exact consent language or terms that were displayed to the customer at the time of consent. Provides evidence of what the customer agreed to for regulatory audits.
Version identifier of the consent policy or terms and conditions that were presented to the customer at the time of this event. Enables tracking of policy changes over time.
System timestamp when this consent event record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Email address of the customer at the time of the consent event. Captured for verification and communication purposes in consent lifecycle management.
Number of days that data will be retained under this consent. Used to enforce data minimization principles and automated data deletion policies.
Unique identifier of the device (mobile, tablet, desktop) from which the consent event was initiated. Used for cross-device consent tracking and fraud prevention.
Precise date and time when the consent event occurred, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). This is the authoritative timestamp for regulatory audit trails.
Type of consent event that occurred. Categorizes the nature of the customer's action for regulatory reporting and lifecycle tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 7, GDPR Article 15, GDPR Article 17, GDPR Article 20, CCPA Section 1798.105

description: Date when this consent is set to expire and require renewal. Used for time-bound consent management and automated re-consent workflows.
Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic location from which the consent event originated. Used for jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3, GDPR Article 3, CCPA Section 1798.140

description: State, province, or region from which the consent event originated. Provides additional geographic context for jurisdiction-specific privacy laws (e.g., CCPA for California).
Internet Protocol address of the device from which the consent event was initiated. Used for fraud detection and regulatory evidence of consent origin.
Indicates whether the customer was identified as a minor (under age of consent) at the time of this event. Critical for GDPR Article 8 and COPPA compliance.
The lawful basis under which personal data processing is justified for this consent event. Required for GDPR Article 6 compliance and regulatory reporting.
The consent status after this event was applied. Represents the current state resulting from the customer's action.
Free-text notes or comments associated with this consent event. May include customer service representative notes for call center-originated events or system-generated messages.
Indicates whether the customer has exercised their right to opt out of the sale of personal information under CCPA. Must be honored across all business processes.
Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was required for this event due to the customer being a minor. Used for COPPA and GDPR Article 8 compliance tracking.
Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was successfully verified for this event. Critical for processing consent events for minors under COPPA and GDPR.
The consent status before this event occurred. Captures the previous state to enable audit trail reconstruction and change tracking.
Date and time when the consent event was fully processed and applied across all relevant systems. Used to measure compliance with GDPR's one-month response requirement.
Detailed description of the specific purpose for which personal data will be processed under this consent. Must be explicit and granular per GDPR requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 5(1)(b), GDPR Article 13(1)(c), CCPA Section 1798.100(b)

description: Current processing status of the consent event. Tracks whether the event has been successfully applied to downstream systems and customer profiles.
Unique identifier for the user session during which the consent event occurred. Enables correlation with other user actions in the same session for behavioral analysis.
Name or identifier of the system that originated this consent event record. Identifies the operational system of record for data lineage and troubleshooting.
Indicates whether the customer has consented to sharing their personal data with third parties. Critical for CCPA 'Do Not Sell' compliance and GDPR Article 6 lawful basis.
Browser or application user agent string at the time of consent. Provides technical context about the device and software used for the consent action.
Method used to verify the identity of the customer before processing the consent event. Critical for data deletion and portability requests to prevent fraud.
Status of identity verification for this consent event. Indicates whether the customer's identity was successfully confirmed before processing the request.
Unique identifier for the NPS survey response record.
Reference to the customer who submitted this NPS response.
reference: Customer Data Platform unified profile identifier

description: Reference to the customer service agent or account manager who handled the follow-up action for this response.
Identifier of the marketing campaign or survey program that this response belongs to. Used for cohort analysis and A/B testing.
reference: Marketing Automation Platform - Salesforce Marketing Cloud campaign tracking

description: Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Links NPS feedback to the purchased product, enabling product‑level sentiment analysis in the NPS reporting dashboard.
Indicates whether this response has been anonymized (customer identifier removed) for privacy compliance or public reporting.
Customer classification based on NPS score: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), detractor (0-6).
reference: NPS methodology - Reichheld Net Promoter System

description: Indicates whether the customer provided explicit consent for their feedback to be used for marketing, testimonials, or case studies.
reference: GDPR Article 7 - conditions for consent, CCPA Section 1798.120

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the customer's location at time of response.
Timestamp when this NPS response record was first created in the system.
Customer segment classification at the time of survey (e.g., VIP, regular, new, at-risk). Used for segment-level NPS analysis.
Number of days the customer has been active with the company at the time of survey response. Used to correlate NPS with customer lifecycle stage.
reference: Customer Data Platform customer lifecycle analytics

description: Type of device used by the customer to complete the survey: desktop, mobile, tablet, or unknown.
Action taken in response to customer feedback: none (no action needed), contacted (customer reached out to), resolved (issue addressed), escalated (sent to management), pending (awaiting action).
Date when follow-up action was completed or customer was contacted regarding their feedback.
Internal notes documenting the follow-up action taken, customer conversation summary, and resolution details. Business-confidential operational data.

Customer-assigned priority ranking for this wishlist relative to other wishlists owned by the same customer. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for personalization and recommendation engines.

Current lifecycle state of the wishlist. Active: in use; Archived: preserved but inactive; Expired: past event date for registries; Deleted: soft-deleted pending purge.

Reason for cancellation of the loyalty enrollment, if status is cancelled. Examples include customer request, fraud, inactivity, or program termination.
Timestamp when this loyalty enrollment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Current available points balance that the customer can redeem. Represents the net of all earned, redeemed, expired, and adjusted points. This is the real-time spendable loyalty currency balance.

description: Current tier or level of the customer within the loyalty program hierarchy. Determines benefits, earning rates, and redemption privileges. Common tiers include Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, and VIP.
Channel through which the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Used for channel attribution and enrollment effectiveness analysis.
Date when the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the beginning of the customer's loyalty relationship.
Human-readable unique enrollment number or membership number assigned to the customer for this loyalty program. Used for customer service and member identification.
Marketing campaign or promotion that drove the customer to enroll in the loyalty program. Used for campaign attribution and enrollment ROI analysis.

Current lifecycle status of the loyalty enrollment. Active indicates the member can earn and redeem points; suspended indicates temporary hold; expired indicates membership lapsed; cancelled indicates voluntary or involuntary termination; pending indicates enrollment awaiting activation.
Date of the most recent loyalty activity (earn, redeem, or adjustment). Used for inactivity detection and dormancy rules.
Date when the customer last earned loyalty points. Used for engagement scoring, churn prediction, and reactivation campaigns.
Date when the customer last redeemed loyalty points. Used for engagement analysis and redemption behavior modeling.
Cumulative total of all points earned by the customer since enrollment. Used for tier qualification, lifetime value analysis, and member recognition. This is a monotonically increasing counter.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes (loyalty points as virtual currency)

description: Cumulative total of all points that have expired due to inactivity or program rules since enrollment. Used for breakage analysis and financial reconciliation.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes (loyalty points as virtual currency)

description: Cumulative total of all points redeemed by the customer since enrollment. Used for engagement analysis and redemption behavior modeling.
reference: ISO 4217 - Currency Codes (loyalty points as virtual currency)

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the membership fee. Used for multi-currency loyalty programs.
Total membership fee paid by the customer for enrollment or renewal in the loyalty program. Zero for free programs.
Remaining points or spend needed to reach the next tier. Calculated as next_tier_threshold minus current qualifying activity. Used for personalized tier progression campaigns.
Points or spend threshold required to qualify for the next loyalty tier. Used for gamification and tier progression messaging. Null if customer is already at the highest tier.
Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive loyalty program communications via email.
Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive marketing communications related to the loyalty program. Supports GDPR and CCPA consent management.
Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive loyalty program push notifications via mobile app.
Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive loyalty program communications via SMS text messages.
Date when the next batch of points is scheduled to expire. Used for customer communication and expiry alerts. Null if no points are scheduled to expire or program has no expiration policy.
Date when a previously suspended or expired enrollment was reactivated. Used for win-back campaign analysis.
Referral code used by the customer during enrollment, if applicable. Links to the referring customer for referral bonus attribution.
Date when the loyalty enrollment was suspended. Null if enrollment has never been suspended.
Reason for suspension of the loyalty enrollment, if status is suspended. Examples include fraud investigation, terms violation, payment failure, or customer request.
Date when the current tier status expires and the customer must re-qualify or be downgraded. Null for lifetime tiers or programs without tier expiration.
Date when the customer qualified for their current loyalty tier. Used to track tier tenure and calculate tier anniversary dates.
End date of the current tier qualification period. After this date, tier status is re-evaluated based on qualifying activity.
Start date of the current tier qualification period. Used to calculate rolling qualification windows and reset tier progress.
Total spend amount that counts toward tier qualification in the current qualification period. Used when tier status is based on spend rather than points earned.
Timestamp when this loyalty enrollment record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the loyalty program's monetary transactions and conversions.
Current number of customers actively enrolled in this loyalty program.
Multiplier applied to base earning rate for this program (e.g., 2.0 means customers earn points at twice the standard rate).
The date when the loyalty program terminates. Nullable for open-ended programs.
Business rules defining which customer segments or criteria qualify for enrollment in this loyalty program (e.g., geographic restrictions, customer type requirements, minimum purchase thresholds).
Username or identifier of the user who last modified this loyalty program record.
Timestamp when this loyalty program record was last updated.
Code linking this loyalty program to a specific marketing campaign for tracking Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) and Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC).
Maximum number of customers allowed to enroll in this loyalty program. Null indicates no enrollment cap.

URL link to the privacy policy explaining how customer data is collected, used, and protected within the loyalty program.

The marketing name of the loyalty program displayed to customers.

Current lifecycle status of the loyalty program: active (accepting enrollments and accruals), inactive (not accepting new enrollments), suspended (temporarily paused), draft (under development), or archived (historical record).

Classification of the loyalty program structure: points-based (earn and redeem points), tier-based (status levels with benefits), cashback (percentage return), subscription (paid membership), hybrid (combination), or coalition (multi-brand partnership).
Minimum number of points required before a customer can redeem rewards. Applicable for points-based programs.

Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Exact carrier service used per transaction is needed for service‑level compliance and buyer ETA guarantees.
Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Supports sales analytics that aggregate revenue by catalog item independent of SKU variations.

Foreign key linking to marketplace.commission_schedule. Business justification: Marketplace transactions need to reference the commission schedule that defines their commission rates; adds commission_schedule_id FK to marketplace_transaction.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for internal cost allocation reports that assign transaction processing costs to specific cost centers.
Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Required for order fulfillment shipping address, used in delivery performance reports and compliance with shipping regulations.
Identifier of the customer who purchased the item through the marketplace platform.
Identifier of the fulfillment center responsible for shipping the item, if applicable for marketplace-fulfilled transactions.
Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Required for the daily accounting posting process that creates a journal entry for each marketplace transaction.
Reference to the specific seller offer accepted by the buyer for this transaction.
Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Regulated/lot‑tracked products require linking transaction to lot for traceability, recall, and compliance reporting.
Reference to the marketplace catalog listing that was purchased in this transaction.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Transaction audit requires linking each transaction to the compliance obligation it satisfies (anti‑fraud, consumer rights), supporting the Transaction Compliance Dashboard.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: REQUIRED: Capturing the stored payment method used for each transaction supports PCI compliance, analytics, and dispute handling.
External transaction identifier from the payment gateway or payment processor for reconciliation and dispute resolution.

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Required for Transaction Pricing Source Report to identify which price list was applied to calculate transaction totals, used in finance reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Enables Detailed Pricing Attribution linking each transaction to the exact price list item (SKU, tier) used, needed for margin analysis.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit‑center profitability reporting by linking each transaction to the responsible profit center.
Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: Route optimization and cost analysis reports tie each transaction to the logistical route it traversed.

Foreign key linking to marketplace.seller_settlement. Business justification: Each transaction is settled in a seller settlement period; adding seller_settlement_id to marketplace_transaction records which settlement the transaction belongs to.
Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Allows transaction‑level reconciliation to internal SKU for accurate inventory deduction and financial reporting.
Identifier of the sponsored advertising campaign if this transaction resulted from a sponsored listing.
Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link sales transaction to stock position for real‑time inventory deduction and on‑hand stock reporting.
Indicates whether this seller won the buy box for this listing at the time of transaction, affecting marketplace visibility and conversion.

The manufacturing batch or lot number to which this certification applies. Used when certifications are batch-specific rather than SKU-wide.
The total cost incurred to obtain this certification, including testing fees, application fees, and laboratory charges. Used for cost accounting and vendor management.
The URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification document, test report, or compliance declaration. Used for audit trails and regulatory reporting.
The unique certification or registration number issued by the certifying body. This is the externally-known identifier for the certification.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Certification Requirements

description: Current lifecycle status of the certification. Valid (active and in force), Expired (past expiry date), Pending (application submitted, awaiting approval), Revoked (withdrawn by issuing body), Suspended (temporarily invalid).
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Certification Requirements

description: The type of regulatory or safety certification. CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission), CE (Conformité Européenne), FCC (Federal Communications Commission), RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), UL (Underwriters Laboratories), FDA (Food and Drug Administration), REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals), California Prop 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPSC|CE|FCC|RoHS|UL|FDA|REACH|California Prop 65 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), European Commission CE Marking, Federal Communications Commission (FCC), FDA Regulations

description: Free-text field for additional compliance information, special conditions, exemptions, or notes related to the certification.
The timestamp when this compliance certification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CNY|CAD|AUD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid. Nullable for certifications with no expiration (perpetual validity).

The classification of potential hazards associated with the product (e.g., choking hazard, flammability, chemical exposure, electrical shock, sharp edges). Used for CPSC compliance and consumer safety warnings.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Hazard Classification

description: The date on which the certification was officially issued by the certifying body. This is the principal business event timestamp for the certification lifecycle.
The name of the regulatory authority, testing laboratory, or certification organization that issued the certification (e.g., CPSC, UL, TÜV, SGS, Intertek).
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), International Organization for Standardization (ISO)

description: Boolean indicator of whether this certification makes the product eligible for marketplace listing. True if certification is sufficient for listing, False if additional certifications are required.
Boolean indicator of whether this certification is legally mandatory for sale in the applicable market. True if mandatory, False if voluntary or optional.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), Federal Trade Commission (FTC)

description: The minimum recommended or required age in years for safe use of the product. Nullable if no age restriction applies.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Age Grading Requirements

description: The date by which the certification must be renewed to maintain validity. Used for proactive compliance management and renewal workflows.
The full mailing address of the responsible party. Required for CPSC compliance and regulatory reporting.

description: The email address of the responsible party for certification inquiries and regulatory correspondence.

description: The phone number of the responsible party for certification inquiries and regulatory correspondence.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Contact Information Requirements

description: The name of the legal entity or individual responsible for ensuring product compliance and maintaining the certification (e.g., manufacturer, importer, private labeler).
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Responsible Party Requirements

description: The date on which the certification was revoked by the issuing body. Nullable if certification has never been revoked.

description: A description of what aspects of the product are covered by this certification (e.g., electrical safety only, full product compliance, packaging materials only).

reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), International Organization for Standardization (ISO)

description: The unique identifier of the laboratory test report that supports this certification. Links to the underlying test data and results.
The name of the accredited testing laboratory that performed the compliance testing (e.g., UL, Intertek, SGS, TÜV, Bureau Veritas).

The timestamp when this compliance certification record was last modified. Used for audit trails and change tracking.
The username or identifier of the user or system that created this certification record. Used for audit trails and accountability.

Indicates whether this attribute value is indexed for search and faceted navigation on Product Listing Page (PLP) and search results.
Indicates whether this attribute value defines a product variant (e.g., size, color) that creates a distinct SKU within a product family.
The date and time when this attribute value record was last updated or modified.

The locale or language code for localized attribute values, following ISO 639-1 language and ISO 3166-1 country format (e.g., en_US, fr_FR, de_DE). Supports multi-language Product Detail Page (PDP) and Product Listing Page (PLP) content.
The raw string representation of the attribute value as captured from the source system or enrichment process.
The system of record from which this attribute value originated, such as PIM, ERP, or third-party data provider.
The attribute value when the attribute type is boolean. Used for yes/no or true/false product characteristics.
The attribute value when the attribute type is date. Used for time-based product attributes such as release date or expiration date.
The attribute value when the attribute type is numeric. Enables range filtering and comparison operations.
The attribute value when the attribute type is string or text. Used for faceted search and filtering.
The unit of measure for numeric attribute values, such as kg, cm, liters, watts, etc. Enables consistent product comparison.
Unique identifier for the catalog item. Primary key for the product catalog master record.

FK to product.product_category
Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: REQUIRED: Product detail pages pull rich HTML content from CMS; linking catalog items to content items drives PDP rendering.

10-character alphanumeric unique identifier used for product catalog management in Amazon-style marketplaces.
Primary color or color variant of the product, used for product differentiation, filtering, and variant management.
Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured or assembled, required for customs, tariffs, and trade compliance.

International standardized system of names and numbers for classifying traded products, used for customs clearance, tariff calculation, and international trade compliance.
Indicates whether the product has age restrictions for purchase (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, mature content), requiring age verification at checkout.

Product length dimension in centimeters, used for packaging, shipping, and warehouse storage optimization.

Current state of the product in its lifecycle. Active products are available for sale, discontinued products are phased out, draft products are under development, archived products are historical records.
The legal name of the company that manufactures the product, used for supplier management and compliance tracking.
The unique part number assigned by the manufacturer, used for warranty claims, technical support, and supplier coordination.
Description of the materials used in the product construction (e.g., 100% Cotton, Stainless Steel), used for product specifications and compliance labeling.

description: The minimum age in years required to purchase this product, enforced during checkout for age-restricted items.

Unique identifier of this product in the source PIM system, used for data lineage and system integration reconciliation.
Name of the Product Information Management system that is the authoritative source for this catalog item master data.
The primary display name of the product shown on Product Detail Pages (PDP) and Product Listing Pages (PLP). Used for customer-facing search and discovery.
Granular subcategory classification within the product taxonomy, enabling precise product discovery and merchandising strategies.
Extended product title optimized for Search Engine Optimization (SEO) and Search Engine Marketing (SEM), including key attributes and brand information.
High-level classification of the product category (e.g., Electronics, Apparel, Home & Garden), used for catalog organization and merchandising.
Number of days from delivery date within which the customer can initiate a return under the RMA policy.

description: Size designation of the product (e.g., Small, Medium, Large, or numeric sizes), used for apparel, footwear, and size-variant products.
Unique alphanumeric identifier for the product used across inventory, order management, and fulfillment systems. The authoritative SKU master identifier.
Tax classification code used to determine applicable sales tax, VAT, or GST rates for the product in different jurisdictions.
12-digit barcode identifier used for retail scanning and inventory tracking in North America.
Duration of the manufacturer or seller warranty in months, used for warranty claim validation and customer service.
Type of warranty coverage provided with the product (manufacturer warranty, seller warranty, extended warranty, or no warranty).
Product weight in kilograms, used for shipping cost calculation, carrier selection, and warehouse capacity planning.
Product width dimension in centimeters, used for packaging, shipping, and warehouse storage optimization.
Unique identifier for the product category node in the hierarchical taxonomy. Primary key.

Reference to the parent category node in the hierarchy. Null for root-level categories. Enables tree traversal and multi-level category structures.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Regulatory Mapping Report requires each product category to reference the primary regulation governing that category, enabling category-level compliance audits.
Average gross margin percentage for products in this category. Used for pricing strategy, promotional planning, and financial forecasting. Business-confidential metric.
Unique alphanumeric business identifier for the category. Used for system integration, API (Application Programming Interface) calls, and internal catalog management.
Detailed textual description of the category displayed on PLP (Product Listing Page) headers. Provides context and improves SEO (Search Engine Optimization) content richness.
reference: ISO 8601 - Data Elements and Interchange Formats

description: Human-readable name of the product category displayed on Product Listing Pages (PLP) and navigation menus. Used for customer-facing catalog browsing.
reference: ISO 8601 - Data Elements and Interchange Formats

description: Default commission rate percentage applied to marketplace seller transactions for products in this category. Used for GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) and seller disbursement calculations.
Identifier of the user or system account that created the category record. Used for audit trail and governance.
Timestamp when the category record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Default shipping class for products in this category. Used for carrier selection, rate calculation, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) determination in TMS (Transportation Management System).
reference: ISO 8601 - Data Elements and Interchange Formats

description: Numeric depth of the category node in the hierarchy tree. Root categories are level 0 or 1. Used for tree traversal algorithms and display logic.
Numeric sequence controlling the sort order of categories within the same parent level. Lower values appear first in navigation menus and PLP (Product Listing Page).
reference: ISO 8601 - Data Elements and Interchange Formats

description: Date when the category is scheduled to be deactivated or archived. Null for indefinite categories. Used for seasonal category management.
Date when the category becomes active and visible to customers. Used for scheduled category launches and merchandising campaigns.
Identifier from the source Product Information Management (PIM) system or ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system. Used for data integration and reconciliation.
Complete hierarchical path from root to current node, typically slash-delimited (e.g., Electronics/Smartphones/Android Phones). Used for breadcrumb navigation and SEO (Search Engine Optimization).

Hazardous material classification for products in this category. Used for shipping restrictions, carrier selection in TMS (Transportation Management System), and warehouse handling in WMS (Warehouse Management System).
URL to the category icon or glyph stored in CDN (Content Delivery Network). Used for compact navigation menus and mobile interfaces.
URL to the category thumbnail or banner image stored in CDN (Content Delivery Network). Used for visual navigation tiles and PLP (Product Listing Page) headers.
Boolean flag indicating whether the category is currently active and visible to customers. Inactive categories are hidden from navigation and search but retained for historical data.
reference: ISO 8601 - Data Elements and Interchange Formats

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this category is promoted as a featured category on the homepage or main navigation. Used for merchandising campaigns.
reference: ISO 8601 - Data Elements and Interchange Formats

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this category is a terminal node (leaf) with no child categories. Leaf categories typically contain SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) assignments.
Boolean flag indicating whether this category appears in customer-facing navigation menus. Hidden categories may exist for internal classification only.
reference: ISO 8601 - Data Elements and Interchange Formats

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this category is included in site search indexing and autocomplete suggestions. Used to control search faceting.
HTML meta description tag content for the category page. Displayed in search engine results snippets to improve CTR (Click-Through Rate).
Comma-separated list of keywords associated with the category for search engine indexing and internal search relevance tuning.
HTML meta title tag content for the category page. Optimized for search engine ranking and displayed in browser tabs and search results.
Total number of active SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) products currently assigned to this category. Used for merchandising analytics and empty category detection.
Boolean flag indicating whether products in this category require age verification at checkout (e.g., alcohol, tobacco). Used for compliance with FTC (Federal Trade Commission) and state regulations.

business_glossary_term: Requires CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission) Compliance Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether products in this category must comply with CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission) safety standards. Used for product onboarding validation.
Default number of days customers have to return products in this category. Used for RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) policy enforcement and customer service.
URL-friendly lowercase hyphenated string used in category page URLs for SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and clean URL structure (e.g., electronics-smartphones).

Industry classification standard used for this category (e.g., GS1 Global Product Classification, UNSPSC, eCl@ss). Supports multi-taxonomy mapping for B2B (Business to Business) and marketplace integration.
Identifier of the user or system account that last modified the category record. Used for audit trail and change management.
Timestamp when the category record was last modified. Used for change tracking, cache invalidation, and ETL (Extract Transform Load) incremental processing.
Unique identifier for the product identifier record. Primary key.
Reference to the catalog item or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that this identifier is associated with.

The identifier of the marketplace or sales channel where this identifier is used. Relevant for marketplace sellers who may have different identifiers across multiple platforms (e.g., Amazon, eBay, Walmart Marketplace). Null for identifiers not specific to a marketplace.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Identifier Registry requires each product identifier to be tied to the applicable regulation (e.g., barcode standards) for legal labeling compliance.

Reference to the seller or vendor who provided or uses this identifier. Relevant for marketplace models where multiple sellers may have their own SKU schemes. Null for identifiers managed centrally by the platform.

The physical barcode symbology or format used to encode this identifier for scanning and automated data capture. Examples include EAN-13, UPC-A, Code 128, QR Code, Data Matrix, ITF-14. NONE if no barcode representation exists. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EAN_13|UPC_A|CODE_128|QR_CODE|DATA_MATRIX|ITF_14|NONE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The check digit or validation character used to verify the integrity of the identifier value. Commonly used in UPC, EAN, and GTIN codes to detect data entry or transmission errors. Null if the identifier type does not use check digits.

Certification or compliance standard associated with this identifier. Examples include CPSC compliance for consumer products, FDA registration for food/drugs, CE marking for European products, or FCC certification for electronics. Null if not applicable.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), FDA 21 CFR, EU CE Marking Directive

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the product associated with this identifier is manufactured or originates. Used for customs classification, trade compliance, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1, World Trade Organization (WTO) Rules of Origin

description: The date and time when this product identifier record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Harmonized System (HS) code used for international trade and customs classification. This code determines tariffs, duties, and regulatory requirements for cross-border shipments. Typically 6-10 digits depending on country-specific extensions.
reference: World Customs Organization Harmonized System, WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement

description: The date on which this identifier ceases to be active or valid. Null indicates the identifier is currently active with no planned end date. Supports temporal tracking and identifier lifecycle management.
The date from which this identifier becomes active and valid for use. Supports temporal tracking of identifier assignments and changes over time.

A URL link to an external registry, database, or authority where additional information about this identifier can be found. Examples include GS1 registry lookup, manufacturer product pages, or regulatory databases.
reference: W3C URI Standards, GS1 Digital Link

description: The specific GTIN format used for this identifier. GTIN-8 (8 digits), GTIN-12 (UPC, 12 digits), GTIN-13 (EAN, 13 digits), GTIN-14 (case/pallet level, 14 digits). NONE if the identifier is not a GTIN.

Current lifecycle status of the product identifier. ACTIVE indicates the identifier is currently in use; INACTIVE indicates it is no longer used; DEPRECATED indicates it has been replaced by a newer identifier; PENDING indicates it is awaiting verification or activation; SUSPENDED indicates temporary deactivation.

The type or classification of the product identifier. Indicates the standard or system that issued the identifier (e.g., UPC for Universal Product Code, EAN for European Article Number, ASIN for Amazon Standard Identification Number, GTIN for Global Trade Item Number, ISBN for International Standard Book Number, MPN for Manufacturer Part Number, HS_CODE for Harmonized System code for customs, SELLER_SKU for marketplace seller SKU, INTERNAL_SKU for internal SKU). reference: GS1 General Specifications, ISO/IEC 15459, World Customs Organization Harmonized System

description: The actual identifier value or code. This is the unique alphanumeric string that represents the product in the specified identifier system (e.g., the 12-digit UPC code, the 13-digit EAN, the 10-character ASIN).
Boolean flag indicating whether this identifier is the primary or preferred identifier for the product. True if this is the main identifier used for product lookup and display; False otherwise.

Boolean flag indicating whether this identifier includes serialization at the unit level. True if the identifier uniquely identifies an individual item instance (e.g., GTIN + serial number for track-and-trace); False if it identifies a product type or SKU only.
reference: GS1 EPCIS, FDA Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this identifier has been verified for accuracy and validity. True if the identifier has been validated against the issuing authority or external registry; False if unverified or pending validation.
The organization or system that issued or assigned this identifier. Examples include GS1 for UPC/EAN/GTIN, Amazon for ASIN, manufacturer name for MPN, or internal system name for internal SKUs.

The lot or batch number associated with this identifier, used for grouping products manufactured or received together. Relevant for quality control, recalls, and expiration tracking. Null if not applicable.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 11, Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) Recall Guidelines

description: The username or identifier of the user or system process that last modified this product identifier record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this product identifier record was last modified or updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.

Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about this product identifier. May include information about identifier assignment rationale, special handling instructions, or historical context.

The level of packaging hierarchy that this identifier represents. EACH for individual consumer unit, INNER_PACK for multi-packs, CASE for shipping cases, PALLET for pallet-level, DISPLAY_UNIT for retail display units. Used for supply chain and logistics optimization.

The unique serial number assigned to an individual unit of the product, used in conjunction with the base identifier for item-level traceability. Null if the identifier is not serialized or if serialization is tracked separately.

The name or code of the source system from which this identifier record originated. Examples include PIM (Product Information Management System), OMS (Order Management System), marketplace integration systems, or EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) feeds.
The date and time when this identifier was last verified or validated against the issuing authority or external registry. Used for data quality auditing and compliance tracking.
The username or identifier of the user or system process that created this product identifier record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Unique identifier for the product listing record. Primary key for the product listing entity.
Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: REQUIRED: Listings can be part of a marketing campaign; linking enables listing‑level spend and conversion attribution.
Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: REQUIRED: Marketplace listings use CMS‑managed content items for titles and descriptions, enabling version control and SEO.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Listing Compliance Audit uses this FK to verify each listing satisfies its specific regulatory obligation, supporting the Listing Compliance Dashboard.

Reference to the seller or merchant account that owns this listing. Relevant for marketplace channels where multiple sellers can list the same SKU. For first-party (1P) listings, this references the company's own seller account.
Reference to the master SKU in the product catalog that this listing represents.

The minimum age required to purchase this product, if applicable. Common values: 18 (alcohol, tobacco), 21 (certain regulated products), 13 (COPPA compliance for digital products). Null indicates no age restriction.
reference: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Consumer Protection Standards

description: The average customer rating for this listing on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Aggregated from customer reviews and used to calculate listing quality score, influence buy box eligibility, and impact conversion rate. Null if no reviews exist.
The brand name displayed for this listing on the channel. May differ from master catalog brand if channel-specific branding or private label strategies are used.

Indicates whether this listing is eligible to compete for the buy box on marketplace channels. The buy box is the prominent 'Add to Cart' section on a product page. Eligibility is determined by factors such as price competitiveness, fulfillment method, seller performance metrics, and inventory availability.

The hierarchical category path where this listing is classified within the channel's taxonomy. Format typically follows a breadcrumb structure (e.g., 'Electronics > Computers > Laptops'). Used for browse navigation and category-based search filtering.

The sales channel where this product listing is published. Examples: WEB (website), MOBILE (mobile app), MARKETPLACE (third-party marketplace), B2B (business-to-business portal), BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), SOCIAL (social commerce platform).

The unique identifier assigned by the sales channel for this listing. For Amazon-style marketplaces, this would be the ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number). For other channels, this is the channel's native product identifier.

The percentage commission charged by the marketplace or channel for sales through this listing. Used to calculate Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) and net revenue. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).

The original or suggested retail price shown for comparison purposes to highlight discounts. Used to calculate savings percentage displayed on PDP and Product Listing Page (PLP). Null if no comparison pricing is used.

The physical condition of the product being sold through this listing. New: brand new in original packaging; Refurbished: restored to working condition; Used: previously owned; Open Box: new but packaging opened; Damaged: functional but with cosmetic or minor defects.

The timestamp when this product listing record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) and GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) requirements.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the listing price. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CAD.
The 13-digit European Article Number barcode identifier for the product. Commonly used in international markets. Equivalent to UPC but with an additional digit for country code.

The date when this listing will be or was deactivated and removed from the channel. Null indicates no planned end date. Used for seasonal products, limited-time offers, or product lifecycle management.

Indicates whether this listing is featured or promoted on the channel's homepage, category pages, or marketing campaigns. Featured listings receive higher visibility and priority in search and recommendation algorithms.

The fulfillment strategy for orders placed through this listing. FBM: Fulfilled By Merchant (seller ships); FBA: Fulfillment By Amazon-style (marketplace fulfillment center ships); 3PL: Third-Party Logistics provider; DROPSHIP: direct from manufacturer; BOPIS: Buy Online Pick Up In Store.

Indicates whether the product is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, labeling, and shipping restrictions. Examples include batteries, flammable liquids, aerosols, and chemicals. Compliance with Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) and transportation regulations is required.

The current available inventory quantity allocated to this listing on the channel. This may be a subset of total warehouse inventory based on channel allocation rules and safety stock policies. Used to determine Out of Stock (OOS) status and listing suppression.
The timestamp of the most recent order placed for this listing. Used to identify slow-moving inventory, calculate Daily Sales Rate (DSR), and trigger automated repricing or suppression rules.

Current operational status of the product listing on the channel. Active: live and purchasable; Suppressed: hidden due to quality or compliance issues; Pending: awaiting approval or activation; Inactive: manually disabled; Suspended: temporarily blocked; Archived: permanently removed.
The manufacturer's unique part number for the product. Used for product identification, catalog matching, and compliance reporting. Distinct from SKU, UPC, and EAN.

The maximum number of units a customer can purchase in a single order for this listing. Used to prevent overselling, manage high-demand products, or enforce promotional limits. Null indicates no maximum limit.
The minimum number of units a customer must purchase in a single order for this listing. Commonly used in B2B channels or bulk purchase scenarios. Null or 1 for standard retail listings.

The current selling price for this product listing on the channel, in the channel's default currency. This is the price displayed to customers and may differ from the master catalog price due to channel-specific pricing strategies, promotions, or marketplace fees.

A marketing badge or label displayed on the listing to highlight special offers or features. Examples: 'Best Seller', 'Limited Time Offer', 'Free Shipping', 'New Arrival', 'Exclusive'. Used to increase Click-Through Rate (CTR) and Conversion Rate (CVR).

A calculated score (0-100) representing the overall quality of the listing based on factors such as content completeness, image quality, keyword optimization, customer reviews, conversion rate (CVR), and compliance with channel guidelines. Higher scores improve search ranking and buy box eligibility.

Comma-separated list of geographic regions (country codes, state codes, or postal code patterns) where this listing cannot be shipped due to regulatory, legal, or business restrictions. Examples: 'CA,NY,HI' for US states, 'DEU,FRA' for countries.
The total number of customer reviews submitted for this listing. Higher review counts increase customer trust and improve search ranking. Used in conjunction with average rating for social proof.

Channel-specific search keywords and tags used to optimize product discoverability in the channel's search and recommendation engine. Supports SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and SEM (Search Engine Marketing) strategies. Comma-separated or space-separated list.
The package dimensions in format 'Length x Width x Height' with unit (e.g., '30x20x10 cm'). Used for shipping cost calculation, warehouse space planning, and carrier compatibility checks.

The total weight of the product including packaging, measured in kilograms. Used for shipping cost calculation, carrier selection, and logistics planning by the Transportation Management System (TMS).
The date when this listing was first activated and made available for purchase on the channel. Used for lifecycle tracking and performance analysis.

Explanation for why the listing status is 'suppressed'. Common reasons include: missing required attributes, policy violations, quality issues, pricing errors, inventory unavailability, restricted product category, or failed compliance checks (e.g., CPSC safety standards, PCI DSS for payment-related products).
The cumulative number of units sold through this listing since activation. Used for Best Seller Rank (BSR) calculation, inventory forecasting, and performance reporting.

The 12-digit Universal Product Code barcode identifier for the product. Required for many retail and marketplace channels. Used for inventory tracking, point-of-sale scanning, and catalog integration.

The timestamp when this product listing record was last modified. Tracks changes to listing attributes such as price, status, inventory, or content. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and synchronization with downstream systems.

business_glossary_term: Seller Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) Mapping Identifier
description: Unique identifier for the seller SKU mapping record. Primary key for the seller SKU mapping entity.

description: Reference to the platform's authoritative catalog item (ASIN-equivalent) that this seller SKU has been mapped to. Enables buy box competition and GMV attribution. Null when mapping status is unmapped or pending_review.

description: Reference to the marketplace seller who submitted this product listing. Links to the seller master record in the Seller Management Portal.

Boolean flag indicating whether this seller SKU is eligible for automatic remapping if the canonical catalog item is merged or deprecated. False requires manual seller intervention for catalog changes.
reference: Product Information Management System - Catalog Maintenance Standards

description: The authoritative brand name from the platform's canonical catalog for the mapped item. Used to detect brand mismatches and potential intellectual property violations.
reference: Product Information Management System - Brand Master Data

description: The authoritative product category hierarchy path from the platform's canonical taxonomy that the mapped catalog item belongs to. Used for category compliance validation.
reference: Product Information Management System - Canonical Taxonomy

description: Status of regulatory and policy compliance validation for this seller SKU mapping. Includes CPSC product safety, intellectual property, restricted product category, and marketplace policy checks.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Product Safety Standards

description: Detailed explanation of compliance check failures including specific regulations violated, policy breaches, or safety concerns. Null when compliance_check_status is passed or not_required.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) - Compliance Reporting

description: The date and time when this seller SKU mapping record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.

business_glossary_term: Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) Attribution Enabled Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether sales of this seller SKU are included in GMV reporting and seller commission calculations. False may indicate mapping is pending or rejected.
Computer vision similarity score (0.0000 to 1.0000) comparing seller-provided product images to canonical catalog item images. Used when image_match mapping method is applied.

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this mapped seller SKU is eligible to compete for the buy box on the product detail page. False may indicate seller performance issues, policy violations, or pricing problems.

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this seller SKU was identified as a duplicate of an existing seller listing for the same canonical catalog item. True indicates the seller is attempting to create multiple listings for the same product.

description: The date and time when this seller SKU mapping record was most recently updated. Tracks any changes to mapping status, canonical item reference, or metadata.
The date and time of the most recent manual or automated review of this mapping. Used for audit trails and re-review scheduling.

Algorithmic confidence score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating the certainty of the mapping between seller SKU and canonical catalog item. Higher scores indicate stronger match signals. Used to prioritize manual review queue.
reference: Search and Recommendation Engine - Matching Algorithm Standards

description: The technique or algorithm used to establish the mapping between seller SKU and canonical catalog item. Exact_barcode_match is highest confidence; manual_review indicates human intervention was required.

description: The system or integration channel through which the seller submitted this SKU for mapping (e.g., Seller Portal UI, API, EDI, Bulk Upload). Used for channel performance analysis.

Current lifecycle status of the seller SKU mapping. Mapped indicates successful linkage to canonical catalog; pending_review requires manual intervention; rejected indicates failed mapping with reason; unmapped indicates no match found.
reference: Product Information Management System - Mapping Workflow

description: The date and time when the seller SKU was successfully mapped to the canonical catalog item. Represents the business event time when the mapping became effective for buy box competition.
Processing priority assigned to this mapping request based on seller tier, product category importance, or business rules. High priority mappings are reviewed first in the manual review queue.
reference: Seller Management Portal - Service Level Agreement (SLA) Standards

description: Free-text explanation provided by the reviewer detailing the specific reasons for rejection and guidance for seller remediation. Null when mapping status is not rejected.
reference: Product Information Management System - Review Process Standards

description: Categorical reason code explaining why a seller SKU mapping was rejected. Used for seller feedback, catalog quality governance, and compliance reporting. reference: Product Information Management System - Catalog Quality Standards

description: The brand name as declared by the seller in their listing submission. Used for brand validation and intellectual property compliance checks during mapping.
reference: Product Information Management System - Brand Registry

description: The product category hierarchy path as declared by the seller in their listing submission. Used for category validation and mismatch detection during mapping.
reference: Product Information Management System - Taxonomy Standards

description: The 13-digit EAN barcode provided by the seller for exact barcode matching to canonical catalog items. Used in international marketplace operations.
The product title or name as provided by the seller in their listing submission. Used for fuzzy matching and manual review processes.

description: The seller's own SKU or product identifier code used in their internal catalog system. This is the seller-provided reference that must be mapped to the platform's canonical catalog.

description: The 12-digit UPC barcode provided by the seller for exact barcode matching to canonical catalog items. Critical for automated mapping via exact_barcode_match method.
The date and time when the seller originally submitted this SKU listing for mapping. Used to calculate mapping SLA performance and queue aging metrics.
Natural language processing similarity score (0.0000 to 1.0000) comparing seller product title to canonical catalog item title. Used when fuzzy_title_match mapping method is applied.

description: Unique identifier for the product bundle. Primary key.
Reference to the brand or manufacturer of the bundle. Used for brand-specific merchandising, reporting, and vendor management.
Reference to the product taxonomy category this bundle belongs to. Used for navigation, search filtering, and merchandising.

Reference to the marketplace seller offering this bundle. Supports multi-seller marketplace models and Fulfillment By Amazon (FBA)-style operations.
Timestamp when the bundle was approved for publication by merchandising or compliance teams. Supports governance workflows and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) audit requirements.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the bundle, used in catalog systems, order management, and fulfillment. Typically alphanumeric SKU-style code.
Detailed marketing description of the bundle, including value proposition, contents summary, and usage scenarios. Displayed on PDP for customer decision-making.
Human-readable name of the bundle displayed on Product Detail Pages (PDP) and Product Listing Pages (PLP).
Current lifecycle status of the bundle in the catalog. Controls visibility and sellability across channels.

Classification of the bundle structure: fixed bundle (pre-defined items), configurable bundle (customer selects components), gift set (curated collection), kit (assembly required), promotional bundle (limited-time offer), subscription box (recurring delivery).

Comma-separated list of regulatory certifications and compliance standards the bundle meets (e.g., Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), CE, RoHS, FDA). Required for cross-border trade and marketplace listing approval.
Total number of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) included in the bundle. Used for fulfillment complexity scoring and customer messaging.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the bundle is manufactured or assembled. Required for customs, tariffs, and World Trade Organization (WTO) compliance.
Reference to the user or system account that created the bundle record. Supports audit trails and General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) data lineage requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 30 - Records of Processing Activities

description: Timestamp when the bundle record was first created in the Product Information Management (PIM) system. Used for catalog audit trails and data lineage.
Comma-separated list of bundle IDs recommended for cross-sell on PDP. Supports recommendation engine and Average Order Value (AOV) optimization.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the bundle price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Timestamp when the bundle was marked as discontinued. Used for product lifecycle reporting and inventory liquidation planning.
Percentage discount applied to the bundle compared to sum-of-parts or list price. Used for promotional messaging and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) calculations.
Date when the bundle is no longer available for sale. Nullable for evergreen bundles. Used for limited-time offers and seasonal discontinuation.
Date when the bundle becomes available for sale. Used for seasonal launches, promotional campaigns, and catalog planning.
International trade classification code for customs and duty calculation. Required for cross-border e-commerce and Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) with customs authorities.
URL of the primary product image for the bundle, hosted on Content Delivery Network (CDN). Used for PDP, PLP, and digital marketing assets.
Indicates whether customers can customize bundle components (select variants, add-ons, or substitutions) at purchase time.
Indicates whether the bundle is eligible for return under the standard Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) policy. Some bundles (e.g., personalized, perishable) may be non-returnable.
Indicates whether the bundle can be purchased as a recurring subscription with automated replenishment.

Indicates whether the bundle is virtual (components shipped separately) or physical (pre-packaged as single unit). Impacts fulfillment strategy and Warehouse Management System (WMS) pick-pack-ship logic.
Manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) or original price before discounts. Used to calculate savings and display strikethrough pricing.
Maximum number of bundle units a customer can purchase in a single order. Used to prevent inventory depletion and manage promotional limits.
Minimum number of bundle units a customer must purchase in a single order. Supports bulk-buy and wholesale scenarios.
Reference to the user or system account that last modified the bundle record. Supports change management and SOX compliance.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the bundle record. Supports change data capture (CDC) and incremental Extract Transform Load (ETL) processes.
Physical dimensions of the bundle package in LxWxH format (centimeters). Used for warehouse slotting, cartonization, and Less Than Truck Load (LTL) / Full Truck Load (FTL) planning.
Selling price of the bundle in the base currency. For sum-of-parts strategy, this may be calculated; for fixed price, this is the set amount.

Method used to calculate bundle price: fixed price (single price regardless of components), sum-of-parts (total of individual SKU prices), discounted (percentage off sum-of-parts), tiered (price varies by quantity), dynamic (algorithmic pricing).
Number of days from delivery date during which the bundle can be returned. Supports Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) and Net Promoter Score (NPS) optimization.
Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases for Search Engine Optimization (SEO) and internal site search relevance ranking. Improves Click-Through Rate (CTR) and Conversion Rate (CVR).
reference: SEO and Search Engine Marketing (SEM) Best Practices

description: Shipping method classification for the bundle. Determines carrier eligibility, Service Level Agreement (SLA) targets, and last-mile delivery options.
Concise summary of the bundle for display on PLP, search results, and mobile views. Typically 150-200 characters.
Tax classification code for the bundle, used to determine applicable sales tax, VAT, or GST rates. Integrates with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) tax engines.
Combined shipping weight of all bundle components in kilograms. Used for carrier selection, freight cost calculation, and Transportation Management System (TMS) route optimization.
Comma-separated list of higher-value bundle IDs recommended for upsell. Supports Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) growth and Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) recovery.
Unique identifier for the bundle component association record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle component must reference its parent bundle to establish hierarchy; existing model only links component SKUs, missing bundle reference.
Reference to the individual component SKU that is part of the bundle.
Reference to the parent bundle product SKU that contains this component.
Reference to the primary supplier for this component. Used for procurement, vendor management, and supply chain traceability.
Textual instructions for warehouse staff on how to assemble or package this component within the bundle. Used during pick-pack-ship operations.

Optional component-level pricing override for this SKU within the bundle context. Used when the component has a different price when sold as part of the bundle versus standalone. Null if standard component pricing applies.
The quantity of this component SKU required in the bundle. Supports fractional quantities for items sold by weight or volume.
The functional role of this component within the bundle (e.g., primary product, accessory, optional add-on, complementary item, replacement part, or sample).

Current lifecycle status of this component within the bundle. Active components are available for sale, inactive are temporarily unavailable, discontinued are permanently removed, and pending are awaiting approval.
The volume of this component in cubic meters. Used for calculating total bundle volume for warehouse space planning and shipping container optimization.
The weight of this component in kilograms. Used for calculating total bundle weight for shipping cost estimation and carrier selection.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where this component was manufactured or produced. Used for customs declarations and trade compliance.

business_glossary_term: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether this component meets Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) safety standards and compliance requirements. True if compliant, False otherwise.
The date and time when this bundle component association record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The order in which this component should be displayed in the bundle configurator UI, Product Detail Page (PDP), and fulfillment documentation. Lower numbers appear first.
The date after which this component is no longer part of the bundle. Null for components with no planned end date. Used for time-bound bundle configurations and product lifecycle management.
The date from which this component becomes part of the bundle. Used for time-bound bundle configurations and seasonal product changes.
Harmonized System code for this component used for international trade classification, customs duties, and tariff determination.
Priority level for reserving inventory for this component during bundle order processing. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by Warehouse Management System (WMS) for allocation logic.
Indicates whether this component is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, packaging, and shipping compliance. True if hazardous, False otherwise.
Indicates whether this component is mandatory for the bundle. True if the component must be included, False if it is optional or can be removed by the customer.
Indicates whether this component can be substituted with an alternative SKU during fulfillment if out of stock. True if substitution is allowed, False otherwise.

Indicates whether this component is visible to the customer on the Product Detail Page (PDP) and Product Listing Page (PLP). False for hidden components like packaging materials or promotional inserts.
The date and time when this bundle component association record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The number of days required to procure or manufacture this component from the time an order is placed. Used for inventory replenishment planning and bundle availability forecasting.
The minimum quantity of this component that must be ordered from the supplier. Used for procurement planning and supplier contract compliance.
The username or identifier of the user who last modified this bundle component record. Used for audit trail and data governance.
Code identifying the substitution group for this component. Components with the same substitution group code can be interchanged during fulfillment. Null if component is not substitutable.
Unique identifier for the product digital asset record. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to content.digital_asset. Business justification: REQUIRED: Central Digital Asset Management links SKU assets to DAM for consistent image/video reuse across channels.
Reference to the SKU this digital asset belongs to. Links asset to product master data.
Boolean flag indicating whether the asset meets WCAG accessibility standards (alt text, captions, transcripts). Required for regulatory compliance.
Classification of the digital asset media type. Determines rendering and display logic on PDP and PLP pages.

Specific content classification within the asset type. Distinguishes between hero images, lifestyle images, product titles, SEO metadata, and other content elements for PDP/PLP presentation. Date and time when the digital asset record was first created in the system. Audit trail for content lifecycle tracking.
Percentage score (0-100) measuring content enrichment quality and completeness. Calculated based on presence of required attributes, SEO metadata, and multi-locale coverage.
Unique identifier from the source system or external DAM platform. Enables cross-system reconciliation and integration.
Boolean flag indicating whether the digital asset is currently active and available for use. Supports soft-delete and content lifecycle management.

Date and time when the digital asset record was last modified. Tracks content update activity for governance and audit purposes.
Name of the upstream system that provided the digital asset (e.g., PIM, DAM, vendor portal). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.
Comma-separated list of descriptive tags for asset categorization and search. Supports content discovery and filtering in DAM systems.
Free-text notes describing usage restrictions, attribution requirements, or special licensing terms. Supports legal compliance and rights management.
