Primary key for relation
Identifier of the marketplace seller who submitted or owns this product relationship. Applicable for seller-submitted relations in marketplace models. Nullable for platform-curated relationships.
Identifier of the source catalog item (SKU) from which the relationship originates. This is the product displayed on the PDP or cart page that triggers the recommendation.
Identifier of the target catalog item (SKU) being recommended or related. This is the product suggested to the customer based on the source SKU.
Current approval state of the product relationship in the merchandising workflow. Applicable for seller-submitted or algorithmically-generated relations requiring manual review before activation.
Username or identifier of the merchandising team member who approved the product relationship for activation. Supports audit trail and accountability for manual curation decisions.
Date and time when the product relationship was approved by the merchandising team. Supports audit trail and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking for approval workflows.

Percentage of customers who clicked on the related product recommendation when displayed, calculated as clicks divided by impressions. Used to measure recommendation effectiveness and optimize display logic.

Percentage of customers who purchased the related product after clicking the recommendation, calculated as purchases divided by clicks. Key performance indicator (KPI) for recommendation quality and revenue impact.
Date and time when the product relationship record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Ordinal position in which the related product should appear in the recommendation widget on the Product Detail Page (PDP) or cart page. Lower numbers indicate higher priority placement.

Date after which the product relationship is no longer active and should not be displayed. Nullable for open-ended relationships. Supports time-bound merchandising campaigns and product lifecycle management.
Date from which the product relationship becomes active and eligible for display in recommendation widgets. Supports time-bound merchandising campaigns and seasonal promotions.
Boolean flag indicating whether the product relationship is currently active and should be displayed in recommendation widgets. True means active, False means inactive or suppressed.

Date and time when the performance metrics (impressions, clicks, conversions, revenue) were last refreshed from the analytics pipeline. Supports data freshness monitoring and Extract Transform Load (ETL) orchestration.

Free-text notes from merchandising team explaining the rationale for the product relationship, special handling instructions, or campaign context. Supports manual curation workflows and knowledge transfer.
Date and time when the product relationship record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and incremental Extract Transform Load (ETL) processing.

Business priority ranking assigned by merchandising team to override algorithmic display position. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for strategic product placement and promotional campaigns.

Origin or method by which the product relationship was created. Indicates whether the relation was manually curated by merchandising team, generated algorithmically by recommendation engine, submitted by marketplace seller, or derived from specific ML techniques (collaborative filtering, content-based, hybrid).

Type of product-to-product relationship. Defines the nature of the recommendation: cross_sell (complementary product), upsell (higher-value alternative), frequently_bought_together (bundle suggestion), accessory (add-on item), replacement (substitute product), spare_part (component or part).

Total revenue in base currency generated from purchases of the related product that were attributed to this recommendation relationship. Used to measure Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) and recommendation engine effectiveness.

Numeric score representing the strength or confidence of the product relationship, typically ranging from 0.0000 to 1.0000. Higher scores indicate stronger associations and higher likelihood of conversion. Used by recommendation algorithms to rank suggestions.
Cumulative count of times customers clicked on the related product recommendation. Used to calculate Click-Through Rate (CTR) and Conversion Rate (CVR).
Cumulative count of times customers purchased the related product after clicking the recommendation. Used to calculate Conversion Rate (CVR) and measure revenue attribution.
Cumulative count of times the related product recommendation was displayed to customers. Used to calculate Click-Through Rate (CTR) and assess recommendation reach.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this cost center master data was sourced. Primarily SAP S/4HANA CO module. Used for data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Silver layer.
reference: NIST Cybersecurity Framework — Data Provenance; ISO 27001

description: Indicates whether this cost center is in scope for Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal controls over financial reporting. SOX-relevant cost centers require documented control procedures, segregation of duties, and periodic audit evidence.
reference: SOX Section 302; SOX Section 404; PCAOB AS 2201

description: Tax jurisdiction code assigned to this cost center for indirect tax (VAT/GST/sales tax) determination and reporting. Used in SAP FI-TX for tax calculation on cost center-related procurement and expense transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX Module; FTC Consumer Protection; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element code associated with this cost center for project cost tracking. Enables project-level cost reporting and capital expenditure (CapEx) vs. operating expenditure (OpEx) classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Module — WBS Elements; GAAP ASC 350 Capitalization

description: The date from which this cost center is active and can accept financial postings. Corresponds to the SAP CO validity period start date. Cost center records are time-sliced; this field defines the start of the current validity interval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module — Validity Period; GAAP ASC 280

description: The date until which this cost center is active and can accept financial postings. Corresponds to the SAP CO validity period end date. A null or far-future date (e.g., 9999-12-31) indicates an open-ended validity period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module — Validity Period; GAAP ASC 280

description: Primary key for fiscal_period
Self-referencing FK on fiscal_period (prior_fiscal_period_id)
Numeric month (1‑12) of the period's start date in the Gregorian calendar.
Indicates whether the period follows the standard Gregorian calendar or a custom fiscal calendar.
Four‑digit calendar year of the period's start date.
Timestamp when the fiscal period record was first created in the system.
Total number of calendar days covered by the period.
Optional free‑text description providing context or notes about the period.
Quarter number (1‑4) within the fiscal year.
Week number within the fiscal year, useful for weekly reporting.
Four‑digit year to which the period belongs, based on the company's fiscal calendar.
True when all postings for the period are finalized and no further adjustments are allowed.
True if this period is the active fiscal period for ongoing transactions.
Numeric month (1‑12) of the period's start date.
Last calendar date of the fiscal period.
Human‑readable name of the fiscal period, e.g., "FY2024 Q1" or "2024‑01".
Sequential order of the period within the fiscal year (e.g., 1 for Jan, 2 for Feb).
First calendar date of the fiscal period.
Current lifecycle status of the period for reporting and posting purposes.
Category of the period indicating its granularity (month, quarter, year, etc.).
Calendar quarter (1‑4) derived from the start date.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the fiscal period record.
Unique surrogate identifier for the profit center record in the enterprise data lakehouse. Primary key for the profit_center master data product.

Method used to allocate shared costs and overhead to this profit center in SAP CO. 'direct' = costs posted directly; 'assessment' = allocated via assessment cycles; 'distribution' = allocated via distribution cycles; 'settlement' = settled from internal orders or projects.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; SOX Section 404; GAAP/IFRS

description: Planned annual operating cost allocation for this profit center in the profit center's primary currency. Supports cost center management, overhead allocation, and budget adherence tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; SOX Section 404; GAAP/IFRS

description: Planned annual revenue target for this profit center in the profit center's primary currency. Used for budget vs. actuals variance analysis and segment-level P&L reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; SOX Section 404; GAAP/IFRS

description: The fiscal year for which the current budget allocation is defined for this profit center. Used to align budget tracking with the enterprise fiscal calendar in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; SOX Section 404; GAAP/IFRS

description: Business justification or reason code for the most recent change to this profit center's master data (e.g., 'Organizational restructuring', 'New business segment launch', 'Merger integration'). Required for SOX audit trail documentation.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA Change Documents

description: SAP company code representing the legal entity to which this profit center is assigned for financial consolidation and statutory reporting. Corresponds to the BUKRS field in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

description: SAP Controlling (CO) area code to which this profit center belongs. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost and profit center accounting is performed. Corresponds to the KOKRS field in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA Module; SOX Section 404

description: Associated cost center code in SAP CO for tracking operating expenses attributable to this profit center. Enables cost allocation and margin analysis at the segment level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Module; SOX Section 404

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country of operation for this profit center. Used for statutory reporting, tax jurisdiction assignment, and regulatory compliance (GDPR, CCPA).
Timestamp when this profit center record was first created in the enterprise data lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per SOX and ISO 27001 controls.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO/IEC 27001; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary transaction currency for this profit center (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for financial postings, P&L reporting, and GMV-to-revenue reconciliation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA; GAAP/IFRS

description: Data classification level assigned to financial data associated with this profit center per enterprise data governance policy. Drives access control, data masking, and retention policies in the lakehouse.
reference: GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798; ISO/IEC 27001; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: The date on which this profit center was deactivated or archived. Null if the profit center is currently active. Used for historical reporting, audit trails, and ensuring no new postings are made to deactivated profit centers.
Organizational department code associated with this profit center. Links the profit center to the enterprise organizational structure for headcount allocation and overhead cost distribution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR/OM Module; SOX Section 404

business_glossary_term: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Profit Center ID
description: Native profit center identifier as stored in the source ERP system (SAP S/4HANA). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational SAP system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA; SOX Section 404; ISO/IEC 27001

description: SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal year calendar applicable to this profit center (e.g., 'K4' for calendar year, 'V3' for April-March). Determines period mapping for financial reporting and budget tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP/IFRS; SOX Section 404

description: Geographic region or market associated with this profit center (e.g., 'North America', 'EMEA', 'APAC', 'LATAM'). Supports regional P&L segmentation and cross-border revenue reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: north_america|emea|apac|latam|global — promote to reference product]
reference: WTO International Trade Regulations; GAAP/IFRS Segment Reporting (ASC 280/IFRS 8)

description: Primary General Ledger account code associated with this profit center for revenue and cost postings. Links profit center activity to the chart of accounts in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL for financial consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Module; SOX Section 404; GAAP/IFRS

business_glossary_term: Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) to Revenue Ratio
description: The standard ratio used to convert Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) to recognized net revenue for this profit center. For marketplace segments, this reflects the take rate (commission percentage). Critical for GMV-to-revenue reconciliation in financial reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX Section 302; SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA

description: Code of the profit center group (node in the standard hierarchy) to which this profit center belongs. Profit center groups enable aggregated reporting across multiple profit centers in SAP EC-PCA.

Numeric level of the profit center within the enterprise organizational hierarchy (e.g., 1 = top-level division, 2 = business unit, 3 = sub-segment). Supports roll-up reporting in SAP EC-PCA standard hierarchy.

SAP trading partner code identifying the affiliated company for intercompany transactions originating from this profit center. Required for intercompany elimination during financial consolidation. Corresponds to the VBUND field in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Consolidation; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS ASC 810

description: Indicates whether this is the dummy profit center used to capture postings that cannot be assigned to a real profit center. Each controlling area has exactly one dummy profit center. Corresponds to the DUMMY field in SAP EC-PCA.

Indicates whether this profit center is used for statistical (informational) postings only and does not carry actual financial balances. Statistical profit centers are used for internal management reporting without affecting the general ledger.

Timestamp of the most recent update to this profit center record. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail compliance, and detecting unauthorized modifications per SOX internal control requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO/IEC 27001; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Indicates whether this profit center tracks Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) as a primary financial metric. Applicable to subscription-based or SaaS business segments. Drives MRR and ARR reporting in financial dashboards.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA

description: Business code of the parent profit center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables hierarchical roll-up of P&L data for consolidated segment reporting. Self-referencing relationship within the profit center hierarchy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA Standard Hierarchy; SOX Section 302

description: Functional classification of the profit center indicating whether it is primarily a revenue-generating, cost-bearing, or investment unit. Used for internal profitability analysis and capital allocation decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA; SOX Section 404; ISO 27001

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the profit center as defined in SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA. Used in financial postings, journal entries, and segment reporting. Corresponds to the PRCTR field in SAP.
Detailed narrative description of the profit center's business scope, revenue streams, and cost responsibilities. Provides context for financial analysts and auditors.
Human-readable business name of the profit center (e.g., 'Marketplace - North America', 'DTC - Apparel', 'B2B - Enterprise'). Used in P&L reports and management dashboards.

Current lifecycle status of the profit center in SAP EC-PCA. 'active' = operational and accepting postings; 'inactive' = temporarily suspended; 'locked' = blocked for new postings (SOX control); 'pending_activation' = created but not yet approved; 'archived' = historical, no longer in use.

Name or employee ID of the business manager accountable for the profit center's financial performance, budget adherence, and P&L outcomes. Used for management reporting and SOX accountability assignments.

Accounting method applied for revenue recognition within this profit center. Determines how GMV converts to recognized revenue. 'point_in_time' for standard product sales; 'over_time' for subscription/SaaS; 'percentage_completion' for long-term contracts; 'milestone' for project-based billing.

Classification of the profit center by primary business model or channel. Drives segment-level P&L reporting and GMV-to-revenue reconciliation. Values: marketplace (third-party seller platform), dtc (Direct to Consumer), b2b (Business to Business), b2c (Business to Consumer), c2c (Consumer to Consumer), fulfillment (logistics/fulfillment services), advertising (ad revenue segment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: marketplace|dtc|b2b|b2c|c2c|fulfillment|advertising — promote to reference product]
reference: FTC Consumer Protection Standards; SOX Section 302; SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA

description: Abbreviated display name for the profit center used in condensed financial reports, dashboards, and column headers where space is limited. Corresponds to the KTEXT field in SAP EC-PCA.

Indicates whether this profit center is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal control requirements. SOX-relevant profit centers require enhanced audit trails, segregation of duties, and periodic management sign-off on financial balances.

The commission percentage charged to marketplace sellers on each transaction, representing the portion of GMV retained as revenue by the platform. Applicable to marketplace and seller-facing profit centers. Used in GMV-to-revenue reconciliation.
reference: FTC Advertising Standards; SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA; SOX Section 302

description: Planned profit margin percentage target for this profit center, expressed as a percentage of revenue. Used as a KPI benchmark for segment-level profitability reporting and management performance evaluation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; SOX Section 302; GAAP/IFRS

description: Tax jurisdiction code assigned to this profit center for tax calculation and reporting purposes. Determines applicable tax rates and rules for revenue and cost postings. Relevant for multi-jurisdiction e-commerce operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TX; FTC; GDPR; CCPA; WTO

description: The date from which this profit center becomes effective and can receive financial postings. Defines the start of the profit center's validity period in SAP EC-PCA. Corresponds to the DATAB field in SAP.
The date until which this profit center remains valid for financial postings. Null or 9999-12-31 indicates an open-ended validity period. Corresponds to the DATBI field in SAP EC-PCA.
Unique surrogate identifier for each bank statement record in the enterprise data lakehouse. Primary key for the bank_statement data product.
Reference to the General Ledger (GL) cash account associated with this bank statement. Links the bank statement to the corresponding GL account for reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GL Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Classification of the corporate bank account type. Determines cash management rules, interest treatment, and reconciliation procedures. Zero-balance accounts (ZBA) are common in e-commerce treasury structures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Cash Management; Treasury Best Practices

description: Number of transaction lines on this statement that were automatically matched and reconciled by the SAP S/4HANA automated bank reconciliation engine without manual intervention. Used to measure automation efficiency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation; Finance KPI Reporting

description: The funds available for immediate use at the statement date, net of holds, pending transactions, and float. May differ from closing_balance due to uncleared items. Used for liquidity management and cash forecasting.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053 AVAI; SAP S/4HANA Cash Management

description: The bank account number associated with this statement as held at the banking institution. Masked or tokenized in non-restricted environments per PCI DSS requirements. Used to identify the corporate cash account.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3; GDPR Article 32; ISO 27001

description: SWIFT Bank Identifier Code (BIC) / Business Identifier Code uniquely identifying the banking institution and branch. Conforms to ISO 9362 standard. Used for international wire transfers and bank identification in SAP S/4HANA.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the banking institution where the account is held. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, cross-border reporting, and GDPR/CCPA applicability assessment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 44; CCPA; FTC Regulations

description: Full legal name of the banking institution that issued the statement (e.g., JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, HSBC). Used for counterparty identification in cash management and treasury reporting.
Unique reference number assigned by the banking institution to this statement transmission or file. Used for bank-side traceability, dispute resolution, and re-import identification.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053; SWIFT MT940 Field 20; SAP S/4HANA

description: The account balance at the end of the statement period as reported by the banking institution. Represents the final cash position for the period. Used for cash flow reporting and GL reconciliation.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053 CLBD; SWIFT MT940 Field 62F; SOX Section 302/404

description: SAP S/4HANA company code identifying the legal entity that owns the bank account. Used for financial consolidation and intercompany reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Timestamp when the bank statement record was first imported and created in the enterprise data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and provides data lineage for the Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the bank account statement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All monetary amounts on this statement are denominated in this currency.

Number of individual transaction lines on this statement that could not be automatically matched to GL entries and require manual investigation. Used for reconciliation quality monitoring and workload management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation; SOX Section 302/404

description: The fiscal accounting period (1–12 for monthly, or 1–4 for quarterly) within the fiscal year to which this statement is assigned. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA posting periods for GL reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

description: The fiscal year to which this bank statement belongs for financial reporting and GL period assignment in SAP S/4HANA. Used for annual financial close, SOX reporting, and MRR/ARR reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

description: The date on which the bank statement was posted to the General Ledger (GL) in SAP S/4HANA. May differ from statement_date due to processing lag. Used for period-end close and financial reporting accuracy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

description: International Bank Account Number (IBAN) for the corporate bank account per ISO 13616 standard. Used for cross-border payment identification and reconciliation in international treasury operations.
reference: ISO 13616 IBAN Standard; PCI DSS v4.0; GDPR Article 32

description: Timestamp when the bank statement file was electronically received and imported into SAP S/4HANA or the data platform via EDI, SWIFT, or API. Used for SLA monitoring of bank data feeds and processing latency tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Statement Import; EDI Standards; ISO 20022

description: Indicates whether the bank account is used for intercompany transactions between legal entities within the Ecommerce enterprise group. Intercompany statements require elimination entries during financial consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Intercompany Reconciliation; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

description: Indicates whether the bank statement record has been locked to prevent further modifications after period-end close or SOX audit sign-off. Locked statements cannot be altered without an authorized unlock workflow.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA Period Close; ISO 27001

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the bank statement record, including reconciliation status changes, balance corrections, or posting updates. Required for SOX audit trail and change tracking.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001; SAP S/4HANA Change Log

description: Free-text field for finance team annotations regarding the bank statement, such as reconciliation exceptions, manual adjustments, or special handling instructions. Supports audit documentation requirements.

The account balance at the start of the statement period as reported by the banking institution. Used as the baseline for reconciliation; must equal the prior statement closing balance for continuity validation.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053 OPBD; SWIFT MT940 Field 60F; SOX Section 302/404

description: The last calendar date of the bank statement coverage period. Defines the closing boundary of the statement window. Typically aligns with the statement_date for end-of-period statements.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053; SAP S/4HANA Bank Statement Import

description: The first calendar date of the bank statement coverage period. Together with period_end_date, defines the statement window for which transactions are reported. Used for period-based cash flow analysis.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053; SAP S/4HANA Bank Statement Import

description: Username or employee identifier of the finance team member who completed and approved the bank reconciliation. Required for SOX segregation of duties (SoD) controls and audit trail documentation.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001 Access Control; NIST SP 800-53

description: The date on which the bank statement reconciliation was completed and approved. Null if reconciliation is still in progress. Used for period-end close tracking and SOX control evidence.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation; GAAP/IFRS

description: Current lifecycle status of the bank statement reconciliation workflow. Tracks progression from import through automated matching to final posting in SAP S/4HANA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unreconciled|in_progress|reconciled|exception|posted|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation; SOX Section 302/404; NIST SP 800-53

description: Identifies the originating system or channel through which the bank statement was received and imported. Used for data lineage tracking, ETL audit, and troubleshooting import failures.
reference: ISO 20022; EDI Standards; SAP S/4HANA Integration

description: The official date of the bank statement as issued by the banking institution. Represents the business event date for the statement period end. Used as the primary date for cash flow reporting and reconciliation.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053; SWIFT MT940; SOX Section 302/404

description: Electronic file format in which the bank statement was received from the banking institution. Determines the parsing and import logic applied during ETL ingestion into SAP S/4HANA and the data lakehouse.
reference: SWIFT MT940/MT942; ISO 20022 camt.052/camt.053; BAI2 Standard; OFX Standard

description: Externally assigned statement number issued by the banking institution. Serves as the primary business identifier for the statement as received from the bank. Used for matching and audit trail.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053 Bank-to-Customer Statement; SWIFT MT940

description: Classification of the bank statement by reporting frequency or finality. Determines processing rules and reconciliation cadence in SAP S/4HANA. Intraday statements support real-time cash visibility.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.052/camt.053; SWIFT MT940/MT942; SAP S/4HANA

description: Aggregate sum of all credit (inflow) transactions on the statement for the period. Used for cash inflow analysis, revenue reconciliation, and GMV-to-cash settlement validation.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053; SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation; SOX Section 302/404

description: Aggregate sum of all debit (outflow) transactions on the statement for the period. Used for cash outflow analysis, accounts payable settlement validation, and expense reconciliation.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053; SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation; SOX Section 302/404

description: Total number of individual transaction lines reported on the bank statement for the period. Used for completeness validation during import and reconciliation to ensure all lines are processed.
reference: ISO 20022 camt.053; SAP S/4HANA Bank Statement Import

description: The monetary difference between the bank statement closing balance and the corresponding GL cash account balance that remains unmatched after automated reconciliation. A non-zero value triggers exception handling and investigation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP/IFRS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each corporate bank account record in the treasury master data. Primary key for the bank_account data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) — Bank Account Master

description: Reference to the GL clearing account in the chart of accounts that this bank account maps to for automated bank reconciliation and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger (FI-GL); SOX Section 404

description: Reference to the owning legal entity (company code) that holds this bank account. Used for multi-entity financial consolidation and SOX entity-level reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Company Code; SOX Section 302/404

description: SAP S/4HANA account ID within the house bank (e.g., 'CHK01', 'SAV01'). Together with house_bank_id, uniquely identifies the bank account in SAP payment programs and electronic bank statement processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Accounting (FI-BL) Account ID Configuration

description: Descriptive name assigned to the bank account for internal identification and treasury reporting (e.g., 'Ecommerce US Operating Account - JPMorgan'). Used in cash position dashboards and SAP S/4HANA bank account master.

Masked representation of the bank account number showing only the last 4 digits (e.g., XXXXXXXX1234). Full account number is stored in a separate secrets vault per PCI DSS requirements. Used for display, reconciliation matching, and audit trail.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3; ISO 27001 A.8.2

description: Free-text description of the specific business purpose of this account (e.g., 'Marketplace seller escrow disbursements - APAC region', 'US payroll funding account'). Provides operational context beyond the account_type classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Treasury and Risk Management (TRM)

description: Current lifecycle status of the bank account. Active: fully operational. Inactive: temporarily not in use. Frozen: blocked by bank or regulatory order. Closed: permanently deactivated. Pending_activation: account opened but not yet confirmed by bank.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Account Management (BAM); SOX Section 302 Internal Controls

description: Functional classification of the bank account within the corporate treasury structure. Operating: day-to-day business transactions. Payroll: employee salary disbursements. Escrow: held funds for marketplace seller settlements. Concentration: cash pooling/sweeping. Disbursement: outbound payments only. Collection: inbound receipts only. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating|payroll|escrow|concentration|disbursement|collection — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA Treasury and Risk Management (TRM); SOX Section 404 Cash Controls

description: Full street address of the bank branch. Used for payment instructions, correspondence, and regulatory filings. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Master Data (FI-BL); ISO 27001 A.8.2

description: Name of the specific bank branch where the account is held (e.g., 'New York Midtown Branch'). Used for correspondence, wire transfer instructions, and bank relationship management.

Email address of the primary bank relationship manager or contact for this account. Used for electronic correspondence, statement delivery notifications, and escalation. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Master Data (FI-BL); GDPR Article 4

description: Name of the primary relationship manager or contact person at the bank for this account. Used for escalation, issue resolution, and bank relationship management.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the bank branch is domiciled (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives currency controls, regulatory reporting jurisdiction, and cross-border payment rules.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WTO International Trade Regulations; GDPR Article 44 (cross-border transfers)

description: Full legal name of the financial institution holding this account (e.g., JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.). Used for treasury reporting, bank relationship management, and counterparty risk assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Master Data (FI-BL); ISO 9362

description: Internal GL book balance for this bank account as recorded in SAP S/4HANA general ledger, in the account's base currency. Compared against current_balance during bank reconciliation to identify timing differences and unrecorded items.
reference: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger (FI-GL); SOX Section 404 Reconciliation Controls

description: Date on which the bank account was officially closed. Null for active accounts. Used for account lifecycle management, historical reporting, and SOX audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Account Management (BAM); SOX Section 404

description: Four-character SAP company code identifying the legal entity in SAP S/4HANA for financial consolidation, GL posting, and regulatory reporting. Aligns with the owning legal entity for SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Configuration; SOX Section 302/404

description: Timestamp when this bank account master record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX record-keeping requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail; ISO 27001 A.12.4 Logging

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account's primary operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the base currency for all statement balances, transaction amounts, and FX conversion in treasury reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Multi-Currency Accounting

description: Most recent confirmed closing balance of the bank account as reported on the latest electronic bank statement, in the account's base currency. Used for daily cash position reporting and liquidity forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Cash Management (TR-CM); SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether this bank account requires dual authorization (two-person rule) for payment initiation per treasury policy and SOX segregation of duties controls. True: dual approval mandatory. False: single authorization permitted.
reference: SOX Section 404 Segregation of Duties; NIST SP 800-53 AC-5

description: SAP S/4HANA house bank identifier that groups bank accounts under a single banking relationship. Used for payment program configuration, bank statement import, and electronic bank reconciliation in SAP FI-BL.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Accounting (FI-BL) House Bank Configuration

description: International Bank Account Number (IBAN) assigned to this account per ISO 13616 standard. Used for cross-border payment instructions, SEPA transactions, and international treasury operations. Nullable for non-IBAN jurisdictions.
reference: ISO 13616 IBAN Standard; SEPA Credit Transfer Scheme; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3

description: Annual interest rate (as a decimal percentage, e.g., 0.0425 = 4.25%) applicable to this bank account for credit balances or overdraft facilities. Used in treasury yield analysis and cost-of-funds reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) — Interest Rate Management

description: Indicates whether this account serves as the header/master account in a cash concentration or notional pooling structure. True: this is the concentration account that receives sweeps from subsidiary accounts. False: standard account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Cash Management (TR-CM) — Cash Pooling

description: Date on which the most recent successful automated bank reconciliation was completed for this account in SAP S/4HANA. Used for cash position accuracy monitoring and SOX reconciliation controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation (FI-BL); SOX Section 404 Reconciliation Controls

description: Date of the most recently received and processed electronic bank statement (EBS) for this account. Used to monitor statement receipt timeliness and identify gaps in automated bank reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) Processing (FI-BL); SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this bank account master record. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and SOX audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail; ISO 27001 A.12.4 Logging

description: Date on which the bank account was officially opened and activated by the financial institution. Used for account tenure analysis, regulatory reporting, and audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Account Management (BAM); SOX Section 404

description: Maximum overdraft facility amount authorized by the bank for this account, in the account's base currency. Used in daily cash position reporting and liquidity forecasting to determine available credit headroom.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) — Liquidity Management

description: Maximum amount in the account's base currency that can be authorized for a single payment transaction from this account without escalated approval. Enforces treasury payment controls and SOX segregation of duties.
reference: SOX Section 404 Payment Controls; SAP S/4HANA Authorization Management

description: Comma-separated list of SAP payment method codes authorized for use with this bank account (e.g., 'T,U,C' for wire transfer, ACH, check). Controls which payment programs can use this account in SAP automatic payment runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Automatic Payment Program (F110); NACHA ACH Rules

description: Current reconciliation status of the bank account as of the last statement processing cycle. Reconciled: all items matched. Unreconciled: outstanding items exist. In_progress: reconciliation run is active. Exception: unresolvable discrepancies requiring manual review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Bank Reconciliation (FI-BL); SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: ABA routing transit number (9-digit) identifying the US financial institution for domestic ACH and wire transfers. Used in payment processing and settlement runs. Applicable for US-domiciled accounts only.
reference: ABA Routing Number Standard; NACHA ACH Operating Rules

description: Comma-separated list of names of individuals authorized to sign on this bank account per the bank mandate. Used for treasury governance, audit, and SOX segregation of duties controls.
reference: SOX Section 302/404 Internal Controls; ISO 27001 A.9.2 Access Management

description: Originating system from which this bank account master record was sourced. SAP_S4HANA: extracted from SAP S/4HANA Bank Account Management. MANUAL: manually entered by treasury team. BANK_PORTAL: imported from bank's online portal.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Integration Framework; ETL Data Lineage Standards

description: Method by which electronic bank statements are delivered from the bank to the ERP system. SWIFT: via SWIFT network. SFTP: secure file transfer. API: direct bank API integration. Email: email attachment. Manual: manual upload.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) Import; SWIFT gpi

description: Format of the electronic bank statement (EBS) received from the bank for automated import into SAP S/4HANA. MT940: SWIFT legacy format. CAMT053: ISO 20022 XML format. BAI2: US cash management format. OFX: Open Financial Exchange. CSV: flat file.
reference: ISO 20022 CAMT.053; SWIFT MT940; BAI2 Cash Management Standard; SAP S/4HANA EBS Configuration

description: SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank and branch for international wire transfers and correspondent banking. Conforms to ISO 9362 standard. Required for cross-border payment routing in the Transportation Management System and ERP payment runs.
reference: ISO 9362 BIC Standard; SWIFT gpi; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: Desired minimum or target cash balance for this account as set by treasury policy. Used in cash concentration/sweeping rules and liquidity forecasting to trigger inter-account transfers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Cash Management (TR-CM); NIST SP 800-53 Financial Controls

description: Unique surrogate primary key for each GMV-to-net-revenue reconciliation record in the finance domain. Serves as the system-of-record identifier for financial close, SOX audit trails, and investor reporting.
reference: SOX Section 302 & 404 — Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: Primary key for gmv_reconciliation
Reference to the SAP S/4HANA cost center responsible for this reconciliation record, enabling P&L attribution, budget tracking, and internal financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) — Cost Center Accounting; SOX Section 404

description: Foreign key linking to search.indexed_document. Business justification: GMV reconciliation per product listing requires linking each reconciliation record to the indexed_document representing that SKU.
Reference to the fiscal period (month/quarter/year) to which this reconciliation record belongs. Drives financial close scheduling, MRR/ARR roll-ups, and period-over-period variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) — Fiscal Period; SOX Section 404

description: Reference to the marketplace seller whose transactions are included in this reconciliation record. Enables seller-level GMV attribution, commission calculation, and settlement reporting via the Seller Management Portal.
reference: Seller Management Portal — Seller Performance Monitoring; FTC Marketplace Transparency Guidelines

description: Name or employee identifier of the finance controller who approved this reconciliation record during the financial close workflow. Distinct from SOX attestation — this is the operational approval step.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls; SAP S/4HANA FI — Approval Workflow

description: Date and time when SOX attestation was formally recorded for this reconciliation record. Provides the precise audit trail timestamp required for regulatory compliance and external auditor review.
Name or employee identifier of the finance controller or CFO who provided SOX attestation for this reconciliation record. Captured as part of the audit trail for regulatory compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302 — CEO/CFO Certification; PCAOB AS 2201

description: Total value of orders cancelled within the reconciliation period, deducted from gross GMV to arrive at net GMV. Sourced from the Order Management System order lifecycle tracking.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Variable Consideration; SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Postings

description: The sales channel through which the GMV was generated: 'web' (desktop/browser), 'mobile_app', 'bopis' (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), 'api' (API-driven B2B orders), 'edl' (Electronic Data Interchange). Supports channel-level revenue attribution and reporting.
reference: Order Management System — Order Capture; GAAP ASC 606

description: System timestamp when this GMV reconciliation record was first created in the data platform. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail; ISO 27001 — Information Security Management

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary amounts on this reconciliation record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations and FX reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; GAAP ASC 830 Foreign Currency Matters

description: The exchange rate applied to convert transaction amounts from the transaction currency to the reporting currency (USD) for this reconciliation period. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI currency translation tables.
reference: GAAP ASC 830 — Foreign Currency Matters; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Translation

description: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger account code to which the recognized net revenue is posted. Enables drill-through from financial statements to reconciliation detail and supports SOX audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL — Chart of Accounts; SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls

description: The date on which the reconciliation amounts were posted to the SAP S/4HANA General Ledger. Determines the accounting period for revenue recognition and is critical for period-end cut-off compliance.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition Timing; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; SOX Section 404

description: Total face value of all orders placed within the reconciliation period before any deductions. Represents the top-line GMV figure used in investor reporting, KPI dashboards, and GMV-to-revenue bridge calculations. Sourced from the Order Management System.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; SOX Section 302 — Revenue Attestation; FTC Advertising Standards

description: Indicates whether this reconciliation record has received formal SOX-compliant management attestation (True) or is pending attestation (False). Required for financial close gate and external audit evidence packages.
reference: SOX Section 302 & 404 — CEO/CFO Certification; PCAOB Auditing Standards

description: SAP S/4HANA accounting document number for the journal entry posting this reconciliation to the General Ledger. Provides the audit trail link between the reconciliation record and the posted GL entry for SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — Accounting Document; SOX Section 302 & 404

description: Total last-mile delivery and fulfillment costs incurred within the reconciliation period, including 3PL charges, carrier fees, and FBA-style fulfillment costs. Sourced from the Transportation Management System and SAP S/4HANA AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Materials Management (MM) — Logistics Invoice Verification; SOX Section 404

description: Business model segment classification for this reconciliation record: 'b2c' (Business to Consumer), 'b2b' (Business to Business), 'c2c' (Consumer to Consumer), or 'dtc' (Direct to Consumer). Enables segment-level revenue reporting and investor disclosures.
reference: FTC Marketplace Transparency; GAAP ASC 606 — Contract Identification

description: The monetary materiality threshold applied to this reconciliation record for SOX purposes. Variances exceeding this amount require escalation, formal explanation, and controller sign-off before the period can be closed.
reference: SOX Section 302 & 404; PCAOB AS 2101 — Audit Planning Materiality

description: Gross GMV less cancelled orders and returns/refunds. Represents the value of successfully completed transactions before revenue deductions. Key intermediate figure in the GMV-to-revenue bridge used in investor reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; SOX Section 302 — Revenue Attestation

description: Free-text field for finance team annotations, adjustment explanations, or audit comments associated with this reconciliation record. Supports the financial close review process and provides context for auditors.
reference: SOX Section 302 — Management Commentary; PCAOB Auditing Standards

description: Total number of orders included in the gross GMV figure for this reconciliation record. Supports Average Order Value (AOV) calculation and volume-based variance analysis during financial close.
reference: Order Management System — Order Lifecycle Tracking; GAAP ASC 606

description: Total fees charged by payment processors and card networks (e.g., interchange, gateway fees) within the reconciliation period. Deducted from gross revenue to arrive at net revenue. Sourced from the Payment Gateway and Processing Platform.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 — Payment Data Security; GAAP ASC 606 — Transaction Costs

description: The last calendar date of the reporting period covered by this reconciliation record. Defines the GMV measurement window close boundary for financial reporting and audit.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition; SOX Section 404

description: The first calendar date of the reporting period covered by this reconciliation record. Used to define the GMV measurement window for financial close and revenue recognition.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition; SOX Section 404

description: Total fixed and variable platform service fees charged to sellers or buyers within the reconciliation period (e.g., listing fees, fulfillment fees, subscription fees). Distinct from commission-based revenue. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA AR.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Distinct Performance Obligations; SOX Section 404

description: Total value of promotional discounts, coupons, and price reductions applied to orders within the reconciliation period. Deducted from gross GMV in the revenue bridge. Sourced from the Marketing Automation Platform and Order Management System.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Variable Consideration; GAAP ASC 340-40 Contract Costs

description: Final net revenue recognized for the period after all deductions: net GMV minus promotional discounts, minus payment processing fees, minus logistics costs, plus platform fees and seller commissions earned. Posted to the SAP S/4HANA General Ledger as the period revenue figure for SOX-compliant financial reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Revenue Recognition; SOX Section 302 & 404; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL

business_glossary_term: Recognized Net Revenue in Reporting Currency
description: Recognized net revenue amount translated into the reporting currency using the period fx_rate. Used for consolidated financial statements, investor reporting, and MRR/ARR calculations in the reporting currency.

Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this reconciliation record, used in financial close packages, investor reports, and SOX attestation documentation. Format: REC-YYYY-MM-XXXXXXXX.
reference: SOX Section 302 & 404; SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) Document Number convention

description: Current lifecycle state of the GMV reconciliation record within the financial close workflow. 'draft' = initial capture; 'in_review' = under finance team review; 'approved' = controller sign-off obtained; 'posted' = posted to general ledger in SAP S/4HANA; 'disputed' = variance flagged for investigation; 'closed' = period locked.
reference: SOX Section 302 & 404 — Financial Close Controls; SAP S/4HANA FI Document Lifecycle

description: Classification of the reconciliation cadence: 'monthly' for standard MRR close, 'quarterly' for investor reporting, 'annual' for ARR and audited financials, 'ad_hoc' for out-of-cycle adjustments or restatements.
reference: SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 code for the functional/reporting currency to which all amounts are translated for consolidated financial reporting (e.g., USD). Distinct from currency_code which reflects the transaction currency.
reference: ISO 4217; GAAP ASC 830 — Foreign Currency Matters

description: Total number of Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) orders processed within the reconciliation period. Used to calculate return rate and validate the returns_refunds_amount deduction.
reference: Order Management System — RMA Processing; FTC Consumer Protection

description: Total monetary value of approved returns and refunds processed via the Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) workflow within the period. Deducted from gross GMV in the revenue bridge. Sourced from the Order Management System and Payment Gateway.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Right of Return; FTC Consumer Protection — Refund Policies

description: The GAAP ASC 606 revenue recognition method applied to this reconciliation record: 'point_in_time' for standard product sales, 'over_time' for subscription or service revenue, 'milestone_based' for project-based arrangements.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Step 5: Satisfy Performance Obligations; SOX Section 302

description: Total marketplace commission fees earned from sellers on transactions within the reconciliation period. Represents platform revenue earned on GMV and is a key component of the GMV-to-net-revenue bridge. Sourced from the Seller Management Portal.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Principal vs. Agent Considerations; FTC Marketplace Transparency

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this reconciliation record was sourced or initiated: 'SAP_S4HANA' (ERP financial consolidation), 'OMS' (Order Management System), 'PAYMENT_GATEWAY' (Payment Gateway and Processing Platform), 'SELLER_PORTAL' (Seller Management Portal), 'MANUAL' (manual finance entry). Supports data lineage and ETL audit trails.
reference: ISO 27001 — Data Provenance; SOX Section 404 — Data Lineage

description: Total sales tax, VAT, or GST collected from buyers on orders within the reconciliation period. Excluded from net revenue recognition as a pass-through liability. Sourced from the Payment Gateway and SAP S/4HANA Tax module.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Taxes Collected from Customers; GDPR Article 5 (data minimization for tax records); SOX Section 404

description: Total tax amounts remitted to tax authorities within the reconciliation period. Reconciled against tax_collected_amount to identify timing differences and tax liability balances in SAP S/4HANA AP.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA Tax Compliance; WTO International Trade Regulations

description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to this GMV reconciliation record. Tracks changes during the financial close review cycle and supports SOX change audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail; ISO 27001 — Information Security Management

description: Monetary difference between the expected net revenue (as per budget or prior period) and the actual recognized net revenue for this reconciliation record. A non-zero variance triggers investigation and may require SOX-compliant explanatory notes.
reference: SOX Section 302 & 404 — Variance Analysis; SAP S/4HANA CO — Variance Reporting

description: Free-text narrative explaining the root cause of any material reconciliation variance. Required for SOX attestation when variance_amount exceeds the materiality threshold. Captured by the finance controller during the review workflow.
reference: SOX Section 302 & 404 — Management Attestation; PCAOB Auditing Standards

description: Total value of platform-funded vouchers and coupon redemptions applied within the reconciliation period. Tracked separately from seller-funded promotional discounts for accurate P&L attribution and marketing spend reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 — Variable Consideration; Marketing Automation Platform — Salesforce Marketing Cloud

description: Primary key for payment_batch
Self-referencing FK on payment_batch (consolidated_from_payment_batch_id)
Identifier of the party (e.g., merchant or internal finance unit) associated with the batch.
Approval outcome for the batch.
External business identifier assigned to the payment batch.
Classification of the payment batch purpose.
Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the batch amounts.
Free-text description or notes about the payment batch.
Code representing any processing error encountered for the batch.
Timestamp when the payment batch was initiated.
Aggregate fees or adjustments applied to the batch.
Indicates whether the batch was generated automatically by the system.
Net amount after fees, representing the amount to be settled.
Channel through which the batch payments were submitted.
Payment instrument used for the batch transactions.
Timestamp when the batch processing completed.
Number of retry attempts performed for failed batch processing.
Planned date for the batch processing.
Timestamp when the batch was settled with the financial institution.
Name of the source system that originated the batch record.
Current lifecycle status of the payment batch.
Total gross monetary amount of all payments in the batch before adjustments.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.
Primary key for seller_disbursement
Unique system-generated primary key identifying each seller disbursement record in the marketplace accounts payable ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; SOX Section 404

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Seller payouts must comply with tax‑withholding and payment‑security regulations, captured as a compliance obligation.
Reference to the settlement execution record in the Payment Gateway and Processing Platform that executed this disbursement transaction.
reference: Payment Gateway and Processing Platform; PCI DSS

description: Reference to the marketplace seller or third-party merchant receiving this disbursement. Links to the seller master record in the Seller Management Portal.
reference: Seller Management Portal; SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master

description: The actual calendar date on which the disbursement was successfully transferred to the seller's bank account. May differ from scheduled date due to banking delays, holidays, or processing issues. Used for SLA compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; ISO 20022; SOX Section 404

description: Net monetary adjustment applied to the disbursement for corrections, promotional credits, dispute resolutions, or other one-time modifications. Can be positive (credit to seller) or negative (debit from seller).
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; SOX Section 404

description: Approval workflow status for this disbursement. SOX-compliant disbursements above defined thresholds require explicit approval before processing. Tracks whether the disbursement has been reviewed and authorized.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA AP Workflow; Internal Financial Controls

description: Employee ID or system username of the finance team member who approved this disbursement. Required for SOX-compliant dual-control authorization and audit trail. Null for auto-approved disbursements below threshold.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA AP Workflow; NIST Cybersecurity Framework

description: Timestamp when the disbursement was formally approved by an authorized finance team member. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail for financial controls.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA AP Workflow

description: Last four digits of the seller's bank account number to which the disbursement was sent. Stored as a masked reference for reconciliation and audit purposes without exposing full account details per PCI DSS requirements.
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 3; PCI SSC; SOX Section 404

description: Bank routing number, SWIFT/BIC code, or IBAN prefix identifying the destination financial institution for the disbursement. Used for payment routing and reconciliation with the payment gateway.
reference: ISO 9362 SWIFT/BIC; PCI DSS; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Total marketplace commission fees deducted from the gross disbursement amount. Represents the platform's revenue share withheld from the seller payout per the seller agreement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; Seller Management Portal; GAAP ASC 606

description: SAP S/4HANA cost center code associated with this disbursement for internal financial reporting, budget tracking, and departmental cost allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Controlling Module; SOX Section 404; GAAP

description: Audit timestamp when this disbursement record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, SOX audit trails, and reconciliation.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this disbursement record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency marketplace operations and cross-border seller payouts.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; WTO International Trade Regulations; GDPR

description: Externally-visible alphanumeric reference code assigned to this disbursement, used in seller-facing remittance advice and reconciliation communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; ISO 20022 Payment Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the disbursement. Tracks progression from pending approval through processing to completed settlement or failure/reversal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|processing|completed|failed|reversed|on_hold — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; SOX Section 404; ISO 20022

description: Classification of the disbursement by payout mechanism or trigger. Standard disbursements follow the regular settlement cycle; accelerated are expedited payouts; manual are operator-initiated; adjustment corrects prior disbursements; reversal claws back funds; advance is a pre-settlement payout. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|accelerated|manual|adjustment|reversal|advance — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; Seller Management Portal

description: Descriptive reason code or message explaining why a disbursement failed or was placed on hold (e.g., invalid bank account, insufficient funds, compliance hold, fraud flag). Null for successful disbursements.
reference: Payment Gateway and Processing Platform; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Total platform service fees deducted from the gross disbursement, including fulfillment fees, storage fees, listing fees, and other operational charges billed to the seller.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; Seller Management Portal

description: The date on which the foreign exchange rate was fixed for currency conversion in this disbursement. Used for FX gain/loss accounting and cross-border financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA Treasury Module; GAAP ASC 830

description: Exchange rate applied to convert the disbursement amount from the marketplace transaction currency to the seller's payout currency. Applicable for cross-border sellers operating in multiple currencies. Null if no conversion was required.
reference: ISO 4217; WTO International Trade Regulations; SAP S/4HANA Treasury Module

description: SAP S/4HANA General Ledger account code to which this disbursement is posted. Enables financial consolidation, P&L reporting, and SOX-compliant audit trails within the enterprise ERP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA GL Module; SOX Section 404; GAAP

description: Total Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) of all orders settled in this disbursement period, representing the full customer-facing transaction value before platform deductions. Used for GMV-to-revenue reconciliation and marketplace financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance Module; GAAP ASC 606; SOX Section 404

description: Total gross amount owed to the seller before any deductions, representing the sum of all settled order revenues attributed to the seller within the settlement period. Core component of the monetary triplet for AP reconciliation and GMV-to-revenue reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP/AR Module; SOX Section 404; GAAP Revenue Recognition ASC 606

description: The real-world business event timestamp when the disbursement was initiated and entered the payment queue. Distinct from system audit timestamps. Used for SLA measurement and settlement cycle reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; SOX Section 404

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the marketplace region in which the seller's sales were generated. Drives tax withholding rules, currency conversion, and regulatory reporting requirements.
reference: ISO 3166-1; WTO International Trade Regulations; GDPR; CCPA

description: Final net amount actually disbursed to the seller after all deductions (commission, fees, tax withholding, refunds, adjustments). This is the actual payout amount transferred to the seller's bank account. Core net-total component of the monetary triplet.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 606; ISO 20022

description: Net disbursement amount expressed in the seller's payout currency after FX conversion. Equals net_disbursement_amount multiplied by fx_conversion_rate. Null if no currency conversion was applied.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA Treasury Module; GAAP ASC 830

description: Free-text field for finance team annotations, special handling instructions, or contextual information about this disbursement. Used for manual disbursements, adjustments, and exception handling documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Reason code explaining why a disbursement has been placed on hold (e.g., seller account under review, tax documentation pending, dispute investigation, compliance verification). Null when disbursement is not on hold.
reference: Seller Management Portal; FTC Consumer Protection; GDPR; CCPA

description: Number of individual customer orders included in this disbursement's settlement period. Used for reconciliation, seller performance reporting, and GMV-to-disbursement ratio analysis.
reference: Order Management System; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: The financial instrument or channel used to transfer funds to the seller. Determines the settlement rail and associated processing timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bank_transfer|payoneer|paypal|check|wire_transfer|virtual_account — promote to reference product if additional methods are needed]
reference: Payment Gateway and Processing Platform; PCI DSS; ISO 20022

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the currency in which the seller actually receives the disbursement. May differ from currency_code when FX conversion is applied for cross-border sellers.
reference: ISO 4217; WTO International Trade Regulations; SAP S/4HANA Treasury Module

description: Total amount deducted from the disbursement to recover costs associated with customer refunds and returns (RMA) processed during the settlement period and charged back to the seller.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; Order Management System RMA Processing

description: Indicates whether a remittance advice notification has been sent to the seller detailing the disbursement breakdown (gross amount, deductions, net payout). Supports seller reconciliation and dispute prevention.
reference: Seller Management Portal; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Timestamp when the remittance advice notification was dispatched to the seller. Used for seller communication tracking and SLA compliance measurement.
reference: Seller Management Portal; SAP S/4HANA AP Module

description: Reference to the original disbursement_reference that this record reverses or corrects. Populated only for disbursements of type 'reversal' or 'adjustment'. Enables full audit trail of disbursement corrections.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; SOX Section 404

description: The planned calendar date on which the disbursement is scheduled to be transferred to the seller's bank account. Used for cash flow planning and seller expectation management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; Seller Management Portal

description: Legal entity classification of the seller receiving the disbursement. Determines applicable tax withholding rates, regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., IRS 1099-K thresholds), and KYC/AML compliance requirements.
reference: FTC; IRS Form 1099-K; GDPR; CCPA; Seller Management Portal

description: The end date of the settlement period covered by this disbursement. Defines the closing boundary of the window during which seller sales transactions are aggregated for payout calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; Seller Management Portal; GAAP ASC 606

description: The start date of the settlement period covered by this disbursement. Defines the beginning of the window during which seller sales transactions are aggregated for payout calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; Seller Management Portal; GAAP ASC 606

description: Type of tax reporting form associated with this disbursement for regulatory compliance. Determines the applicable tax reporting obligation based on seller entity type and jurisdiction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1099-K|1042-S|W-9|W-8BEN|VAT|GST|none — promote to reference product if additional form types are needed]
reference: IRS Form 1099-K; IRS Form 1042-S; EU VAT Directive; FTC; WTO

description: Amount of tax withheld from the seller disbursement in compliance with applicable tax regulations (e.g., VAT, GST, income tax withholding for cross-border sellers). Reported to tax authorities as required.
reference: FTC; GDPR; WTO International Trade Regulations; IRS Form 1099-K; EU VAT Directive

description: External transaction reference number returned by the bank or payment processor confirming the fund transfer. Used for bank reconciliation, dispute resolution, and audit trail in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AP Module; ISO 20022; SOX Section 404

description: Audit timestamp when this disbursement record was last modified. Tracks status changes, corrections, and reversals for SOX compliance and audit trail integrity.

Unique surrogate identifier for each accounts payable record in the AP subledger. Primary key for the accounts_payable data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete AP invoice or payable obligation with a full lifecycle.
Reference to the cost center responsible for this payable obligation. Used for internal cost allocation, budget tracking, and financial reporting.

Identifier of the automated payment run batch in which this invoice was included for payment processing. Corresponds to the SAP F110 payment run identifier. Used for payment reconciliation, bank statement matching, and audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP F110 Payment Run; SOX Section 302/404; PCI DSS

description: Reference to the originating purchase order that generated this payable obligation. Used for three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) validation and PO settlement tracking.
Reference to the vendor or supplier master record associated with this payable obligation. Links to the vendor/supplier master entity for counterparty resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Vendor Master (LFA1); ISO 27001

description: Aging classification of the outstanding payable based on the number of days past the invoice date or due date. Used for AP aging reports, cash flow forecasting, vendor relationship management, and SOX financial disclosure. Recalculated daily during Silver layer processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Aging Report (S_ALR_87012085); GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404

description: Current lifecycle status of the AP record within the payment workflow. Drives aging analysis, payment run eligibility, and cash flow forecasting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|pending_approval|approved|scheduled|paid|disputed|cancelled — promote to reference product]

The current approval workflow status of the invoice within the AP approval chain. Invoices must be approved before payment release. Supports segregation of duties controls required for SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Workflow; SOX Section 302/404; COSO Internal Controls Framework

description: The username or employee ID of the individual who approved the invoice for payment. Required for SOX segregation of duties audit trail and internal controls documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Workflow; SOX Section 302/404; COSO Internal Controls Framework

description: The date and time when the invoice was approved for payment processing. Part of the SOX-required audit trail for AP internal controls. Distinct from posting date and payment date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Workflow; SOX Section 302/404; COSO Internal Controls Framework

description: The net payable amount converted to the company code base currency (typically USD) using the exchange rate at posting date. Required for consolidated financial reporting, GL posting, and multi-currency AP subledger reconciliation. Corresponds to DMBTR in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (DMBTR); GAAP ASC 830; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 4217

description: The SAP company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit responsible for this payable obligation. Required for multi-entity financial consolidation, intercompany elimination, and entity-level SOX reporting. Corresponds to BUKRS in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BUKRS); SOX Section 302/404; GAAP ASC 810

description: Timestamp when the AP record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking. Corresponds to CPUDT/CPUTM in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 27001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the invoice amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP management, foreign exchange revaluation, and cross-border vendor payment processing.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (WAERS); PCI DSS; GDPR

description: Number of calendar days the invoice has exceeded its payment due date. Negative values indicate days until due. Zero indicates due today. Used for AP aging analysis, vendor penalty calculations, and cash flow management. Recalculated daily.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404

description: The cash discount amount available if payment is made within the early payment discount window defined in the payment terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Supports working capital optimization and dynamic discounting programs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (SKFBT); GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404

description: The last date by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount. Calculated from invoice date plus discount period days in the payment terms. Drives dynamic discounting and working capital optimization decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (SKFDT); GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404

description: The reason code for placing an invoice in disputed status. Populated when ap_status is 'disputed'. Drives dispute resolution workflows, vendor communication, and root cause analysis for AP exception management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Dispute Management; FTC Consumer Protection; SOX Section 302/404

description: The date by which payment must be made to the vendor to satisfy the invoice obligation and comply with agreed payment terms. Calculated from invoice date plus payment term days. Critical for cash flow planning and avoiding late payment penalties.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (FAEDT); UCC Article 2; SOX Section 302/404

description: The exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency amount to the company code base currency at the time of posting. Required for foreign currency AP revaluation, FX gain/loss reporting, and multi-currency reconciliation. Corresponds to KURSF in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (KURSF); GAAP ASC 830; SOX Section 302/404; ISO 4217

description: The fiscal posting period (01–12 for regular periods, 13–16 for special closing periods) within the fiscal year. Used for period-end close, monthly AP subledger reconciliation, and financial reporting. Corresponds to MONAT in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (MONAT); GAAP; SOX Section 302/404

description: The fiscal year in which this AP document is posted. Used for period-end close, financial reporting, and year-over-year AP analysis. Corresponds to GJAHR in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (GJAHR); GAAP; SOX Section 302/404

description: The goods receipt document number from the Warehouse Management System confirming physical receipt of goods or services associated with this invoice. Required for three-way match validation. Corresponds to LFBNR in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-GR (MIGO); WMS; SOX Section 302/404

description: The total gross amount of the vendor invoice before any deductions, discounts, or adjustments. Represents the full obligation as stated on the vendor invoice. Used for GMV-to-revenue reconciliation and AP aging analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (WRBTR); GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404

description: The date printed on the vendor invoice document. Serves as the principal business event date for the payable obligation and is the baseline for payment term calculations and aging bucket assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BLDAT); GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404

description: The externally-assigned invoice number provided by the vendor or supplier on the source invoice document. Used as the primary business identifier for matching, dispute resolution, and audit trails. Corresponds to BELNR in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BELNR); SOX Section 302/404; FTC Invoice Standards

description: Classification of the AP document type. Standard invoices represent normal vendor payables; credit memos reduce outstanding balances; debit memos increase obligations; recurring invoices are for subscription/SaaS vendors; prepayments are advance payments; intercompany invoices are for internal entity transactions. Corresponds to BLART in SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BLART); GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404

description: Indicates whether this invoice is part of a recurring payment schedule (e.g., SaaS subscriptions, lease payments, maintenance contracts). Recurring invoices are auto-generated by SAP recurring entry programs. Used for MRR/ARR tracking and cash flow forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Recurring Entries (FBD1); GAAP ASC 606; SOX Section 302/404

description: The net amount payable to the vendor after deducting applicable discounts, credits, and adjustments from the gross invoice amount. Represents the actual cash outflow obligation. Used for payment run execution and cash flow forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 302/404
