description: Maximum resolution time in hours guaranteed by SLA for case closure or escalation.

business_glossary_term: Response Time Service Level Agreement (SLA) Hours
description: Maximum response time in hours guaranteed by SLA for initial agent response to customer inquiry.
Number of days after delivery during which the customer can initiate a return under this entitlement (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).
Indicates whether RMA requests under this entitlement are automatically approved without manual review.
Level of access to self-service tools, knowledge base, and automated resolution features (basic, advanced, premium, full).

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Document Uniform Resource Locator (URL)
description: URL link to the detailed SLA document specifying response times, resolution commitments, and service guarantees.
Service support tier level defining the quality and speed of support (basic, priority, premium, dedicated, enterprise).
Indicates whether technical product support (setup, troubleshooting, configuration) is included in this entitlement.

business_glossary_term: Terms and Conditions Uniform Resource Locator (URL)
description: URL link to the full terms and conditions document governing this entitlement tier.
Number of days of free trial period offered for this entitlement tier. Null indicates no trial available.
Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this entitlement record.
Timestamp when this entitlement record was last modified.
Version number of this entitlement configuration, incremented with each modification to track change history.
Number of additional warranty months provided beyond standard manufacturer warranty under this entitlement. Null indicates no extension.
Unique identifier for the escalation event. Primary key.
Reference to the customer associated with the escalated case, enabling customer-centric escalation pattern analysis.
Reference to the support agent who initiated or triggered the escalation.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Escalations triggered by compliance breaches must reference the underlying compliance_obligation to track remediation and reporting.
Reference to the support agent who received and accepted the escalated case.
Reference to the support team or queue to which the case was escalated.
Reference to the originating support case that was escalated.
The communication channel through which the escalation was initiated or communicated.
Monetary value of compensation issued to the customer as part of escalation resolution.
Boolean indicator of whether customer compensation (refund, credit, discount, gift) was issued as part of escalation resolution (True) or not (False).
The type of compensation provided to the customer (e.g., refund, account credit, discount code, gift card, free shipping, product replacement).
Estimated or actual cost associated with handling the escalation, including labor, compensation, and operational overhead.
The date and time when the escalation record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Post-resolution customer satisfaction score for the escalated case, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.
The customer segment or tier at the time of escalation, used for prioritization and SLA (Service Level Agreement) determination.
Human-readable business identifier for the escalation event, formatted as ESC-NNNNNNNN.
Current lifecycle status of the escalation event within the support workflow.
The date and time when the escalation event occurred, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Classification of the escalation mechanism: functional (to specialist team), hierarchical (to supervisor/manager), automatic (system-triggered), or manual (agent-initiated).
Free-text notes or comments provided by the escalating agent explaining the context, urgency, or special handling requirements for the escalation.
Net Promoter Score collected after escalation resolution, measuring customer loyalty on a scale of 0-10.
The priority level assigned to the escalation, indicating urgency and required response time.

The trigger or reason that caused the case to be escalated. Common reasons include SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach, customer demand, case complexity, VIP customer status, regulatory concern, or repeat issue.
reference: ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) Service Management Framework

description: Boolean indicator of whether the case was successfully resolved at the escalated tier (True) or required further escalation (False).
Standardized code indicating how the escalated case was resolved (e.g., refund issued, replacement shipped, technical fix applied).
The date and time when the escalated case was resolved, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The identified root cause category for the issue that led to escalation, used for continuous improvement and pattern analysis.
Boolean indicator of whether the escalation was triggered by an SLA breach (True) or not (False).

business_glossary_term: SLA (Service Level Agreement) Target Resolution Minutes
description: The target resolution time in minutes as defined by the applicable SLA for the escalated case.
The support tier or level from which the case was escalated.
The support tier or level to which the case was escalated.
The elapsed time in minutes from escalation timestamp to resolution timestamp, used for performance analysis and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: Customer Service KPI (Key Performance Indicator) Framework

description: The date and time when the escalation record was last modified, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Primary key for feedback_response
Reference to the customer who provided the feedback response. Links to the unified customer profile in the Customer Data Platform.
Reference to the order associated with this feedback, if the survey was triggered by an order-related service interaction or post-delivery survey.
Reference to the customer service agent who handled the case being evaluated. Used for agent performance tracking and coaching.
Reference to the service case or support ticket that triggered this feedback survey. Links the feedback to the specific service interaction being evaluated.

The category of the service case that triggered this feedback (e.g., order_inquiry, return_request, product_defect, delivery_issue, account_management, technical_support). Used to analyze satisfaction by case type and identify problem areas.

The total time in hours from case creation to case closure for the service case being evaluated. Captured at the time of survey to enable correlation analysis between resolution speed and customer satisfaction.

The channel through which the customer received service for the case being evaluated. Used to analyze satisfaction by service channel and optimize channel strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|email|chat|social_media|in-app|self-service|store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The categorical classification derived from the numeric score. For NPS: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), detractor (0-6). For CSAT: satisfied (4-5), neutral (3), dissatisfied (1-2). For CES: low_effort (6-7), high_effort (1-3). Used for segmentation and aggregate metrics calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promoter|passive|detractor|satisfied|neutral|dissatisfied|low_effort|high_effort — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Net Promoter Score (NPS) Methodology, Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) Standards

description: The specific reason the customer contacted support, as classified by the service team. More granular than case_category, capturing the detailed nature of the inquiry or issue (e.g., damaged_item, late_delivery, wrong_item_shipped, refund_status, promo_code_issue).
The date and time when this feedback response record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and tracking data freshness.
reference: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, Data Governance Standards

description: The customer segment classification at the time of feedback submission. Used to analyze satisfaction and loyalty metrics by customer cohort (e.g., VIP, frequent_buyer, new_customer, at_risk, dormant). Enables segment-specific experience improvement strategies.

The channel through which the survey was delivered to the customer. Email and SMS are common for post-interaction surveys, in-app and web for real-time feedback, IVR for phone-based surveys, chat for conversational feedback collection.
Boolean flag indicating whether the service case was escalated to a supervisor or specialist team. Escalated cases typically have different satisfaction patterns and require special analysis.
The unique identifier for this survey response in the external survey platform or CRM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and to link back to the source system for detailed survey metadata.

Boolean flag indicating whether the customer's issue was resolved on the first contact without requiring follow-up interactions. Key performance indicator (KPI) for service efficiency and a strong predictor of customer satisfaction.

Boolean flag indicating whether this feedback response requires follow-up action from the service team. Typically set to true for detractor responses, low CSAT scores, or when verbatim feedback indicates unresolved issues. Triggers service recovery workflows.
Boolean flag indicating whether the required follow-up action has been completed. Used to track closure of service recovery loops and ensure no customer feedback is left unaddressed.
The date and time when the follow-up action was completed. Used to measure time-to-resolution for service recovery and closed-loop feedback processes.
Boolean flag indicating whether an incentive (discount, reward points, gift card) was offered to encourage survey completion. Used to analyze response bias and response rate impact of incentives.
The total number of customer-agent interactions required to resolve the case being evaluated. Used to measure case complexity and correlate with effort scores and satisfaction ratings.

The numeric rating provided by the customer. For CSAT surveys this is typically 1-5 (1=very dissatisfied, 5=very satisfied). For NPS surveys this is 0-10 (0=not at all likely to recommend, 10=extremely likely). For CES surveys this is typically 1-7 (1=very difficult, 7=very easy).

The main topic or theme identified in the verbatim feedback through text analytics or manual coding. Examples include product_quality, delivery_speed, agent_professionalism, resolution_time, website_usability, pricing, return_process. Used for root cause analysis and improvement prioritization.
Type of device used by the customer to submit the feedback response. Used to analyze response patterns by device and optimize survey design for different form factors.

ISO 639-1 two-letter language code indicating the language in which the customer provided their feedback. Used for multilingual sentiment analysis and to route feedback to appropriate language-capable service teams.

Status of the survey response. Completed indicates all required questions were answered, partial indicates the survey was started but not finished, abandoned indicates the customer left mid-survey, expired indicates the survey link expired before response, bounced indicates the survey invitation was undeliverable.
The date and time when the customer submitted their feedback response. Used to calculate response time from survey delivery and to track feedback recency.
Additional topic or theme identified in the verbatim feedback when multiple themes are present. Captures the secondary concern or compliment expressed by the customer.

Categorical sentiment classification derived from sentiment score and verbatim text analysis. Positive indicates favorable feedback, negative indicates unfavorable feedback, neutral indicates balanced or no strong sentiment, mixed indicates conflicting sentiments within the same response.
reference: Text Analytics and Sentiment Analysis Standards

description: Machine-generated sentiment score derived from verbatim feedback text analysis. Typically ranges from -1.0 (very negative) to +1.0 (very positive), with 0 being neutral. Used to augment numeric ratings with natural language understanding.

Boolean flag indicating whether the service case was resolved within the committed SLA timeframe. Used to correlate SLA compliance with customer satisfaction and identify the impact of SLA breaches on customer experience.

Identifier of the source system that captured this feedback response (e.g., Qualtrics, Medallia, SurveyMonkey, Salesforce Service Cloud, custom feedback platform). Used for data lineage and multi-system integration tracking.
The date and time when the survey invitation was sent to the customer. Used to calculate response lag and survey completion rates.
The specific business event that triggered the delivery of this feedback survey. Identifies the touchpoint in the customer journey being measured.

Type of customer feedback survey administered. CSAT measures satisfaction with specific interactions (1-5 scale), NPS measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend (0-10 scale), CES measures ease of interaction (effort score). Post-Delivery surveys capture feedback after order fulfillment, Relationship surveys are periodic assessments, Post-Case surveys follow service case closure.
Version identifier of the survey template used. Enables tracking of survey design changes over time and ensures consistent analysis when survey questions or scales are modified.
reference: Survey Design and Version Control Standards

description: The date and time when this feedback response record was last modified. Tracks changes to follow-up status, theme classification, or other mutable fields.
reference: ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format, Data Governance Standards

description: Free-form text comments provided by the customer explaining their rating. This qualitative feedback provides context for the numeric score and is used for sentiment analysis, theme extraction, and identifying specific improvement opportunities. Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of customer-specific details.
reference: GDPR Article 4 - Personal Data, Text Analytics Standards

description: Unique identifier for the knowledge base article. Primary key.
Identifier of the primary product category this article applies to. Links article to relevant product catalog segments.

Foreign key linking to search.index. Business justification: Associates each knowledge article with the search index it resides in, required for index management, re‑indexing, and search performance reporting.

Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: Knowledge Base creation reuses existing content items; linking provides traceability and version sync between KB articles and source content.

Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Knowledge‑base articles often address issues specific to a marketplace listing; the link enables article‑to‑listing relevance reporting.
Identifier of the customer service agent or knowledge manager who authored the article.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Knowledge articles that address regulatory guidance need to be tied to the applicable regulation to ensure content validity and auditability.
Identifier of the agent or manager who reviewed and approved the article for publication.
Number of times agents have referenced or attached this article during case resolution. Indicates agent knowledge utilization.
Externally-visible unique article number used for reference and citation in support interactions. Format: KA-XXXXXXXX.
Classification of the article content type. Determines presentation format and usage context.
Comma-separated list of URLs pointing to supplementary attachments such as PDFs, videos, screenshots, or downloadable resources stored in Content Delivery Network (CDN).
Number of support cases attributed as deflected by this article through self-service resolution. Measured via customer journey tracking.
Comma-separated list of case types this article is designed to resolve. Links article to specific support case categories.
Rich text content body of the article containing the full resolution steps, policy details, or instructional content. May include HTML formatting, images, and embedded media.
Timestamp when the article record was first created in the knowledge management system.
Date when the article is scheduled to expire and be automatically archived or removed from active knowledge base. Nullable for evergreen content.
Count of positive helpfulness ratings (thumbs up) received from users who viewed the article.
Calculated helpfulness percentage based on ratio of helpful to total votes. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Used to identify high-quality and low-quality content.
Comma-separated list of issue taxonomy tags for categorization and search optimization. Examples: shipping_delay, payment_failure, account_access, product_defect.
Current lifecycle status of the article in the approval and publication workflow.
Timestamp when the article record was last modified, including content updates, metadata changes, or status transitions.
Date when the article content was last modified and republished.
Language and region locale code for the article content. Format: ISO 639-1 language code underscore ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., en_US, es_MX, fr_FR).
Count of negative helpfulness ratings (thumbs down) received from users who viewed the article.
Business priority level of the article content. Critical articles address high-impact issues and are promoted in search results.
Date when the article was first published and made available to agents or customers.
Comma-separated list of knowledge article IDs that are contextually related or frequently viewed together. Used for recommendation engine.
Target date by which the article should be reviewed for accuracy and relevance. Used to trigger periodic content audits.
Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases optimized for internal search engine indexing and article discovery.
Human-readable title of the knowledge article displayed in search results and article headers.
Sequential version number of the article. Incremented with each published revision to track content evolution.
Total number of times the article has been viewed by agents or customers. Used to measure article popularity and relevance.
Defines the audience scope for article visibility. Determines which user groups can access the article.
Unique identifier for the customer support case or service ticket. Primary key for the service support case entity.
Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Account‑level case reporting KPI for B2B customers requires linking each support case directly to the billing account.
Reference to the customer service agent currently assigned to work on this case. May be null if case is unassigned or in queue.
Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Campaign Effectiveness Support Impact Report linking each support case to the marketing campaign that caused the issue.

Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Support case investigations often need the carrier responsible for the shipment to resolve delivery issues; agents reference carrier_id for accurate root‑cause analysis.

Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: When a case concerns service level or transit time, the specific carrier service used must be known for SLA compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Support cases often arise from issues at a specific fulfillment center (e.g., inventory or pickup problems); linking enables Center‑Level case reporting and SLA tracking.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation report requires each support case to be charged to a cost center for internal accounting.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: Customer service cases about delivery delays, tracking errors, or lost parcels need a direct reference to the shipment for root‑cause analysis and compensation decisions.
Reference to the order associated with this support case, if the case relates to a specific order (e.g., shipping issue, order cancellation, item defect).

Foreign key linking to content.content_item. Business justification: Support Case Investigation process records the specific content item that triggered the case, enabling root‑cause analysis and content quality reports.
Reference to the knowledge base article used to resolve this case. Links case resolution to documented solutions for knowledge management and self-service improvement.
Foreign key linking to marketing.lead. Business justification: Needed for Lead‑to‑Support Conversion tracking, showing which marketing leads become support cases.

Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Required for support case reporting: each case must reference the specific marketplace transaction that triggered the issue, enabling accurate SLA tracking and root‑cause analysis.
Reference to a parent case if this case is a child or follow-up case. Used for linking related cases and tracking case hierarchies (e.g., original case and subsequent escalation or related inquiry).
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis of support services links cases to profit centers to measure contribution.

Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Required for Promotion Impact on Support Cases report, linking each case to the promotional campaign that triggered the purchase, enabling analysis of support volume per campaign.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: Privacy incident case tracking requires linking each support case to the specific regulation (e.g., GDPR) for reporting and audit compliance.
Reference to the RMA record if this support case resulted in or is associated with a product return authorization.
Reference to the marketplace seller if this case involves a third-party seller transaction or marketplace dispute. Null for first-party sales.
Stock Keeping Unit of the product related to this support case, if the case involves a specific product (e.g., defect, return, compatibility issue).
The service queue or team to which this case is currently assigned (e.g., Tier1_Support, Billing_Team, Returns_Processing, Escalations, VIP_Support).
Externally visible unique case identifier displayed to customers and agents. Typically follows a format like CS-12345678 or CASE-0001234567.

Classification of the support case by the nature of the customer issue: billing inquiry, shipping problem, product defect, account access issue, returns processing, refund request, marketplace dispute, or general inquiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|shipping|product_defect|account_access|returns|refund|marketplace_dispute|general_inquiry — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date and time when the case was formally closed and removed from active case inventory. May occur after resolution once customer confirms satisfaction or after a waiting period.
Monetary compensation or goodwill credit provided to the customer as part of case resolution (e.g., account credit, discount voucher, refund). Null if no compensation was provided.
Number of times the support team attempted to contact the customer regarding this case. Used for tracking customer responsiveness and case aging.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the customer initiated the support case (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).
Date and time when the support case was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the case lifecycle and SLA clock.
Customer satisfaction score collected after case resolution, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Measures customer satisfaction with the support experience and resolution quality.

The service entitlement level of the customer at the time the case was created: standard (basic support), premium (priority support), VIP (dedicated support), enterprise (white-glove service). Influences SLA targets and routing.

Indicates whether and to what level the case has been escalated: not_escalated (standard handling), tier2 (specialist support), tier3 (expert/engineering), management (supervisor review), executive (C-level attention).
Free-text feedback comments provided by the customer in post-case surveys. Captures qualitative insights about the support experience, agent performance, and areas for improvement.
Date and time when the first substantive response was provided to the customer by an agent. Key SLA metric for measuring initial response time.
Total number of customer-agent interactions (calls, emails, chats) associated with this case. Indicates case complexity and effort required for resolution.
Boolean flag indicating whether this case involves a VIP or high-value customer requiring special handling, expedited service, or executive attention. True for VIP cases, False otherwise.
reference: Customer Segmentation and Service Tier Standards

description: Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the case was handled (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French).
Date and time of the most recent update to any field in the case record. Used for tracking case activity and audit purposes.
Net Promoter Score collected from the customer after case resolution, on a scale of 0 to 10. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend the company based on the support experience.
The customer service channel through which the case was initially created: phone call, email, live chat, social media (Twitter, Facebook), self-service portal, or mobile app.

Final disposition of the case: resolved (issue successfully addressed), unresolved (could not be resolved), duplicate (merged with another case), transferred (moved to another system or team), withdrawn (customer cancelled request).
Business priority assigned to the case based on urgency and impact. P1 (critical/urgent), P2 (high), P3 (medium), P4 (low). Determines SLA targets and routing rules.
reference: ITIL Service Management - Incident Priority Matrix

description: Number of times this case has been reopened after being marked as resolved or closed. Indicates resolution quality issues or recurring customer problems.
Detailed notes documenting the resolution provided to the customer, actions taken, root cause identified, and any follow-up required. Populated when case is resolved or closed.
reference: Knowledge Management and Case Resolution Standards

description: Date and time when the case was marked as resolved, indicating that a solution was provided to the customer. Key SLA metric for measuring time-to-resolution.

Classification of the underlying root cause identified during case resolution (e.g., system_error, process_gap, product_defect, user_error, policy_exception, carrier_issue). Used for trend analysis and continuous improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: system_error|process_gap|product_defect|user_error|policy_exception|carrier_issue|payment_failure|inventory_issue|data_quality — promote to reference product]
Automated sentiment analysis score derived from customer communications (emails, chat transcripts) during the case lifecycle. Scale typically -1.00 (very negative) to +1.00 (very positive).
reference: Natural Language Processing (NLP) Sentiment Analysis Standards

description: Detailed description of the customer issue, inquiry, or request as captured during case creation. May include customer-provided details, agent notes, and initial diagnostic information.

Current lifecycle status of the support case: new (just created), open (assigned), pending_customer (awaiting customer response), pending_internal (awaiting internal action), in_progress (actively being worked), escalated (moved to higher tier), resolved (solution provided), closed (finalized), cancelled (withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|pending_customer|pending_internal|in_progress|escalated|resolved|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Boolean indicator of whether this case breached its SLA targets for first response time or resolution time. True indicates SLA was breached, False indicates SLA was met.
Reference code for the SLA policy governing response and resolution time targets for this case, based on customer tier, case priority, and case type.
Brief summary or subject line describing the customer issue or inquiry. Typically 100-200 characters.
Unique identifier for the SLA policy record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulation. Business justification: SLA policies must meet regulatory consumer protection standards; linking SLA policy to the governing regulation allows compliance monitoring.
Reference to the newer SLA policy that replaces this one. Nullable for current active policies. Creates version chain for policy evolution tracking.
Approval status of the SLA policy. Policies must be approved before becoming active. Supports governance and change control processes.
Identifier of the user who approved this SLA policy. Typically a senior operations leader or executive. Nullable for unapproved policies.
Timestamp when this SLA policy was approved. Nullable for unapproved policies. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.
Boolean flag indicating whether automatic escalation is enabled when escalation_threshold_hours is reached. True enables automated escalation workflow, False requires manual escalation.
Monetary amount of penalty or credit applied when SLA is breached. Applicable when breach_penalty_type is financial or credit. Stored in base currency.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the breach penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Type of penalty or action triggered when SLA is breached. Options include no penalty, financial penalty, customer credit, automatic escalation, or notification to management.
Comma-separated list of business days when SLA is active (e.g., 'Monday,Tuesday,Wednesday,Thursday,Friday'). Used when time_calculation_method is business_hours to exclude weekends or specific days.
End time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.
Start time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.

Priority level of cases this SLA policy applies to. Used in conjunction with case_type to form the SLA matrix (e.g., High priority order inquiries have different SLA targets than Low priority inquiries).

Type of customer service case this SLA policy applies to (e.g., 'Order Inquiry', 'Product Return', 'Technical Issue', 'Billing Dispute', 'Account Management'). Defines the scope of cases governed by this policy.
Customer service channel this SLA policy applies to. Different channels may have different response time commitments (e.g., chat has faster response than email).

Boolean flag indicating whether this SLA policy is driven by regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR data subject request response times, financial services complaint handling). True indicates regulatory mandate.
Timestamp when this SLA policy record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.
Customer segment this SLA policy applies to (e.g., 'VIP', 'Premium', 'Standard', 'Enterprise', 'SMB'). Enables differentiated service levels by customer tier.
Date when this SLA policy expires or is superseded by a new version. Nullable for currently active policies with no planned end date.
Date when this SLA policy becomes active and begins governing case handling. Part of the effective date range for policy versioning.
Numeric escalation tier this SLA policy triggers (e.g., 1 = supervisor, 2 = manager, 3 = director). Defines the organizational level to which cases are escalated when thresholds are breached.
Time threshold in hours that triggers automatic escalation if the case is not resolved. Typically set before the resolution target to allow time for escalated handling.

Description of cases or conditions excluded from this SLA policy (e.g., 'Excludes cases opened during system maintenance windows', 'Does not apply to third-party vendor issues'). Defines policy boundaries.
Target time in hours for the first response to a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed before initial acknowledgment or response to the customer.
Geographic region or market this SLA policy applies to (e.g., 'North America', 'EMEA', 'APAC'). Supports region-specific service level commitments.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default SLA policy applied when no specific policy matches a case. Only one policy per case_type should be marked as default.
Timestamp when this SLA policy record was last updated. Part of standard audit trail for change tracking.
Boolean flag indicating whether automated notifications are sent when SLA thresholds are approaching or breached. True enables notification workflow.
Percentage of SLA target time elapsed that triggers a warning notification (e.g., 80 means notify when 80% of resolution_target_hours has passed). Used for proactive SLA management.
Identifier of the business user or manager responsible for defining and maintaining this SLA policy. Typically a customer service director or operations manager.
Unique business identifier code for the SLA policy used in operational systems and reporting.
Human-readable name of the SLA policy (e.g., 'Premium Support SLA', 'Standard Response Policy').
Classification of the SLA policy indicating the service area it governs (customer support, technical support, returns processing, escalation handling, VIP service, standard service).
Name of the regulatory framework or law that mandates this SLA policy (e.g., 'GDPR', 'CCPA', 'FTC Consumer Protection', 'PCI DSS'). Populated when compliance_required is True.
Target time in hours for full resolution of a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed to completely resolve and close the case.
Detailed description of the SLA policy including its purpose, scope, and any special conditions or exceptions. Provides context for policy application and interpretation.
Current lifecycle status of the SLA policy indicating whether it is actively enforced, inactive, in draft state, archived, or temporarily suspended.
Method used to calculate SLA time targets. Business hours counts only operational hours (e.g., 9am-5pm weekdays), while calendar hours counts all hours including nights and weekends.
Version number of this SLA policy. Incremented when policy terms are updated. Supports policy change tracking and historical analysis.
Unique identifier for the customer service team. Primary key.
Reference to the employee or manager who leads this team. Used for escalation paths and team performance accountability.
Reference to the finance cost center to which this team's expenses are allocated. Enables financial reporting and budget tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance - Cost Center Accounting

description: Reference to the parent team in a hierarchical team structure. Null for top-level teams. Enables organizational hierarchy and escalation path modeling.
Reference to the team to which cases are escalated when this team cannot resolve them. Null if this is the highest escalation tier. Enables escalation path configuration.
reference: ITIL Service Operation - Incident Management - Escalation

description: Total team capacity expressed in Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) headcount. Used for workforce planning, capacity forecasting, and case load balancing.
Timestamp when the team record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Target Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score for the team, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Key performance indicator (KPI) for customer experience quality.
Date when the team became operational and started accepting cases. Used for team lifecycle tracking and historical reporting.
Primary email address for the team, used for case assignment notifications and internal communication. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.

Geographic region or market this team serves, using 3-letter ISO country codes or region identifiers (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, EUR, APAC, GLOBAL). Supports geo-based routing and regional SLA (Service Level Agreement) management.
Indicates whether the team operates remotely (distributed workforce) or from a centralized physical location. True if remote, False if on-site.
Comma-separated list of languages the team can support using ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh, ja). Enables language-based case routing.
Maximum number of cases the team can handle concurrently based on capacity and workload models. Used for case routing throttling and queue management.
Timestamp when the team record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the team (e.g., special operating procedures, temporary capacity changes, pilot program details).
Target Net Promoter Score (NPS) for the team, ranging from -100 to +100. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend based on team interactions.
Indicates whether the team operates on public holidays. True if holiday coverage is provided, False if holidays are non-operational.
Indicates whether the team operates on weekends (Saturday and Sunday). True if weekend coverage is provided, False if weekends are non-operational.
Daily end time of the team's operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team's local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.
Daily start time of the team's operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team's local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.
Primary phone number for the team, used for internal escalations and coordination. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.
Physical office or site location where the team is based (e.g., New York HQ, Manila Contact Center, Remote). Used for workforce planning and facility management.

Brief description of the team's primary business function and scope of responsibility (e.g., Order inquiries and tracking, Returns and refunds processing, Account and billing support, Technical troubleshooting).

Target time in hours for the team to resolve a case, as defined by the team's SLA tier. Key performance indicator (KPI) for case closure efficiency.

Human-readable name of the customer service team (e.g., Tier-1 Frontline Support, Returns Specialist Team, VIP Customer Care).
Current operational status of the team: active (currently operational and accepting cases), inactive (temporarily not operational), suspended (on hold pending review), dissolved (permanently closed).

Classification of the team based on its role in the service organization: tier_1_frontline (first contact resolution), tier_2_escalation (complex issue handling), specialist (domain-specific expertise such as RMA or fraud), back_office (non-customer-facing support), quality_assurance (monitoring and coaching), training (onboarding and skill development).
